Crescent Mall is a modern, mid-sized shopping centre located in the District 7 area. It opened in late 2011 and took around 3 years to complete, becoming one of the first international-standard malls in the city.

The layout is easy to navigate with a clean, airy feel and a stylish curved design. The food court and CGV cinema are located on the upper floors, with plenty of seating and a mix of both Western and Asian options. The lower levels feature a good variety of well-known international brands such as MUJI, Nike, H&M, and more.

We stopped by MUJI for a look—great range of minimalist clothing and household goods, and even a small café tucked inside. The mall wasn’t too crowded, which made for a relaxed shopping experience.

Afterwards, we walked over to the Starlight Bridge and enjoyed the peaceful parkland nearby—a great way to wind down the trip.

Overall, a great combination of shopping, dining, and a nearby scenic spot if you’re spending time in District 7.

user_reviews.Name: Tap Nguyen
user_reviews.Description: Clean. Diverse goods. Many world-renowned brands.

user_reviews.Name: Ashok Patel
user_reviews.Description:
It’s located in District 7. One of the modern mall includes famous brands for shopping.
We bought some T-shirt and perfume.

user_reviews.Name: Scott Nguyen
user_reviews.Description:
Crescent Mall, located in District 7 of Ho Chi Minh City, is one of the city’s premier shopping destinations, known for its sleek, modern design and wide array of retail, dining, and entertainment options. Nestled along the scenic Crescent Lake, the mall offers a refreshing atmosphere, providing a relaxing shopping experience in a spacious, well-designed environment.

The mall houses a mix of international and local fashion brands, electronics stores, and home decor outlets, making it a one-stop shop for a variety of needs. From high-end luxury boutiques to more affordable mainstream stores, Crescent Mall caters to a broad demographic, ensuring there’s something for everyone.

Dining at Crescent Mall is another highlight, with a wide range of restaurants and cafes offering diverse cuisines. Whether you’re looking for fast food, casual dining, or something more upscale, there are plenty of options to satisfy any craving. The mall also features a large supermarket, CGV cinema, and an entertainment area for kids, making it a popular destination for families.

Crescent Mall’s picturesque location next to the lake allows for leisurely strolls along the promenade after a day of shopping. The area is well-maintained and designed for a comfortable, upscale shopping and dining experience, making it a favored spot for locals and expats alike in Saigon.

title: UNION SQUARE
address: 171 Đồng Khởi, Bến Nghé, Sài Gòn, Hồ Chí Minh, Vietnam
plus_code: QPG2+GP Sai Gon, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam
description: Retail center in an ornate building, featuring upscale boutiques & casual eateries.

user_reviews.Name: Naveen Shetty
user_reviews.Description:
Union Square, Vietnam

It’s an icon of its time and one of the most visited place in Vietnam. In front this iconic building you will also see the statue of “Father of Vietnam” Mr Ho Chi Minh.

user_reviews.Name: Dimitris Apollonas Poseidonas Venetis
user_reviews.Description: This mall is very beautiful. You can see how carefully was built with good materials. Looks very unique. The problem is that is totally empty. Luxury brands that people do not have money for spending on them. But you should definitely visit it just to get the vibe and energy. For your information there is no ATM in this mall. I have asked.

user_reviews.Name: karen ivey
user_reviews.Description: Quite pretty Christmas decorations for children. Place always feels empty  but L2 good for a quiet coffee and to catch metro

user_reviews.Name: Edwin Petrus
user_reviews.Description: Union Square is a high end shopping mall in the heart of Ho Chi Minh City. International branded products are sold here. The mall is also nicely decorated. There are high end cinema and eateries here as well. Private shopping is also possible when the store is closed for public. Union Square is situated at the prime location of the city, just next to the famous Ho Chi Minh statue and major landmarks as well as hotels in District 1.

user_reviews.Name: Enzo Warren
user_reviews.Description: Loved UnionSquare, especially Rue Miche—such a great spot. Clean, well-maintained restrooms made the visit even better. Will definitely be back!

user_reviews.Name: Nhàn Nguyễn
user_reviews.Description: Elegant, luxurious, and trendy. Highly recommend visiting for shopping and perfect for photo shoots. Only a few luxurious boutiques here, but no food and beverage options. Good service at the information desk 👌

user_reviews.Name: Ak
user_reviews.Description: If you want high brands products, this is the place. Gucci, Dior, Hermes, Bulgari, etc. As a shopping mall, this one is pretty underwhelming since there is nothing else rather than these brands stores.

user_reviews.Name: Nathanael Tan
user_reviews.Description: After so many years still a ghost town. A real shame of prime location. Mandarin Hotel expected to open.

title: Menas Mall Saigon Airport
address: 60A Trường Sơn, Phường Tân, Tân Sơn Hòa, Hồ Chí Minh 70000, Vietnam
plus_code: RM78+68 Tan Son Hoa, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

complete_address.state: Tan Son Hoa, Ho Chi Minh

user_reviews.Name: Tokyo Nguyên
user_reviews.Description:
🌃🛬My favorite spot after a long flight back to Saigon. Just across from Tan Son Nhat International Airport. Not many shops here, but it is definitely one of the best places to grab dinner after a flight.
🥘🍔The food court is on the 5th floor and offers a wide variety of options. We ordered hotpot for 2 at an affordable price. We sat by the window watched people rushing by below and the airport late at night.

user_reviews.Name: Joanne Bailey
user_reviews.Description: This shopping centre has a great food court so many choices in a great area with lots of tables . The few shops we purchased things at had good friendly staff . Good signage to show the toilets and exits . Unfortunately it never seems very busy when we have been there.  It's perfectly positioned to the hotels around the airport.

user_reviews.Name: Gia Thanh Dang
user_reviews.Description: The Yum Food Village food court is a great place to eat! It has a beautiful view, spacious and comfortable seating, and a wide variety of dishes to choose from. The prices are reasonable, making it a perfect spot for both quick bites and relaxed meals. Highly recommended for anyone looking for good food in a nice setting.

user_reviews.Name: Lien Nguyen Vu Minh
user_reviews.Description: Menas Mall truly exceeded my expectations. The space is not only modern and beautifully designed, but also incredibly spacious and comfortable. It’s the perfect spot for shopping, dining, or just relaxing with friends. An ideal destination for anyone looking for a pleasant and enjoyable experience.

user_reviews.Name: Dhonam Pemba
user_reviews.Description: Good mall, grocery store in basement, coffee shop in first floor, movie theater on top floor and food court and seafood buffet near the top floor

user_reviews.Name: Elmer S
user_reviews.Description:
Nothing special in this mall. On the 5th floor you’ll find variety of restaurant, from 65K VND.

If you will stay and kill time prior your departure from Ho Chi Minh, suggested go to more nicer mall. This mall is only really food and eating.

user_reviews.Name: Carole A
user_reviews.Description: Pretty irrelevant for a mall. No shops really. More like a pit stop with a couple of cafes, restaurants and a food court or a place to just chill for free. The parking lot is horrific. Tiny and overcrowded. The restrooms were super clean though. Don't know if its reliable but they say: they have a free shuttle bus to and from the airport!

user_reviews.Name: Tan Oo
user_reviews.Description: Still very quiet. Most of store space still empty. Don't hope too much if you wanna shopping here. Just have food court which you can rely on, since it has quite much option and relatively cheap.

title: TAKA PLAZA
address: TAKA PLAZA 4 Phạm Ngũ Lão, Phường Phạm Ngũ Lão, Bến Thành, Hồ Chí Minh, Vietnam
plus_code: QM9V+JF Ben Thanh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam
description: Bustling complex of over 200 stalls offering wholesale & retail clothing, cosmetics & street food.

complete_address.borough: Phường Phạm Ngũ Lão, Ben Thanh

user_reviews.Name: Harry Chiem
user_reviews.Description: One of the best and most consistent shopping markets in Saigon. Might be even better now that New Playground has been separated from it. They house a pretty good billiards bar, some restaurants and food court but more importantly some great markets. An awesome place to go and test your mettle in bartering. Highly recommend!

user_reviews.Name: Peter
user_reviews.Description: Taka Plaza is a bustling, multi-level labyrinth that goes far beyond its reputation for electronics. While you can certainly find gadgets and phones, it's a vibrant one-stop marketplace for clothes, shoes, jewelry, accessories, souvenirs, and local crafts—from brocade bags and silk ties to fine wood carvings and ceramics. A major advantage is that the entire complex is fully air-conditioned, providing a cool respite from the Saigon heat, and it houses a convenient indoor food court for a quick refreshment break. It's a chaotic, fascinating dive into both modern and traditional Vietnamese commerce under one very comfortable roof. Just remember your bargaining skills!

user_reviews.Name: Adam Gibson
user_reviews.Description: This has been my favourite market to go to for clothing, foot ware and luggage. Was able to buy traditional Vietnamese women’s dresses for 550k each (we purchased 3). No food is being sold here. It’s all undercover and air conditioned. There is a food court in the middle. The sellers are friendly and helpful and not pushy at all like Ben Than Markets. I found the prices fair and negotiated a little but was honestly happy to pay their asking price. Went multiple times on different days and received the same level of attention and service. Would definitely recommend to anyone looking to buy clothing without any of the pressure sales. Did struggle with larger sizing for mens shoes (but I expected that), so if you’re looking for anything larger than a US 11 mens you might have a hard time finding it here.

user_reviews.Name: Lisa Johnson
user_reviews.Description: One of the best markets in Saigon. Quite good variaty of stuf to buy, prices are reasonable even before we start to bargain. And best of all its got aircondition, which is super nice!

user_reviews.Name: Francine T.
user_reviews.Description: Great place to eat! I came here for the food court and it's very spacious, with a lot of other things to do. In the entrance, there's a lot of stalls selling clothes and electronic accessories and stuff, there's also a claw machine area and a picture booth. It doesn't feel very crowded and there's a lot of space and seating! Air conditioning is also great! In the food court, there are a lot of stalls with a lot of variety in food. Prices are also affordable and taste great!

user_reviews.Name: Shabbir Raja
user_reviews.Description: Taka Plaza also Taka Central Market is an underground shopping area with some good shops for Shoes, Clothes, Belts, Caps, Sports wear, etc. You can do some bargaining at shops. Shopkeepers are very friendly. They won't mind if you joke with them. I would recommend this place. Remember, you cannot see this place from Main Road. Find a way to go down and then you will see the shops.

user_reviews.Name: rajesh kazhipurath
user_reviews.Description:
Taka Plaza is a popular, air-conditioned underground marketplace in District 1, Ho Chi Minh City, offering a more relaxed, less-aggressive alternative to Ben Thanh Market. Located at 102 Nam Ky Khoi Nghia, it features over 200 stalls selling clothes, accessories, shoes, and local crafts, alongside a food court and varied souvenirs.

A more refined and easier place to shop.

user_reviews.Name: K O
user_reviews.Description: Rude staff, they clearly don't want your business.  Everywhere you go, they say it's a fixed price and it is extortionate and overpriced.  You cannot haggle and the staff are extremely rude and aggressive.  Avoid, better off going to Saigon Square or even Ben Thanh

title: Aeon Shopping Mall - Binh Duong Canary
address: 1 Đại lộ Bình Dương, Thuận Giao, Hồ Chí Minh 700000, Vietnam
plus_code: WPJ6+WQ Thuan Giao, Binh Duong, Vietnam
description: Bustling, contemporary mall with retail stores, boutiques, play areas, a food court & supermarket.

owner.name: Aeon Shopping Mall - Binh Duong Canary (Owner)

user_reviews.Name: Hc Tan
user_reviews.Description: It is considered the largest and best shopping mall in the area, with comprehensive living and entertainment facilities.

user_reviews.Name: Quý Khôi Nguyễn (Kaden Nguyen)
user_reviews.Description:
Review by Khoi, Sun 09/07/2023
I had a great time at Aeon Mall last weekend. It's a huge shopping complex with everything you could ask for: clothes, electronics, books, food, entertainment and more. The mall is clean, spacious and well-designed. There are plenty of parking spaces and public transport options nearby. The staff are friendly and helpful.

One reason I didn't give it five stars is because some of the stores were closed due to the pandemic and the food court was a bit crowded. The food court had a variety of cuisines to choose from, such as Korean, Japanese, Thai and Italian. The prices were reasonable and the portions were generous. I enjoyed a delicious sushi platter although I had to wait in line for a while and it was hard to find a seat. The atmosphere of the food court was lively and cheerful, with music playing and people chatting. It felt like a mini festival of flavors and cultures. But overall, I highly recommend Aeon Mall for anyone looking for a fun and satisfying shopping experience.

user_reviews.Name: Ric Kee
user_reviews.Description: Nice shopping mall, and you can get all the daily groceries you want at here.

user_reviews.Name: Phuong Tam
user_reviews.Description:
Beautiful, very clean shopping mall. Lots of good products. Lots of good choices.
But NOISY.
Inside Aeon Mall Canary is a daily chaotic brouhaha of commercial sounds CONSTANTLY SURPASSING safe hearing sound level of 70 dBA, some reaching POTENTIALLY HEARING DAMAGE level of 85+ dBA:
- Uncomfortably LOUD background music
- Excessive LOUD speakers advertising propaganda from Aeon supermarket
- in-mall competing shops which have their own private music trying to get LOUDER than one another
- occasional out-mall independent sellers LOUD promotion sale activities.

World Health Organization (WHO) recommend maintaining environmental noises BELOW 70 dBA to prevent Noise-Induced Hearing Loss (NIHL).

Continued exposure to noise above 70 dBA over time will cause hearing loss.

Sound levels exceeding 85 dBA are considered dangerous and potentially damaging to human hearing.

user_reviews.Name: Ravinder K
user_reviews.Description: This mall is absolutely fantastic! ✨ I had such a blast exploring all the amazing shops and the vibe was just incredible! 🛍️ Can't wait to come back for more fun! 😄 It's a must-visit! 💯

user_reviews.Name: Andrew Nguyen - English (Andrew)
user_reviews.Description: Although it’s usually considered as smaller in scale and number of shops, you maybe miss understood that this is not a mall. I can buy almost all the necessities here tbh.

user_reviews.Name: Khuong Thanh Vinh
user_reviews.Description: Love this place. Very clean. There are a lot of goods for shopping. There are some rooms only for taking care baby. I never seen something like these in Vietnam, even in luxury shopping malls.

user_reviews.Name: Ky Co
user_reviews.Description: This mall has everything, the Supermarket is huge, there are restaurants, and all kinds of stores. McDonald's, Starbucks, and Texus Chicken are found here as well.

title: Chợ Kim Biên
address: 42a Đ. Kim Biên, Chợ Lớn, Hồ Chí Minh, Vietnam
plus_code: QM24+29 Cho Lon, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Geoff Edwards
user_reviews.Description: Good selection of products.. Sensible prices.

user_reviews.Name: Hoàng Bảo Phạm (Pauz19)
user_reviews.Description: Ok

user_reviews.Name: Joo
user_reviews.Description: Ok

user_reviews.Name: Vũ Tống
user_reviews.Description: Ok

user_reviews.Name: Duy Lê
user_reviews.Description: Ok

user_reviews.Name: philip ducharme
user_reviews.Description: Great shopping for led lights .tools .paint.  plumbing. Electronics. And more.

user_reviews.Name: Pham Quang
user_reviews.Description: good

user_reviews.Name: Hoàng Nguyễn
user_reviews.Description:
Chợ Kim Biên là một trong những khu chợ đầu mối nổi tiếng bậc nhất Sài Gòn
Chợ Kim Biên là khu chợ đầu mối lâu đời và nổi tiếng tại Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh. Chợ được thành lập từ những năm 1960 và là một trong những điểm mua sắm phổ biến, quen thuộc với người dân trong thành phố cũng như các vùng lân cận. Chợ Kim Biên ban đầu hoạt động như một nơi trao đổi, mua bán đôla và hàng hóa quân tiếp vụ. Tuy nhiên, sau năm 1975, chợ đã điều chỉnh phương thức hoạt động bằng việc cho phép các tiểu thương thuê sạp để mở rộng kinh doanh và bán đa dạng các mặt hàng khác nhau. Với không gian sôi động, nhộn nhịp nhưng vẫn không kém phần bình dị, Chợ Kim Biên đã trở thành một biểu tượng văn hóa của Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh và là điểm đến hấp dẫn không chỉ cho người dân địa phương mà còn cho du khách trong và ngoài nước.

Dù không có quy mô lớn như một số chợ nổi tiếng khác trong thành phố như chợ Bình Tây hay Bến Thành, chợ Kim Biên vẫn giữ vững vị thế là một chợ đầu mối đáng tin cậy. Đây là địa điểm chuyên phục vụ mua bán hàng giá buôn, giá sỉ và thu hút đông đảo tiểu thương lựa chọn để cung cấp hàng hoá cho thị trường địa phương. Chợ Kim Biên còn mang trong mình những giá trị văn hóa và tinh thần giao thoa của các nền văn hóa. Đến đây, bạn sẽ có cơ hội tìm hiểu, thưởng thức nét đặc trưng của cuộc sống cũng như văn hóa truyền thống của người dân miền Nam.

title: Ben Thanh Market
address: Bến Thành, Hồ Chí Minh, Vietnam
plus_code: QMFX+26 Ben Thanh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam
description: Well-known standby for handicrafts, souvenirs, clothing & other goods along with local eats.

user_reviews.Name: Darwin Mainke
user_reviews.Description: Saigon's iconic, bustling central marketplace. Easily accessible by all modes of transport especially with their new MRT line just underneath. Went there around noon time and it was indeed hot. Though sellers will be bit persuasive but they're not. The market sells all kinds of items and you will need to have best haggling skills if you want to buy it cheaper.

user_reviews.Name: Wayne L
user_reviews.Description:
Local market with lots of history turned tourist spot. I’m not sure if there’s anything local that’s left, considering how most shops have fully committed to milking every dong from you. Products, be it cashew, coffee beans or fashion, are homogenous - they may share the exact same suppliers, even.

Suggested approach: do your homework - watch travel vlogs and decide what you might want to purchase. Consider how much you want to pay, and simply look for a seller that works best for you - they are all selling the same product anyway. Personally, I prefer a quieter, less pushy shopping experience. Hence, I opted to wander the inner lanes and settled for a shop that was way quieter. The seller I encountered was sweet, helpful, and was never pushy with sales. If anything, that made me want to buy more.

If something intrigues you, stop and haggle - ideally without looking too curious or desperate. Guess it helps if you’re the first customer of the day.

Would personally skip the cooked food here.

user_reviews.Name: vie Privee 11
user_reviews.Description:
typical Saigon market experience — busy, colorful, and full of energy. You’ll find everything here: souvenirs, clothes, coffee, dried goods, snacks, and fresh fruits. It’s very touristy, but still fun to walk around and explore.

Prices are usually higher at first, so be ready to haggle. Don’t feel shy — negotiating is part of the experience. If you don’t bargain, you’ll probably overpay.

Good place to buy small gifts and try local fruits, but not the cheapest market in the city. Come for the vibe and experience, not necessarily the best deals.

user_reviews.Name: S Young
user_reviews.Description:
Most foreigners visiting HCMC will end up shopping at local markets for manufactured goods like bags, shoes, clothes, and especially football and basketball jerseys. Haggling and negotiating are very much part of the culture here.

For many Western travelers, this can feel uncomfortable at first since we’re used to clearly marked, fixed prices. After spending 10 days in Da Nang and Hanoi, our hotel staff gave us helpful advice: if a vendor starts at 100K VND, counter with around 25 VND and you’ll usually end up paying about 40% of the original price.

That said, if the price already feels reasonable, it’s often not worth negotiating. But if you’re buying multiple items, bargaining becomes part of the process. While time is money and negotiating isn’t my favorite thing to do, when purchasing several  jerseys, it made sense and it’s important to remember that bargaining here is cultural, not disrespectful. I have successfully bought a few shorts, linen shirts and football jersey!  We also are some street food outside the market! Just remember that there are hundreds of the same stores and it's ok to walk away. A female vendor was trying to guilt trip me into buying a shirt that is too small but she said you already tried on. So what? It's too tight and I don't want it. She them said something in Vietnamese which I don't care. It's exhausting to shop there since it's freaking hot.

user_reviews.Name: Alex Haas
user_reviews.Description:
This indoor market was just a short walk away from our Hotel. It's open until around 6pm and offers lots of products.

Most of it is about clothes, bags, accessories and stuff like that. Cheap stuff or fake brands In the middle of the market it's all about haggle for a good price. It's fun to browse, but most products didn't catch our attention. In the outer "ring" of the market there are more - also traditional - clothes to be found. With fixed prices, so you won't have to haggle aorund. We actually liked that and found some nice clothes and caps here. Good quality, fair prices and friendly staff.

It's not mainly a food market, but there is a good number of food stalls, quite aggressively fighting for customers. If you can ignore that, you will find some enjoyable food here. We did. Quality wasn't the very best on this trip, but cheap and cheerful.

At night, when the main market is closed, lots of stalls will line up just outside the market, offering all kinds of products. A nice time to enjoy the evening, with all the bustling life, the lights...it's a nice atmosphere.

We enjoyed our visit. Not something to do daily, but surely a must if you've never been to.

user_reviews.Name: Fritz
user_reviews.Description:
I spent my afternoon at Ben Thanh Market in Ho Chi Minh City, and it was such a fun experience! The market is definitely one of those must-see places if you’re in the city. It’s got that perfect mix of vibrant energy and local charm that makes it feel like you’re truly in the heart of Ho Chi Minh.

The first thing that hits you is the sheer variety of things you can find there. From fresh fruits, local spices, and snacks, to souvenirs, clothing, and handmade crafts, it’s like a maze of different goods packed into one bustling space. I couldn’t help but browse through the stalls, checking out everything from cool handmade bags to unique jewelry. The prices can be a bit higher than other spots in the city, but the quality is good, and the experience is definitely worth it.

One of the highlights for me was the food. I grabbed some delicious Vietnamese snacks like banh xeo (crispy pancakes) and fresh spring rolls, and they were AMAZING. The flavors were so fresh and tasty—definitely a must-try if you’re into street food.

Even though the market can get crowded, especially in the afternoon, it’s got such a lively atmosphere that it makes the hustle and bustle part of the fun. The vendors are friendly, and some even have a little playful banter, which makes shopping here feel more personal.

Overall, Ben Thanh Market is a great spot to get a taste of local culture and pick up some souvenirs or fresh food. It’s one of those places that feels like you’re getting a real slice of the city, and I definitely enjoyed my afternoon there!

user_reviews.Name: Shreyanshi Shukla
user_reviews.Description: Ben Thanh Market is a great place to explore if you enjoy shopping and local markets. It has a huge variety of items including souvenirs, clothes, handicrafts, and street food. Prices are usually higher for tourists, so bargaining is important. If you know how to bargain well, you can get some really good deals. A lively place with lots of options.

user_reviews.Name: Nur Syafiqah
user_reviews.Description: I was on a tightly scheduled business trip and didnt have time to explore elsewhere for souvenirs. I stumbled upon this market unintentionally during my morning run. Since I was in a rush, I just went inside, picked one shop, and bought everything I needed there like a few fridge magnets and some bags. Actually I was lucky they accepted cards, as my colleague had warned me that most shops here only take cash, and I had none. I know the prices are a bit high, but with my limited time, I didnt have much choice. What I found funny was that a few other shopkeepers, whose stalls I didnt buy from, seemed irritated or frustrated when I tried to bargain, even slightly. The initial prices are often three to four times higher than in other places. If you have the time, its wise to do your due diligence and research fair prices for goods so you can negotiate effectively here or at other tourist markets like Saigon Square etc. Nevertheless, for the experience, its fine to go there once for a quick walkthrough to see the bustling action. Have fun, embrace the experience, and dont take offense if sellers get angry or curse in their native tongue. Just let it roll off and keep walking. Its a wonderful market, I can say. Its like a maze. I almost forgot which gate I came in from and ended up exiting through a different one, which was quite exciting

title: Binh Tay Market
address: 57A Tháp Mười, Phường 2, Bình Tây, Hồ Chí Minh 700900, Vietnam
plus_code: PMX2+PC Binh Tay, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam
description: Covered market with stalls selling home goods, produce & local dishes like pho & spring rolls.

user_reviews.Name: Food Exprorer
user_reviews.Description:
The Heartbeat of Real Saigon – A Must-Visit for Authentic Culture Seekers! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
​"If you want to escape the tourist traps and witness the true, pulsing energy of Ho Chi Minh City, skip Ben Thanh for a morning and head straight to Binh Tay Market in Cholon (Chinatown).
​As a resident here, I can tell you this is where the real Saigon happens. The yellow French-Chinese architecture is a masterpiece, but the magic is inside. It’s a wholesale paradise—everything from premium dried seafood (I highly recommend Stall 315, Hùng Phón, for top-tier fish maw and sea cucumbers) to exotic spices and textiles you won't find anywhere else.
​Why Binh Tay is better for explorers:
​Authenticity: This isn't curated for tourists; it’s a living, breathing ecosystem of local trade.
​Pricing: Since it's a wholesale hub, you get significantly better value than at District 1 markets.
​The Atmosphere: The central courtyard with the shrine of Quach Dam offers a serene contrast to the bustling aisles.
​Pro-Tips for Visitors:
​Go Early: Arrive around 8 AM to see the peak wholesale activity.
​Cameras Ready: The narrow aisles and hanging goods create incredible 'Street Photography' opportunities.
​Local Eats: The food court area serves some of the best 'Bun Bo Hue' in the district.
​A true sensory overload in the best way possible. If you’re looking for the soul of Vietnam, you’ve found it here!"
​#SaigonLocalGuide #BinhTayMarket #Cholon #HoChiMinhCity #VisitVietnam #HiddenGem #SaigonTravel #AuthenticVietnam #WholesaleMarket

user_reviews.Name: Danny
user_reviews.Description: Loved the time I spent here at this market, can't find any fault with this venue holding 1200 vendors. Few tourists spotted and mostly a local vibe with heavy wholesale influence. Retail is available and the atmosphere is pleasant even though it's cramped quarters in a lot of areas. You can shop here for most everything you see at other markets at lower prices. Prepared foods as well as produce, dried mushrooms and candy are widely available. Nice central plaza with fountain and plenty of photo opps.

user_reviews.Name: Selma Jensen
user_reviews.Description:
vibrant and authentic taste of Vietnamese street food culture. As a night market, it really comes alive after sunset with a mix of local vendors, delicious dishes, and friendly energy. You can find classic Vietnamese favorites like banh beo, grilled meats, seafood, and sweet treats, all at very affordable prices — perfect for casual eating and trying new flavors.
What makes Cửa Tây special is its lively atmosphere. The lights, chatter, and scents of food cooking create a welcoming place to mingle with both locals and visitors. It’s a great spot to wander, snack, and soak up the culture of Ho Chi Minh City without the formality of a sit-down restaurant.
That said, some stalls offer similar souvenirs and goods, so shopping can feel repetitive, and bargaining isn’t always straightforward — but the overall vibe and food make it worth a visit.

user_reviews.Name: Anne Chin
user_reviews.Description: It's just like any public market. Reminds me of Divisoria within China Town in Binondo Manila Philippines. Lots of different kind of nuts and candies selling at the front of the entrance. They sell fabrics and interesting pcs of finished products made of fabrics.

user_reviews.Name: Rikun
user_reviews.Description:
No 3. Noodle stall is highly recommended for breakfast if you visit this market. The seafood ingredients they used are really fresh amd very affordable. 40k a bowl.l, do your maths.

It is ashame i could not find a gmap pin for this stall. But it is behind the raincoat area.

user_reviews.Name: Róża _
user_reviews.Description: Big market with even bigger choice of goods. Not many tourists shop there, mostly local people. Great place to buy fruits, spices, sweets, coffee, tea and even more.

user_reviews.Name: Anika Patil
user_reviews.Description: Binh Tay Market is full of energy — busy markets, old temples, and great street food. It’s a bit chaotic but very authentic. A perfect place to explore local culture and take photos. Worth a visit!

user_reviews.Name: Kimchi
user_reviews.Description:
Binh Tay Market, located in Ho Chi Minh City’s Chinatown (Chợ Lớn), is a bustling local market that caters mainly to everyday needs and wholesale trading. The market is well known for its wide range of affordable, non-branded goods, including lingerie, bags, footwear, clothing, household items, and dried food products. Many local retailers source their stock from here due to its competitive wholesale prices.

Compared to Ben Thanh Market, which focuses more on branded items, souvenirs, and tourist-oriented shopping, Binh Tay Market offers a more authentic local experience. Branded products are rare here, and the emphasis is on practicality, bulk purchasing, and value for money rather than labels.

Beyond shopping, Binh Tay Market is also a great place to explore local food. You’ll find stalls selling Vietnamese snacks, fresh produce, spices, and ready-to-eat dishes that reflect the daily lifestyle of the locals. Don’t forget to pick up a traditional Vietnamese coffee filter (phin), it’s widely available here at bargain prices and makes a perfect, functional souvenir.

If you’re looking to experience local trading culture, hunt for good deals, or shop like a local rather than a tourist, Binh Tay Market is definitely worth a visit.

title: Tân Định Market
address: 336 Hai Bà Trưng, Tân Định, Hồ Chí Minh, Vietnam
plus_code: QMQR+X2 Tan Dinh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam
description: Bustling market known for its fabrics & clothing, plus food stalls serving noodles & seafood.

user_reviews.Name: Angela Merici
user_reviews.Description: Nice local market, good to buy dried fruits. I bought dried Jackfruit and they where delicious. 100g for 25.000 VND, 11.2025. Also they have normal things for local Wholesale people. Fishmarket... take care about the prices. Bit high, ask before you buy! Enjoeyed also a real local soup there, good, but not hygnenic.

user_reviews.Name: Nazanin R
user_reviews.Description:
If you are nearby and curious, you can visit Tam Dinh Market. However, there is nothing particularly special about it. The market had a very strong unpleasant smell and it felt quite dirty.

If you want to see a local market, it’s better to visit Ben Thanh Market. Another option is Chợ Bến Thành, which is a larger market and is close to the Ho Chi Ming City Museum of Fine Arts, about a 5-minute walk

user_reviews.Name: dannyt
user_reviews.Description: Very local! There are better souvenirs in Ben than and at central market, if that’s what you. But as for TD.. good to walk around in still as a tourist just to wander. Unless au dai is what you’re after. There’s a bit of variety to try. The Tan Dinh church worth a pic.

user_reviews.Name: Wen-Ping Shih
user_reviews.Description:
Stall No. 525, VAN SON Women’s Wear: turn left halfway along the fabric section and walk all the way to the end. The shop is run by a shy yet friendly father-and-son duo.
I bought a beautiful áo dài and several light, stylish modernized traditional outfits. Enjoying such attentive, local service in the middle of a crowded market made it a genuinely pleasant shopping experience. The quality was great for the price, and I’ll definitely come back next time I’m in Ho Chi Minh City.
BTW, Vietnamese girls are quite slim—this is the first time in my life I’ve ever bought an XXL

user_reviews.Name: Ray Sim
user_reviews.Description:
Typical market. Items on sale were more or less similar. Dominantly selling cloth, nuts and dried goods in the main area. There was a wet market behind it, food stalls on one side and mainly fruits stalls on the other side.

There was this nice dried goods stall named A Muoi in it.

Friendly staffs who can speak Mandarin, Cantonese, Japanese, and some English languages. Not pushy and were patience.

They were more than happy to let you sample any item generously.

The cashew nut with skin was plump and creamy. The macadamia nut was sweet and creamy. The pumpkin seed was nice.

The dried mango was meaty, fragrant, and not overly sweet.

The chilli pink guava was nice.

The mango and lychee soft sweets had unique jelly liked texture, fragrant, and not overly artificially sweet. Yummy.

This market was a 4/5

user_reviews.Name: Nguyễn Mạnh Giáp
user_reviews.Description:
Tan Dinh Market

This is a long-standing traditional market located in the heart of Ho Chi Minh City.

The market is divided into several sections: dried goods, household items, fresh food, and a food court area.

If you want to experience the charm of a traditional Vietnamese market, you should visit this place at least once.

user_reviews.Name: Richard A Francisco
user_reviews.Description: Tan Dinh market is about 50 meters away from Tan Dinh Catholic church. It's on a busy street so it can be hard to cross if you're coming from the other side. Typical of Vietnamese markets, clothing and dry goods are up front while fresh food is at the back.

user_reviews.Name: vu nguyen
user_reviews.Description: Bustling market that had restaurants, clothes , fruits, meats, seafood and everything in between. Good  spot to wander around and see how the locals live.

title: Chợ tạm Làng Đại Học
address: 6/9 Đường Vào Làng, Đại Học, Linh Xuân, Hồ Chí Minh 740000, Vietnam
plus_code: VQCX+Q9 Linh Xuan, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Võ Bình Thới - Sifu
user_reviews.Description:
Chợ tạm Làng Đại Học, tọa lạc tại số 6/9 Đường Vào Làng, khu vực Làng Đại học, phường Tân Phú (TP. Thủ Đức), là điểm mua sắm quen thuộc của sinh viên, người lao động và cư dân xung quanh. Chợ mang đậm nét sinh hoạt đời thường với không khí nhộn nhịp từ sáng sớm, phản ánh rõ nhịp sống giản dị nhưng sôi động của khu vực đông dân cư, trường học.
Các gian hàng được bố trí dọc hai bên lối đi, bày bán đa dạng thực phẩm tươi sống như rau xanh, củ quả, trái cây, thịt cá, hải sản, trứng, gia vị và nhiều mặt hàng thiết yếu hằng ngày. Rau củ luôn tươi mới, phong phú chủng loại; cá tôm được bán ngay trong ngày; trái cây theo mùa có giá cả phải chăng. Người bán thân thiện, dễ trao đổi, tạo cảm giác gần gũi cho người mua, đặc biệt phù hợp với sinh viên và người thuê trọ.
Dù là chợ tạm, không gian vẫn giữ được nét đặc trưng của chợ truyền thống Việt Nam: giản dị, mộc mạc nhưng đầy sức sống. Chợ tạm Làng Đại Học không chỉ là nơi cung cấp nhu yếu phẩm mà còn là điểm giao thoa của đời sống cộng đồng, góp phần tạo nên bản sắc sinh hoạt riêng của khu vực Làng Đại học Thủ Đức.

title: Thảo Điền Market
address: Nguyễn Văn Hưởng, Thảo Điền, An Khánh, Hồ Chí Minh, Vietnam
plus_code: RP2H+W7 An Khanh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Morry Tafakory
user_reviews.Description:
Thảo Điền Market – A Disorganized Gem Hiding Culinary Gold

Thảo Điền Market in District 2 is not the sanitized, air-conditioned market experience you might expect. It is a raw, bustling, and wonderfully chaotic slice of local life in Saigon's expat-heavy Thảo Điền neighborhood. For visitors, it can be overwhelming; for residents, it's a daily lifeline offering both frustration and fantastic finds.

The main, covered section is a dense labyrinth of stalls selling everything from fresh produce, dripping seafood, and hanging meats to household goods and cheap clothing. The sensory overload is immediate: shouts of prices, the scent of herbs mingling with raw meat, and the constant hum of motorbikes weaving through narrow aisles. It's messy, humid, and not for the faint of heart.

However, the true magic lies in the perimeter. Circling the market building is where you'll find the neighborhood's best-kept culinary secrets. Here, unassuming food stalls and makeshift plastic-table restaurants serve exceptional, authentic dishes. You can find everything: flawless bánh mì for breakfast, steaming bowls of hủ tiếu or bún riêu for lunch, and outstanding regional specialties from central Vietnamese bún bò Huế to southern bánh canh cua. The quality is often superior to more polished cafes, at a fraction of the price.

Ultimately, Thảo Điền Market is a dichotomy. It’s disorganized and challenging, yet vibrant and authentic. Skip it if you seek comfort and order. Embrace it if you’re an adventurous foodie or want to observe the unfiltered rhythm of local commerce. Look past the initial chaos, head straight for the food stalls on the edges, and you’ll discover why this market remains an indispensable, if imperfect, heart of the community.

user_reviews.Name: Vaisakhan S
user_reviews.Description: The best place to find affordable items in Thao Dien like food, tofu, vegetables, fruits, meat, fish, egg, dried shrimp, and other cooking essentials. Few shops are available for clothes, hardware etc. Some vendors sell cooked food also inside that you could try. I go there in a weekly basis to purchase all items for the weekdays. Affordable rates than the supermarket. People are friendly, just point out what you want and they will show you the price. I did not have to bargain here as the prices were lesser than the supermarket prices. Can use metro and exit at Thao Dien station and walk to the market. Not so tidy and since they have meat products will not be the best place if you are very sensitive to smell (should be fine for most of the people). I have survived without knowing any Vietnamese, you can too.

user_reviews.Name: NGUYEN TONY
user_reviews.Description: The pool is located on 4th floor of Thao Dien Pearl. It is so clean and the environment is so nice. It is not crowded so you could swim comfortably. There are options of paying daily or monthly with reasonable price. On average, you pay just around 30,000VND for a day. I would recommemd swimming here.

user_reviews.Name: Nguyen Thi Diem Trinh
user_reviews.Description: I'm really payment my order but I'm no time wait for take my drink that's why really done know drink I'm order good of not.

user_reviews.Name: chi hui Yu
user_reviews.Description: On google it says 24 hr open but I am not sure if it’s correct or not . I got there around 13:30 but it’s pretty dead at that time and not so clear on first floor so I didn’t continue explore further.around the market there are many vendors selling fruit. Normally I would love to go to local market to at least eat something but unfortunately I couldnt get anything from this market

title: An Dong Market
address: 34-36 An Dương Vương, Phường 9, An Đông, Hồ Chí Minh 700000, Vietnam
plus_code: QM5C+7W An Dong, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam
description: Indoor market with a huge selection of goods including fabrics, clothing, dried foods & handicrafts.

user_reviews.Name: Reno
user_reviews.Description:
I visited this market because I heard it was cheaper than Ben Thanh Market.

The vendors aren’t as aggressive as at Ben Thanh, and it doesn’t have the same lively atmosphere, but the market is air-conditioned, so it’s a good spot if you want to shop quietly and take your time.

Since there are fewer customers, some vendors quietly follow you while you browse, which can feel a bit pressuring.
I was looking for casual T-shirts and pants, but the fashion felt very dated compared to other markets, so I didn’t buy anything.

Overall, Ben Thanh and Saigon Square offer a much wider variety of fashion and souvenirs.
If you have plenty of time, it’s worth a visit, but if your schedule is tight, I’d stick with Ben Thanh or Saigon Square.

user_reviews.Name: Daniel Tran
user_reviews.Description:
Visit: October 2025

Loved this market. Prices aren't atrocious and some stalls mainly do wholesale. You can bargain with them. There's many floors with lots to see.

Great place to get coffee, dried fruits, clothing, etc. went to B35 for weasel poop coffee and dried fruits and the owner was really kind! E32 had excellent quality clothes as well

user_reviews.Name: SICILIAN-AMERICAN
user_reviews.Description: My first time at this market and I liked it. They have lots of clothing and some souvenirs too. Their selection and prices were good. I got a nice quality pair of cargo shorts with metal zipper and metal snap buttons for 290k ($11 USD) they wouldn’t bargain but I thought the price was reasonable for the quality. They also had my size for 6ft 1in 215lb foreigner. Also,they were not pushy like in other markets and I respect that. Highly recommended

user_reviews.Name: Ray Tan
user_reviews.Description: It’s a relatively huge market selling almost everything u can think off. Upper floors are stalls selling clothes and some trinkets shops. A lot of the vendors can speak basic mandarin. The lower level basement are shops mostly selling jewelry. Worth a visit if in District 5.

user_reviews.Name: T Nguyen
user_reviews.Description: Food court on first floor. Shoes, fabrics, clothings, etc…on Second floor. Plaza is clean and a bit narrow between shops. Lots of pressures in food court to eat at their stall. Clean restroom. Easy come and go for Grab and buses.

user_reviews.Name: Tim Chu
user_reviews.Description: This market is definitely not as agrressive as Ben Thanh, and the prices here are somewhat reasonable compared to Ben Thanh. However, you will still end up paying a tourist tax here without bargaining or negotiation with the vendors. Locals visit here as well for great wholesale price. Multiple floors to visit, nice AC inside as well.

user_reviews.Name: Sandra Pfeiffer
user_reviews.Description: Great local market with jewellery, clothing and food of all kinds. Original and great to get in touch, we were the only tourists there. And fruits are delicious.

user_reviews.Name: Andrews
user_reviews.Description: Local clothes market catering to local people. This is a good market if you want to buy things in bulk. This market mainly has clothing, footwear, home decor, wooden art and crafts. Traditional decorative articles are also available. One unique thing we can buy is the snake wine on 3rd Floor. Price seem to be quite reasonable. We also have money exchange in the basement immediately after you enter the building.

plus_code: QJXW+8Q Tan Binh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam
description: Lively market with vendors for fruit, vegetables, meat, spices, household items & clothes.

user_reviews.Name: Tim Chu
user_reviews.Description: This is a great local market that gives you a taste of what it means to be a citizen of Ho Chi Minh City. As usual, you would see the meat and produce section here. You would also see a lot of venders selling clothing items and accessories. Expect to pay tourist tax here but not as bad as some other popular spots in the city. Reasonable price here, very friendly (and profit making) people.

user_reviews.Description:
A market where the locals go to buy groceries and eat. There is fresh fish,  fruits, meat, spices on sale. There are also new clothes, second-hand clothes, new shoes, reject shoes, sandals and slippers on sale.

I tried the Banh Flan and it was excellent. The fresh coconut water went for VD$15k (about US$0.65). Cheap!

user_reviews.Description: Great local market. I bought a t-shirt for 40k. Lots of food and second hand clothes, shoes, etc.

CHEAP PRICES I TELL U. I got 4 skirts for 200,000 VND (70k VND per piece but the lady gave me a discount). Skirts that look similar to those in Closete.Saigon in Cafe Apartment.

Only of the original markets in the city. It still has that same original / authentic charm and feel.
It’s just a short 200m street but it’s bustling and feels authentic.
It’s not far from the Saigon Center mall so easy to visit.
The area is changing so not sure how much longer this market will survive. There are several very cool hidden bars popping up, and the winner of “best restaurant in Vietnam” is located on this market street too (Restaurant Anan).

user_reviews.Description: Even they call it old market but you can find lots of interesting things, as a photographer, and as a tourist, to discover. The road is filled with lots of stalls and food, both sides, where you can enjoy the local food (springrolls, noodle, milktea, ...), and where you can take lots of nice photo of a local life~

user_reviews.Name: Glen Belovsky
user_reviews.Description: 1 block long market with food, groceries, meat, and liquor. Cool to knock around if you're at nearby Saigon Square or Nguyen Hue.

user_reviews.Name: Hieu Huynh
user_reviews.Description: Ton That Dam street is located in the heart of district 1 Sai Gon but there are so many market stalls that very dirty and ugly looking 👎 This adversely affected the urban landscape of the center of Saigon !!!

user_reviews.Name: Anh Tuan Nguyen
user_reviews.Description: hidden bar inside the old market , one of the most oldest quater of our city

user_reviews.Name: Joseph Godfrey Crasta
user_reviews.Description: Walk through street with wet market. Meat stalls, fish, vegetables, tender coconut, fruits and liquor Vietnamese style on the road side. wide choices very clean. Yes of course food stalls too at either end. I was here when the world locked down for COVID and not a single stall was scalping anyone.

user_reviews.Name: Catherine Montiel
user_reviews.Description: Typical Vietnamese market, you can find anything from sea living things to deodorants and Brand clothing.

user_reviews.Name: Juergen Mohs
user_reviews.Description: A must for tourists. This marked did not change the last decades. This is the place where Saigon people buy the daily food freshly. Its in the centre and you can combine with many tourists markets and shops near by. Have A nice walk around - JM

plus_code: QPJ2+53 Sai Gon, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Naoya Ishida
user_reviews.Description:
I went to the Russian Market after it moved. (The exterior of the building and the entrance are a little difficult to find, but please check the attached photos.)
There are stores on the first and second floors, but only the second floor was opened. There stores were more organized than the previous location and the environment was bright and clean. There were a lot of winter clothes, but they also sold bags and other items. The prices were relatively low, and I bought a backpack.

user_reviews.Name: Julie Monast-Nadeau
user_reviews.Description: Visited the new location on November 2. There were a handful of stall operating, a good dozens and many empty stalls not yet setup or moved. The new location is nice in the 1st floor.  Excellent for large size shopping and some good gems.

user_reviews.Name: Adnan Buchari
user_reviews.Description: Looking for jackets, ended up buying backpacks  at this B38 shop. The best price from all the shops here. Helen will give you the best price and she will gives you advice to go shopping in Ho Chi Minh. B38 is highly recommends if you are looking for bags!

user_reviews.Name: Yukima
user_reviews.Description: Market on 2 floors selling mainly jackets, coats and bags (but there are also other kind of clothes too). Good place to shop, the vendors are friendly, I walked around trying different coats and got one for 850k đ.

user_reviews.Name: Tony Toka
user_reviews.Description: Hello friends!  Listen there is confusion about this Russian market because they have kept the orginal name but moved a new location central in district 1.   They have also kept all the old positive reviews.  If you want to judge this location look at the newest reviews.  (Vietnamese Russian Mall). At this new location  there are only about 10 to 15 stalls, the first and third floor are still under construction.  I hope this place will flourish in the future but I hate to see someone come here thinking it's the old Location.  The original location (along the river) still exists under its new name the "Viet Nga Mall"

user_reviews.Name: TM D
user_reviews.Description: There are now TWO Russian markets. As mentioned by another reviewer the old location on Vo Van Kiet is still there but the new name is Viet Nga Mall. It doesn’t have a google map listing yet so might be tricky to find if you don’t have the address. This is where most of the sellers are. The owner of the brand Cho Nga has opened the Hai Ba Trung location but since it’s fairly new there’s not that many sellers.

user_reviews.Name: 麓健太郎

It appears they moved in October 2025.
New address: Floor 125, Hai Ba Trung Street, Ben Nghe Ward, District 1

I went there right away, but there were only a few stores open, and it looked like new stores were opening soon.

A drive by motorcyclist tried to snatch my mates phone and drive off but we were able to get him off her... be alert but have fun with the food!

user_reviews.Description: At night, there are a lot of choices for small snacks, chicken rices and deserts. Quite cheap as well and the quality is good. This area is famous for their night time restaurants/stalls.

Cheap, very cheap price

Cons :

user_reviews.Name: Jerry Vo
user_reviews.Description: I found my bun ca nha trang here, very yummy. This place offer a wide variety of food places. The cleaniness is questionable. I always opted for hot food just to be safe.

user_reviews.Description: If you live on this area, it’s very convenient to shop some clothing or any personal hygiene. There’s also food court downstairs from dessert to bowl soup, which ever your heart content content.

user_reviews.Name: Mrinal Thakore
user_reviews.Description: Best market to shop. Prices are extremely reasonable. No need to negotiate over here. Shop keepers are super friendly and not aggressive to force you to buy.

user_reviews.Name: Looda Kramer
user_reviews.Description: Vietnam, as the locals live. Awful

user_reviews.Name: Ak
user_reviews.Description: Many of the sellers at this market were still open and doing their businesses at night even after 8pm. The market itself is pretty spread out.

user_reviews.Name: Mister Gee
user_reviews.Description: Nice food stalls, authentice vietnam market

user_reviews.Name: Abel Alejandro
user_reviews.Description: Best market to have a sensational breakfast. Make sure you have a bowl of Bún mắm there, it's the best I ate in Saigon.

user_reviews.Name: Yohana Martinez
user_reviews.Description: Google says this market is closed after 11am. While the food might not be open many clothing and accessories shops are! I went at 4pm and it was open. Just go inside up the stairs it’s a tight place but cool to visit if you’re exploring District 4

user_reviews.Name: Mark Clarke
user_reviews.Description: Bought a huge bouquet of beautiful roses, very reasonable price

title: Ho Thi Ky Food Street
address: Hồ Thị Kỷ phường Vườn Lài, Vườn Lài, Hồ Chí Minh 70350, Vietnam
plus_code: QM8G+G9 Vuon Lai, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

complete_address.street: Hồ Thị Kỷ phường Vườn Lài

user_reviews.Name: AmyinNewzealand
user_reviews.Description:
Amazing! Highly recommend!
Authentic Local Experience!
If you like seafood! Or are interested in trying out some local cuisine that you can do on your budget! This is it!

Friendly people! Local living! Wide variety of food options!

Lobster, crab, oysters, scallops, prawns, octopus, snails, noodles, rice, vegetables, soups, stir fry.

It starts about 5pm. Goes till 10-11pm.

user_reviews.Name: Mai N. Kristensen
user_reviews.Description:
Great authentic street food with loads of different local foods to choose from. Come here for a real Vietnamese experience. Came here with my family and enjoyed the atmosphere.

Only downside: Heard from several people that you should stay clear of the first seafood vendor on the right hand side as he/they will scam the life out of you and don’t serve fresh seafood (see photo).

Otherwise definitely worth a visit!

user_reviews.Name: S Young
user_reviews.Description:
Our hotel is within 10 minutes walking distance of this street, and we noticed that during the day it functions as a flower market, while at night it transforms into a food street. Many of the food vendors set up in the evening.

Food options are fairly limited and repetitive—mostly skewers (sausages, hot dogs, and various meats), seafood like lobster and snails, bánh khọt (savory mini pancakes), and fresh juices. There isn’t a wide variety, but it’s lively and convenient. One of the night markets in HCMC.

We tried the bánh khọt in mixed option which came with eight pieces for 45,000 VND. They were freshly made and tasty, though a bit oily and greasy, as expected. Vendors only accept cash.

We also grabbed sugarcane juice from a nearby stall and asked for less ice. Even then, it still came with one scoop (apparently the standard is two). Overall, it’s a fun street to walk through at night, especially if you’re nearby and want a quick, casual bite.

user_reviews.Name: Amarylise Bessey
user_reviews.Description: The food choices are amazing and it's a great atmosphere except for the motorbikes constantly coming down the very crowded street! Expect lots of little stool action! Grab some skewers of meat and some fruit tra.  If you are feeling more adventurous,  check out the fresh seafood at the entrance end

user_reviews.Name: lida chan
user_reviews.Description:
There’s an amazing variety of food and snacks, all very affordable and delicious. I loved it so much that I went two days in a row and tried different things each time. The market starts getting lively around 5 pm and while there are people around, it doesn’t feel overly crowded.

If you enjoy street food and want to experience local flavors, I definitely recommend checking this place out when you’re in Ho Chi Minh City.

user_reviews.Name: Weijie Aw
user_reviews.Description: A narrow alley with a few food stalls that have limited selection of street food. If you are comparing it to street food markets of Bangkok or Taiwan's, you will be disappointed but if you are there just looking to have some cheap local street food and chill, you will be satisfied! 👍🏻

user_reviews.Name: Dian Kadek
user_reviews.Description: I visited Ho Thi Ky twice on my last trip. I wish I go there more often🥹 So many food choices, fresh seafood, light snacks, and else. This is definitely a place you don't want to miss!

user_reviews.Name: Pawas Shrivastava
user_reviews.Description: Amazing food options - so many seafood varieties on the street. However most people here don’t speak English - and I couldn’t understand most of the foood. However, pictures and google translate helped a lot. Had class, multiple types of snails and oysters - loved it

user_reviews.Name: BBO Danny
user_reviews.Description:
There are wet market and store for local.
I was going to buy some instant noodles and hope to have a reasonable price.
But they knew I am foreigner then gave me the price which is double to supermarket.
Maybe vietnam culture. Always need to negotiate but I didn’t.

user_reviews.Name: Richard A Francisco
user_reviews.Description: Thai Binh market is a market where the locals shop. It's at a busy intersection and has plenty of fresh food for sale. I'm talking freshly butchered pork, beef, chicken, duck as an as fish, shellfish, fruits and vegetables! They also have a section for clothing and dry goods.

user_reviews.Name: xin
user_reviews.Description:
Had breakfast at Thai Binh Market like a local. Not many tourists around and the food is very flavourful!! especially the flan!!

Oh and just a heads up that the cleanliness isn’t great, but if you don’t mind that, it’s a nice spot to explore the wet and dry morning market

user_reviews.Name: vu nguyen
user_reviews.Description: Local morning markets are my jam!!! Love seeing all the food stalls and all the vendors getting ready for the day.

user_reviews.Name: Kelly Chen
user_reviews.Description:
‼️Overpriced Fruit – Tourist Scam‼️

Avoid this fruit seller lady!!!

1/ I bought longan here and was charged 50,000 VND per kg,

2/ which is 4–5 times the local price (normally around 10,000 VND/kg in August 2025)

user_reviews.Name: chaiting Teo
user_reviews.Description: Very local market, here sellers better than the famous market,the price is reasonable and sellers more friendly

user_reviews.Name: Jena Huynh
user_reviews.Description: I recently revisited this charming people's market, Thái Bình Market, after 8 years of being away and that's been in my family for three generations! My grandmother started our stall (SẠP 154-155) when the market first opened, and my relatives have maintained her tradition of fair pricing across our diverse array of goods, dry foods, and products. From all the stalls throughout the market, your eyes feast on colorful displays of everything from fresh produce to handcrafted items, and to a whole spectrum of everyday useful goods. Don't worry about language barriers—just point or show a photo, and our friendly vendors (including my relatives!) will happily assist. It's not just our family business but the humblest of vendors and everyday folks buying and selling goods that make up a vibrant community space connecting me to my grandmother's legacy.

user_reviews.Name: Tze Ying Teoh
user_reviews.Description: If you are searching for a nice and affordable local breakfast,  Thai Binh Market is a good place to go for. There are varieties of food to try on, and it tastes very good. Centella drink is very refreshing, and grill pork vermicelli is super amazing. The food stall only accepts cash and might not be fluent in English but still able to speak simple English.

title: Ho Thi Ky Flower Market
address: Hẻm 52 Hồ Thị Kỷ, Phường 1, Vườn Lài, Hồ Chí Minh 700000, Vietnam
plus_code: QM8G+5R Vuon Lai, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam
description: Vendors with colorful bouquets of flowers line this busy outdoor market also offering street food.

user_reviews.Name: Abdulaziz Karimy
user_reviews.Description:
A beautiful and lively place worth visiting

The Flower Night Market in Ho Chi Minh City is definitely worth a visit. I really enjoyed walking between the flower shops the atmosphere is colorful, calm, and very pleasant, especially at night. It’s a great place to relax, take photos, and enjoy the local vibe.

Right next to the flower market, there is also a night market full of street food and seafood. The variety is impressive, the food smells amazing, and there are plenty of options to try local dishes and fresh seafood. It’s a perfect combination of sights, smells, and flavors.

Overall, it’s a lovely place to walk around, eat, and experience a different side of Ho Chi Minh City. Highly recommended for anyone visiting the city.

user_reviews.Name: Francine Laura
user_reviews.Description:
The Ho Thi Ky Market is Saigon’s largest wholesale flower market, operating 24/7.   Most of the flowers come from the Da Lat, north of here.

We happened to be there on a very low key day since it was the last day of Tet but we still saw many beautiful flowers, especially the orchids.

user_reviews.Name: Ray Tan
user_reviews.Description: Came here after seeing ridic prices online. It’s seriously rows of florists here, with some food stalls. Bought a bouquet of pink roses here for VND250k. Not sure if that’s the market rate but at least it’s a fraction of the price i saw online. The stall i bought from even offers delivery for a small fee. Will definitely return to visit in the future.

user_reviews.Name: Fuiringu Ou
user_reviews.Description: Very fun place to walk - nice flowers to look at and particularly enjoyed looking at the food stalls. Prices were very reasonable as well

user_reviews.Name: C
user_reviews.Description:
Ho Thi Ky Flower Market in Ho Chi Minh City is a vibrant, colorful spot known as the city’s largest wholesale flower market. Tucked away in District 10, it’s been operating since the 1980s and is often called the city’s “Little Dalat,” thanks to its fresh flowers sourced from Dalat and other regions.

The narrow alleyways are filled with stalls overflowing with roses, orchids, sunflowers, lilies, and exotic tropical blooms, alongside decorative plants and floral accessories. The atmosphere is bustling yet charming, especially early in the morning when the market is at its liveliest.

Beyond flowers, the area has become a bit of a street food hotspot, with vendors selling snacks, drinks, and local delicacies.

While it’s primarily a wholesale market, visitors are welcome to stroll, take photos, and purchase flowers by the bunch at reasonable prices.

If you’re looking for a colorful, authentic local experience, Ho Thi Ky Flower Market is definitely worth a visit — best in the early morning or late afternoon for the freshest flowers and most lively atmosphere.

user_reviews.Name: Stephen
user_reviews.Description: 24 hour flower Street. If you want flowers this is the place to go. So many options so many markets

user_reviews.Name: Kristine Hara
user_reviews.Description:
The flowers here are really beautiful and they also have some great vases/pottery at very reasonable prices. It is a really impressive range of flowers, you can find anything here!

Heads up that traffic is really intense here and motorcycles can drive through the market. So it is a bit stressful to look at the flowers during rush hour (5-7pm), I recommend going at quieter times.

user_reviews.Name: 21 Wiser
user_reviews.Description: Flowers of all kinds on one side, a culinary experience on the other. A bit of a hidden gem outside of the city centre but definitely worth visiting. If you come in the morning, you will get to experience a bit of the local life too. Not too far away from Ben Thanh Market. You should definitely spare a bit of time to explore this place a bit more. There aren't that many stalls like you might see in other night markets, but still plenty of good options for a way better price than at Ben Thanh Market.

title: Central Market
address: 4 Phạm Ngũ Lão, Phường Phạm Ngũ Lão, Bến Thành, Hồ Chí Minh, Vietnam
plus_code: QM9V+JF Ben Thanh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

complete_address.borough: Phường Phạm Ngũ Lão, Ben Thanh

user_reviews.Name: Waan waan
user_reviews.Description: This market is a nice place to relax and take a break from walking around the city. There is a dedicated dining area with a wide variety of restaurants to choose from, though the prices are relatively high. The clothing and souvenir items are quite common and not particularly unique. Overall, it’s a good spot for a casual stroll and some downtime while exploring Ho Chi Minh City, especially with the comfort of air-conditioned indoor spaces.

user_reviews.Name: Selma Jensen
user_reviews.Description: Ho Chi Minh Central Market is an amazing place for shopping and exploring! It’s like an outlet where you can find clothes, bags, and shoes from all kinds of brands at really affordable prices, even if some are from previous seasons. The market is always busy with locals and visitors hunting for great deals. There’s also a food and drink section, which is perfect for taking a break and trying some local flavors. Definitely a must-visit if you love shopping and experiencing the lively market atmosphere!

user_reviews.Name: Vince Yen
user_reviews.Description:
Compared with other markets around the town. This one is highly recommended. Quite new with some stores & dinners. Easier to shop since most merchants here are reasonably / clearly priced. Therefore, I stocked the most and did enjoy shopping here. Souvenirs / clothes / sneakers are good to buy. It is comfortable to shop here, the store owners are not that "pushy" and love to close the deals with customers in some way. After shopping, you can simply dine / have drinks here for refreshing.
( in short, you can always ask for some more discounts for shopping here. )

user_reviews.Name: Farah Tugad
user_reviews.Description:
My first time visiting Central Market, and it turned out to be a fun and interesting stop. As soon as I walked in, I found a busy food court filled with different stalls. Theres a food court down, and a wide variety of food to choose from, so it’s easy to explore and try something new.

If you visit, the Indian food stall is definitely a must-try. It’s flavorful, filling, and worth the hype.

Most of the stalls are not the traditional “OG” type, but they’re clean, modern, and class A, which gives the place a comfortable vibe for tourists. Just a heads-up: prices are foreigner-friendly (meaning slightly higher), but the good thing is you can haggle or bargain. Most sellers speak some English or use translations, so communicating is not a problem at all.

Overall, Central Market is a fun stop for food lovers, especially if you want variety and easy choices right near Bùi Viện. A nice place to explore, eat, and enjoy.

user_reviews.Name: Joseph
user_reviews.Description: I actually liked this more than Ben Thanh! Ben Thanh has everything, but Central Market feels better overall — cleaner, less crowded, and way cheaper. Lots of good finds and souvenir options here. We got most of our pasalubong from this place. Highly recommend checking it out if you want good deals without the chaos.

user_reviews.Name: Kenny Tang
user_reviews.Description:
If you want air cons when you eat, come here!

Clean modern market with a food court in the centre. The food is made to order and not super expensive. I thought the Bun Rieu was great! There are also clean toilet facilities here.

user_reviews.Name: vivek
user_reviews.Description:
Happy to have stumbled upon this market.
So vibrant and diverse!
Compared to Ben Than market, this is much more organised and helpful.
It's a local market for the locals. So many local brands and VERY REASONABLY PRICED.
At other one the rates start so high that one doesn't even want to bargain.
Found some really good tshirts at very good price!
Definately a place to visit for shopping!
There's also a big food court with a large number of options to choose from!

user_reviews.Name: Marit Cat
user_reviews.Description: Perfect spot for shopping high end goods in one area. The placed is air conditioned and have a perfect food court for several choices of food that are really delicious with really good prices. Bring cash for shopping and shops are open with haggling prices.

user_reviews.Description:
Stumpled upon this market on a Sunday around midday. Oh wow - there were simply no other Westerners about.
One can buy anything from Bra's to Vegetable and anything in between.

user_reviews.Name: valeriy ogurtsov
user_reviews.Description: In centre, you can get there everything for meal, meat, a lot of fruits, fish and shrimps still alive, I found even turtles 😳

user_reviews.Name: Mihai
user_reviews.Description: Thi Nghe market in Ho Chi Minh City is a true reflection of everyday life, bustling with energy and authentic charm. While it may be a bit messy, it more than makes up for it with a wide variety of fresh produce, street food, and household goods. The surrounding food stalls serve delicious local dishes at affordable prices, making it a great spot to experience the city's vibrant street food culture.

user_reviews.Name: The MY World
user_reviews.Description: It's very locak market near dist.1. I think this is the nearest market as very local market. Surely there are some market in dist.1, but they have been no more local market, targetting to foreigner a bit. Obviously the target this market is local people. So the price is cheap. It's also located beside river and the opposit side of the river is zoo. Anyway it's good to come here if you feel local market.

user_reviews.Name: Felena Hanson
user_reviews.Description:
This market is very dirty.
Saw two rats run by 😳
I do not recommend if you are accustomed to western standards… steer clear.

user_reviews.Name: Hieu Nguyen
user_reviews.Description:
The local local market that expats should visit. It is the model of cover-market originally from France. There are some small roads inside in a well-arranged shape. The price is a bit higher than other local markets but the ambience and the feeling it brings is quite comfortable and energetic to me.
The architecture of the neighborhood makes me feel relaxing as well.
There are some crowded food stall/store nearby. One is the 'banh mi' (baguette) stall which has a good patê, opens around 4pm til 2am of the next day. Price is 15k for one. Another is a noodle store which is famous of the Aromatic broth with Roasted ducked and egg noodle. Price is 80k/bowl (~ $4). You should try.

user_reviews.Name: Batman
user_reviews.Description: A great colourful flower market with delicious food stall for street food

user_reviews.Name: Duy Nguyen
user_reviews.Description: My childhood market. It got rebuilt but the lay out is basically the same. You can find tons of good eateries around and inside the market.

user_reviews.Name: Vũ Gia Bảo
user_reviews.Description: nv tụ lại nch với nhau, tính tiền thì lề mề, đồ ăn thì kh hâm lại

user_reviews.Name: Le Hoang Nhat Tan
user_reviews.Description: May cho cửa hàng là giới hạn đánh giá thấp nhất là một sao nên được đánh giá 1 sao, chứ training nhân viên không tới mà dược ra đứng quầy như vậy thì không chấp nhận được. Không phải lần đầu tiên mình mua hàng ở đây mà gặp bị nhân viên thái độ. Cực kì thất vọng, đều là sinh viên thì ai cũng biết đi làm mệt thế nào, nhưng mà đó không phải là lí do mấy bạn thái độ với khách hàng, khách hàng mua hàng chứ không phải xin mấy bạn bán, khách hàng vô mà mấy bạn đứng quay lưng lại xong bấm điện thoại, mình phải gọi lần thứ 2 bạn nhân viên mới quay lại nhận order của mình. Nói chung là cần xem xét lại.

user_reviews.Name: Minh Châu
user_reviews.Description:
07/03/2026 lúc 8g06p
Tôi có mua đồ bên mini với tổng tiền 24.000. Tôi không có tiền nhỏ nên đã đưa tờ 500.000. Khi nhận tờ tiền bạn nhân viên kêu “ Trời ơi “ vậy cho hỏi câu đó ý nghĩa là gì ? Và khi bỏ đồ vào bịch đưa khách nếu tôi không nắm kịp thì đã bị rớt xuống đất rồi. Đây không phải lần đầu tôi bị bạn này thái độ. Rất nhiều lần tôi mua đồ và trúng bạn này thanh toán và hầu như lần nào thái độ cũng như 1. Mong bên ministop sẽ tiếp nhận ý kiến này và giải quyết ạ. Nhìn không nhầm thì bạn nhân viên tên Diễm Quỳnh ạ. Cảm ơn.

user_reviews.Name: Tâm Trần
user_reviews.Description: Thứ 4 (21/01/2026) Lúc 6g10p sáng nay sang mua đồ gặp nhân viên khó ở với khách. Không có tiền mặt để thối thì có rất nhiều cách để nói khách nhưng cách nói năng của bạn này rất khó chịu, cọc cằn. Bạn nam cao cao đeo kính nhân viên phục vụ ca sáng siêu tệ. Vì trải nghiệm này mà từ khách quen thành bỏ luôn ministop cơ sở này.

user_reviews.Name: Duong Ly
user_reviews.Description:
Nhân viên báo hết trứng bù Tokbokki, mình ok chấp nhận
Nhưng khi hết nước dừa matcha nhân viên k hề nói gì với mình mà gửi shipper đơn hàng đi giao, mình k nhận đc tiền hoàn ly nước sau đó ( sau khi shipper đã mang đơn hàng ăn đi) bạn nhân viên có gọi báo hết nước bù thêm Tokbokki vào nhưng nhìn phần ăn nó k bù tới 28k tiền ly nước, lẽ ra phải bù nhiều hoặc thối lại tiền chứ?
Cửa hàng trưởng k liên hệ gì với mình, mình cảm thấy bên cửa hàng giải quyết chưa thoả đáng
Còn về phần ăn này mọi người nghĩ nó đáng giá 66k ko????

user_reviews.Name: Trần Nhã Linh Nguyễn
user_reviews.Description:
Mình rất thích ghé GS25 này!
Không gian cửa hàng luôn sạch sẽ, sáng sủa, đi vào cảm thấy rất thoải mái. Hàng hóa đa dạng, từ đồ ăn vặt, nước uống cho đến những món tiện lợi như cơm hộp, mì ly, đều được sắp xếp gọn gàng, dễ tìm.
Nhân viên ở đây thân thiện, nhiệt tình, lúc nào cũng tươi cười chào hỏi khách.
Giá cả hợp lý, nhiều khuyến mãi hấp dẫn nên mỗi lần ghé là mua được đủ thứ. Đây đúng là địa điểm tiện lợi để ghé bất kỳ lúc nào trong ngày. Đặc biệt, không gian đẹp, sáng sủa nên check-in cũng xinh lắm luôn! 💙

user_reviews.Name: Phúc Lê
user_reviews.Description:
22h45 ngày 18/9/2025
mình đi làm về mua đống đồ này vì có chương trình khuyến mãi, ra tính 106k mình xin bill thanh toán thì nhân viên bảo không có kêu thông cảm, làm ăn lừa bịp mồ hôi xương máu người ta như vậy không bền đâu

user_reviews.Name: Minh Hà Haley
user_reviews.Description:
Tính ra lần đầu đi cửa hàng tiện lợi mà dui dẻ vậy luôn ak, mua có cái bánh mà bạn nhân viên tư vấn nhiệt tình quá trời luôn. Còn kêu sinh nhật ra mua bó bông bằng bánh kẹo rồi bạn gói free cho mình. Mình có thấy bạn ấy tên Chi.
Ban đầu thấy cửa hàng bị đánh giá thái độ nhân viên nên cũng ngại ghé mà lần này có cái nhìn khác hẳn về dịch vụ khách hàng 😋 Xin cám ơn.

user_reviews.Name: Tung Nguyen
user_reviews.Description:
My favorite 7-Eleven in all of Ho Chi Minh City!
There’s always a super chill and friendly dog hanging out right in front of the entrance – such a sweet vibe!
The store itself has everything you might need: sunscreen, nail clippers, 7-Eleven slippers – you name it.
I stayed at an Airbnb nearby and would always grab my drinks and my favorite Samanco ice cream here.
The staff is really nice and speaks good English. Plus, you can easily pay with Visa.
The store has air conditioning and even a small seating area with stools if you want to sit down and enjoy your instant ramen.
Such a great little spot!

user_reviews.Name: SAMRAT BORAH
user_reviews.Description: Super friendly staff, lot of options to select from for any sort of purchase and located very centrally excellent location. I love this place.

user_reviews.Name: vijay wadia
user_reviews.Description:
7 Eleven is a chain of stores famous for having all type of food items. This store is located on main road.
It's a small store but has all the required food and drinks.
They also have fresh fruits like coconut, apple, mangos, sweet lime, oranges, Avacado.
All the soft drinks as well as beers.
Chips, biscuits goes without saying.
All the products are at very reasonable price.

user_reviews.Name: Lily K
user_reviews.Description: First 7-11 I've checked out in vietnam. It had so many awesome and unique goodies that I cannot get back in the states. I love the quality of the food.  Everything was extremely affordable.

user_reviews.Name: Caramelita
user_reviews.Description: This 7-Eleven is a fun stop for anyone who enjoys trying new snacks. They have a wide selection of unique chips and candy flavors that you won’t usually find elsewhere, making it perfect for discovering something different. A convenient and enjoyable place to grab quick treats while exploring!

user_reviews.Name: Gemini Notes
user_reviews.Description:
Since food hygiene issues have become more serious, I’ve changed my eating habits. I now prefer larger, established restaurants, buy fruit from reliable sources, and usually get my drinks from convenience stores.

In Vietnam, food safety is no longer a minor concern or a matter of being picky. It has truly become a matter of survival. Eating well is important, but eating safely is what really gives me peace of mind.

user_reviews.Name: Tien Nguyen Krstic
user_reviews.Description: Great staff. I had a small travel to Thu Duc and visited here to get some breakfast and bamboo charcoal milk tea. The milk tea is made perfectly good, I've been trying it in other branches and already started to dislike the drink :))) but this place provokes back my love for charcoal milktea

user_reviews.Name: Hoàng Lê
user_reviews.Description: ok

user_reviews.Name: Rishabh Sharma
user_reviews.Description: nice staff

user_reviews.Name: Minh Tuấn Phan
user_reviews.Description: Good

user_reviews.Name: Mai Đức Phạm
user_reviews.Description: Ok

user_reviews.Name: Hoàng Thủy Tiên Võ
user_reviews.Description: Sản phẩm không vấn đề. Tuy nhiên mình không hài lòng về thái độ phục vụ của cửa hàng. Nhân viên nói chuyện cộc lốc, thiếu tôn trọng khách hàng. Hy vọng cửa hàng cải thiện để giữ khách lâu dài hơn.

user_reviews.Name: Thành Lợi Nguyễn
user_reviews.Description: Nhân Viên làm ca 0h15p ngày 14/2/2016 tại chi nhánh lê văn việt mua matcha hạnh nhân không cho ly đá , hỏi vấn đề tại sao không có ly đá nhân viên nói là không có ly đá phải mua thêm , lúc trước mình mua có nhân viên cho sẳn vào ly đá , thái độ nhân viên vào ca làm khó chịu cọc cằn cần xem lại thái độ làm việc , nhân viên như này thì chả bao giờ phát triển được

user_reviews.Name: dau be
user_reviews.Description: Hôm nay 11/12 lúc 21h mình ghé mua nước, thấy chương trình mua 3 lon nước tặng gấu bông, lúc thanh toán nhân viên báo hết gấu nên mình trả lại và chỉ mua 1 chai nước thì bạn nam nhân viên thái độ ra mặt, mình xin bịch đựng thì bạn ấy bảo mình cầm tay không và ko đưa bịch cho mình luôn. Nên thật sự chấn chỉnh lại thái độ 2 nhân viên :)

title: NamiMart
address: VRG5+993 NamiMart, Đông Hòa, Hồ Chí Minh, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Lợi Nguyễn Hữu
user_reviews.Description:
Chất lượng dịch vụ tệ!
Mở đầu ngày mới đi làm bằng thái độ của nhân viên thì cảm nhận mọi người ra sao?
Chuyện là sáng sớm mình có xuống mua bánh mì ăn sáng thì lúc thanh toán mình có hỏi bạn nhân viên nữ ( đeo mắt kính, đeo khẩu trang, tóc búi) là bao nhiêu để mình chuyển khoản thì bạn ấy trả lời nhỏ vì bạn ấy đeo khá dày, nên mình đã nghe nhầm là 10k và hỏi lại bạn cho chắc thì bạn ấy trả lời kiểu “nhìn lên màn hình QR là thấy số tiền đấy”. Thế thì à mình mới biết là 14k và chuyển đúng số tiền đó và mình k còn nghe lời gì từ bạn ấy nữa
