Special thanks to Chuong and Khang at the front desk for their excellent service, and to the guard who assisted us every day with getting in and out of our Grab car — such thoughtful help really made a difference.
The room was small but very clean and well maintained, with all the basic amenities we needed for a comfortable stay. The location is also excellent, close to many popular Saigon tourist attractions and easy to get around.
Thank you to everyone at Concept Hotel for making our stay so pleasant and enjoyable. It was truly a great experience, and we would happily stay here again

user_reviews.Name: Renee Pugh
user_reviews.Description: I was booked into concept hotel through my dental clinic. I stayed in the city view suite on the 5th floor. The room was small but it had everything I needed to recover comfortably from surgery. The room boasts 2 aircons. 1 split system and ducted system. Both were powerful and quiet to cool the room to the temp of my liking. Bathroom is beautiful and comes with the shampoo, shower gel and complimentary items like toothbrush, cotton buds and floss. My husband and toddler stayed with me in the room, it served us well. Room service was great, fresh towels and bedding changed everyday. Fridge stayed cold and kept drinks/ice pack cold. We watched netflix at night on big tv. Our toddler often fell asleep to tv running. Hotel has 24 hour self serve laundromat, it is always clean. Staff are always present in lobby. Laundry capsules are complimentary. Staff are so friendly and kind! They really go over and beyond to keep their guests happy. Thank you Trang and team for being so accommodating. Especially to security man who always smiled and helped us into hotel. Finally, location of hotel is in prime position. Close to shops, street food, markets and more. We are so close to everything! Very happy with our stay here!

user_reviews.Name: C6400SUmanueld
user_reviews.Description: To be honest we took hours looking online on agoda and booking.com for the right hotel for us. Sadly many have bad reviews so we were never really sure.||After extensive research we chose the concept hotel at the city center. Upon arrival we were greeted by a fantastic guard in front of the hotel. This guy was always so happy and polite.||The staff of the hotel was very polite, well versed in english and very helpful. The checken took 1 minute.||The room was everything you could ask for. Although in the City center you could hear almost nothing on the 3rd of 9 floors. The room was clean, the sheets were freshly wasser, the ac was working and there was no trace of waterdamage, mold or ants. Out of 6 hotels in vietnam so far this was definetly the nicest. ||A cool extra is that the hotel has a free Gymnasium, free washing and drying, free WiFi and a scale to use for your luggage.

user_reviews.Name: peter parker
user_reviews.Description: My stay was amazing from start to finish! A friend suggested this hotel, and now I understand why. What really stood out was Thy she went above and beyond every single day. I’m not very familiar with Vietnam, so I kept asking for help, and she always answered with a smile and made everything so easy for me. Because of her, I felt comfortable and well taken care of. I’ll definitely come back here on my next trip. Truly recommend this place to everyone.

user_reviews.Name: Trip.com Member
user_reviews.Description: New hotel, cleanliness is well maintained. Reception staff is very helpful and well mannered.The hotel do provide free laundry service with dryer is adding points, drinking water machine provided in lobby which is very convenient for the customer. Highly recommended for the hotel.

user_reviews.Name: John chau nguyen
user_reviews.Description: There's nothing bad to say about this hotel, except it can get quite hectic when waiting on a grab during peak hours. Location is just a 8 minute walk to Bui vien street and you have numerous restaurants around the area. There's also a circle k and 7/11 that's nearby for your daily needs. The staff hear are amazing people and very accommodating. Each time we requested something for the room, they came within a minute. They also have free laundry and coffee on the first floor! Shout out to the security guy in the front. Super friendly and always goes out his way to help enter or exit our grabs rides. 10/10 I recommend and will be back.

user_reviews.Name: Emily Tran
user_reviews.Description: This was a last minute booking as our other accomodation cancelled on us. We were impressed with the service and the facilities here. You can even do your laundry which is so helpful when travelling. The rooms are modern and spacious, clean and aesthetically pleasing. Newly built and you cant beat the central location and the price! We already rebooked for when we return in a few days. Special thanks to Quin and Hang for their friendly service and making our stay so comfortable!

title: Vinpearl Landmark 81, Autograph Collection
address: 720A Điện Biên Phủ, Phường 22, Thạnh Mỹ Tây, Hồ Chí Minh 700000, Vietnam
plus_code: QPWC+3Q Thanh My Tay, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam
description: Chic rooms & suites in a polished hotel offering a spa, a pool & dining, plus an observation deck.

complete_address.state: Thanh My Tay, Ho Chi Minh

user_reviews.Name: Jonah Jagadeshan
user_reviews.Description:
This Autograph property is an absolute premium experience and lives up to its brand value. Firstly, it’s the tallest tower in all of Vietnam with great panoramic views of the entire city. Here’s a shot from my flight, it was cool to see the tower from up above.

The staff is polite and service oriented. Special shout out to Ted, Rachel, Chau Ann and Chris for making our stay here super special.

Connectivity: the place is close to the city center, 15min by Grab to Independence Palace, War Museum, Night Market and central market amongst other places. The building is also connected to a mall from the inside.

Food - Chef is great here, good spread of food for breakfast.

user_reviews.Name: Mark Lewis
user_reviews.Description:
I stayed at Vinpearl Landmark 81 for 3 days and 2 nights in the Panoramic Suite, and honestly, it was one of the most memorable hotel experiences I’ve ever had. From the moment I arrived, I felt like I was somewhere truly special. The check-in was seamless and relaxed, and I was up in my suite before I even got my luggage settled.

The suite itself was incredibly spacious, modern, and flooded with light from the massive floor-to-ceiling windows. Waking up to 360-degree panoramic views of Ho Chi Minh City and the Saigon River was unforgettable. I’d pour a coffee in the morning, sit by the window, and just watch the city come to life, it felt like my own private observatory above the skyline.

Everything in the room was super comfortable. The bed was so soft and inviting after long days exploring, and having a separate living space made the suite feel more like a luxury apartment than just a hotel room. I especially loved how the décor blended sleek modern design with high-end finishes, it all felt fresh and stylish.

The hotel’s facilities were next level too. I spent time at the gym and took a dip in the infinity pool with views that made swimming feel surreal, like floating above the whole city. The breakfast buffet at the Oriental Pearl restaurant was amazing, with a terrific variety of dishes and a coffee that hit the spot every morning.

What really stood out was how peaceful everything felt despite being in such a tall building. I loved watching the sunset and then seeing the city light up at night right from my suite, that view never got old. And even simple things like the in-room coffee machine or the plush towels added that extra bit of comfort that made the stay feel special.
( sadly I didn’t take any photos of the room but, I did take one of the pool )

user_reviews.Name: Anna S
user_reviews.Description:
A truly beautiful hotel! The views are amazing, especially at night! The rooms are comfortable and modern, and generally the place feels luxurious. It is pretty cozy and quieter than in downtown, and the building itself is definitely a landmark in Saigon (though the hotel doesn’t occupy the whole building of course). There is a shopping mall in the same building, so it’s convenient but not crowded. And the observation deck (you still have to buy tickets for it) is great!
Also the evening cocktail at the lounge is superb, we loved it!
The staff is so welcoming, kind and helpful! Especially the check-in and the valets downstairs, the barman at the executive lounge ( I unfortunately don’t remember the name, but we really thank him a lot)!

user_reviews.Name: Trip.com Member
user_reviews.Description: Staying at the Vinpearl Luxury Landmark 81 was an absolutely magnificent experience. The hotel truly lives up to its reputation as a world-class luxury destination, particularly due to its stunning location in Ho Chi Minh City. The panoramic views from the high-floor room were breathtaking, offering an unmatched perspective of the city and the Saigon River—truly unforgettable. The room itself was immaculately clean, beautifully furnished, and provided every comfort one could wish for. Beyond the impressive facilities, the service provided by the entire staff was exceptional; everyone was professional, warm, and highly attentive. I highly recommend this hotel for anyone seeking the ultimate luxury stay in Ho Chi Minh City. I can't wait to visit again!

user_reviews.Name: Mark Warnquist
user_reviews.Description: Very glad we chose the VinPearl for our last stay in Asia before heading home. The property itself is stunning and the views from our room and the restaurant are truly amazing!! But what sets the VinPearl apart in our view is the world class service. Across the board, everyone is quietly attentive and caring. It is clear that the VinPearl attracts very high quality talent. A special shout out to Mocha in the Oriental Pearl restaurant. She took such great care of us. Just one example is bringing us chamomille tea with ginger on her own initiative because she knew we were leaving early and wanted us to have best possible sleep. She is awesome!!

user_reviews.Name: Germain R
user_reviews.Description:
The view and the overall service were generally very good, and the staff were polite and professional. However, the room was extremely small for a five-night stay, which significantly affected the overall comfort.
Before arrival, Ms. Nhi Lam mentioned that she would monitor availability for a complimentary upgrade. Unfortunately, throughout the stay, there was no follow-up or proactive communication, and it felt lacking in sincerity—especially considering this is a low travel season.
As a Marriott Gold member, I was expecting a bit more recognition. I have stayed at comparable properties such as Phuket Marriott, St. Regis Singapore, JW Marriott Saigon, Ritz-Carlton Fukuoka, and Ritz-Carlton Bali, where the benefits and treatment for elite members were noticeably better. In comparison, this hotel did not offer any particularly special or thoughtful consideration for Gold members.
Overall, while the view and basic service were good, the room size and elite member experience did not meet expectations for a hotel at this level.

user_reviews.Name: Randall Stempler
user_reviews.Description: 5 stars for the view and service! Stunning luxury property located in Vietnam’s tallest building. Excellent Elite benefits—the lounge is beautiful with great food and even better views. The staff is world-class. The rooms look incredible, though I wish they were a bit more functional with more counter/table space. Still, a breathtaking stay. Highly recommend for the experience alone!

user_reviews.Name: Diana Pham
user_reviews.Description:
I stayed in a room with a panoramic view and club access. The room was nicely designed, though a bit on the smaller side. The staff were warm and attentive, and the bathroom was decent overall.

However, for a hotel positioning itself as a true 5-star property, I would strongly recommend upgrading the toilet spray hose to a proper bidet toilet system, similar to what hotels like The Reverie Saigon offer. It would significantly elevate the bathroom experience and align better with luxury expectations.

title: New World Saigon Hotel
address: 76 Lê Lai, Bến Thành, Hồ Chí Minh 700000, Vietnam
plus_code: QMCW+83 Ben Thanh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam
description: Upscale hotel offering elegant dining, a gym & a spa, plus an outdoor pool with a lively bar.

user_reviews.Name: Naomi Perier
user_reviews.Description:
My stay at New World Saigon Hotel was truly exceptional. From the moment I arrived, what stood out most was the caliber of the people who run it!

What made this experience so special was the staff, especially Phu and Tuyet that were working in the lobby area.

Their sense of hospitality was on a completely different level. They were incredibly observant, anticipating needs. Fast-witted, warm, and effortlessly professional, they really created an atmosphere that felt both welcoming and refined! It’s rare to meet people who combine efficiency with genuine care, but they really did it seamlessly.

Thank you!

user_reviews.Name: Trip.com Member
user_reviews.Description: Amazing hotel in a perfect location right in the middle of the city. The service was outstanding, and every staff member I met was friendly and professional. I had Club Lounge access – highly recommended! The lounge is excellent, with great drinks and tasty dishes in the evening. Overall, a fantastic stay – I’ll definitely come back.

user_reviews.Name: Cedric Van
user_reviews.Description: Was a part of the residence club which is an added part of our package with Luxury Escapes.  The main area is level 10 with priority check in. Fabulous dinner every night and all day excellent service. And 2.5 hours free flow drinks from 5.30 pm.  Includes a great cognac at the end of your meal. Many thanks to Van, Da, Phuc, Dang, Nghi and Dat who made our experience so much more enjoyable. We will speak highly of you when we get back to Australia

user_reviews.Name: Roger Denton
user_reviews.Description:
Rooms are small (30m2) but well equipped and in good condition.

We had a dim sum lunch at the Black Vinegar restaurant where we found the food and service to be excellent (thanks to Hai, Ngoc, Chau and Loc).

Overall, a good impression of the hotel and its location.  Would definitely stay here again.

user_reviews.Name: Yorkie22 *
user_reviews.Description: My family and I visited Ho Chi Minh and absolutely adored our stay. They have the best service in Vietnam hands down. Specifically Vân was awesome and helped us every day at the lounge, giving us recommendations for places to explore in the city, food recs, and more. Best hotel staff I’ve interacted with by far, and I’ve been to many countries.

user_reviews.Name: Ahrem Khabilihaza
user_reviews.Description: I loved every single thing about this hotel, from the staffs to the atmosphere here. Talking about the staffs, the housekeeping and restaurant staffs they are so kind and thoughtful. Especially the bellboy Natori. On the day i checkin, he now this is my first time here in Vietnam so he taught me how to use the Vietnamese money and show me best places to visit. Not only that, but also on the day i checkout, he helped me pack all my heavy luggage and put on a note with their weight on each luggage for me to know because i mentioned i have short-term memory on the day i checkin so i may ask alot. This is truely a 5 star hotel that worth you time and money, this hotel will be my go-to hotel when i'm coming back to Vietnam.

user_reviews.Name: Annie Chen
user_reviews.Description:
We had such a wonderful stay at New World Saigon! From the moment we arrived, the hospitality and service were absolutely top-notch. Every staff member was warm, attentive, and genuinely welcoming, which made our trip even more enjoyable.

A special thank you to the bellboys, Fu and Duy — they were amazing! They kindly helped us load our luggage and went above and beyond when a rude Grab driver refused to take us. They immediately assisted us in calling another Grab and made sure we were taken care of. Their professionalism and kindness truly stood out.

The breakfast spread was also fantastic with so many delicious options to choose from — fresh, plentiful, and a great way to start each day.

We felt so well taken care of throughout our stay and would absolutely stay here again. Highly recommend! 😊

user_reviews.Name: Hảo
user_reviews.Description:
I recently had the pleasure of visiting the New World Saigon Hotel for an evening event. Even though I didn’t stay the night, the few hours I spent there left a lasting impression on me. It’s one of those places that manages to feel both grand and incredibly welcoming at the same time.

What stood out to me the most was the genuine kindness of the staff. From the moment I stepped into the lobby, I was met with smiles that felt sincere, not just professional. They went out of their way to make sure I knew where I was going, and that small touch of hospitality made me feel truly seen and valued. It’s rare to find a place where the team makes you feel like an old friend within minutes.

The highlight of the night, however, was definitely the food. I’ve attended many events in Ho Chi Minh City, but the catering here was on another level. The dishes were incredibly creative—you could tell the chefs put a lot of thought into the presentation and the fusion of flavors. It wasn't just "event food"; it was a culinary experience that kept me coming back for more. Every bite was a pleasant surprise.

I am giving the New World Saigon a 4/5 rating. Since I didn't experience the rooms, I can't give a perfect score, but for an evening visit, it was nearly flawless. If you are looking for a venue that combines a sophisticated atmosphere with a warm, human touch and top-tier food, this is a wonderful choice. I left the hotel feeling happy and well-taken care of, which is the best feeling you can have after a long day.

title: Northern Hostel 2
address: 353/46 Phạm Ngũ Lão, Bến Thành, Hồ Chí Minh 70000, Vietnam
plus_code: QM8R+H7 Ben Thanh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: jasmine swann
user_reviews.Description:
Wonderful hostel and so clean! The owner very kindly opened up the doors for us at 4am with no booking after our Homestay let us down. Really kind lovely people ☺️

We did however leave a blue/green Ripcurl hoodie in the room, could someone please reply to this review with a WhatsApp number so I can message you and see if you have it? Thank you!!

user_reviews.Name: Hadi Wijaya
user_reviews.Description:
highly recommended for you guys travelling solo or maybe in small group looking for accomodation near everything than this is it. the location is very good, you can reach almost everything by walking starting from banh mi and some pho and all any kind of culinary stuff, beside of that this hostel also very near from bus terminal in Saigon so if you go to or from airport is very good location.

the owner are very kind, explain in detail and accomodate all of your needs and i think she got all the answer hahaaa...
thank you guys for excellent service...

user_reviews.Name: Ruben Overduin
user_reviews.Description: The rooms are small and cramped, there are not enough showers and toilets for the amount of quest. However it was clean and the staff is very friendly. The location is excellent

user_reviews.Name: Tobias Barth
user_reviews.Description: Very nice, very clean. Early in the morning you'll hear the women from the street market around. Super central but not noisy at night.

user_reviews.Name: Chris Dawson
user_reviews.Description:
They really went above and beyond to help me, I couldn't make it in time to check in and couldn't contact them either but they stayed up to wait for me and then when they eventually had to cancel my booking due to no show but when I arrived the following day they very kindly saved me by letting me stay 1 night in their staff room even allowing me to use it in the day to rest. They also let me check into the room early when it became available and generally we're super helpful and friendly for the 4 nights I spent there.
Genuinely thank you so much.
Also the rooms and bathrooms and common areas are very clean and the rooms have very good ac
Also free coffee

user_reviews.Name: Christina Gräfe
user_reviews.Description: This hostel is a true gem! Location is excellent - super central but tucked away from the busiest street. Rooms are well kept and very clean with comfy beds and good lockers. The best thing about this place are its hostess and her daughter. These two are the loveliest people you can imagine, treating their guests like family, incredibly helpful and welcoming. Thank you so much, I had an excellent stay!

user_reviews.Name: Aimee Egan
user_reviews.Description:
After a month of backpacking, this was by far the nicest room we stayed in. Beautifully decorated with excellent facilities, it felt like a real treat. There's a hair dryer, kettle for tea and coffee, fridge dowstairs, somehwere to hang your clothes and also a full length mirror which was a lovely touch. The location is perfect - close to all the must-see sights and great restaurants, yet surprisingly quiet despite being near the party street. We didn't hear a thing from our room.

It can be a bit tricky to find at first, but just follow the host's directions: stop at the  Seven-Eleven store - 26 Do Quang Dau Street, District 1. And then from there you look opposite to see an alley, you walk straight to the alley for 60m, then look ay the left side to see our hostel. There is a sign you can clearly see and just follow Google maps as well

The host is absolutely lovely - such a kind woman who genuinely cares about her guests. She speaks a bit of English but has no problem communicating through Google translate. She replaced our towels daily and went out of her way to make us feel at home. Don't let that one negative review from Simon fool you it's so obviously fake. Plus, there's an adorable cat that lives here too! Thank you so much for letting us stay here, I wouldn't stay anywhere else in Ho Chi Min city.

user_reviews.Name: Emma Lin
user_reviews.Description:
I recently stayed at a small hostel run by a lovely mother-daughter team, and it exceeded my expectations. The place looks exactly like the pictures—clean, with great bathrooms, plenty of hot water, and excellent air conditioning. The beds are very comfortable, ensuring a good night's sleep. Located right in the city center, it's also great value for money.

Although it's a new hostel with few reviews, people shouldn't worry about that. I’m glad I gave it a chance. The hosts are very helpful and responsive, making my stay even better. It might not be the most social hostel, but you can always meet people elsewhere and return here for a peaceful night’s sleep. Highly recommended for those who value cleanliness and comfort.

title: JW Marriott Hotel & Suites Saigon
address: Đường Hai Bà Trưng, Sài Gòn, Hồ Chí Minh 70000, Vietnam
plus_code: QPJ2+96 Sai Gon, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam
description: Polished rooms & suites in a choice hotel with an outdoor pool & a spa, plus 3 restaurants & 2 bars.

user_reviews.Name: Nutchanok Koedim
user_reviews.Description:
Hotel staffs are very well-informed about dietary restrictions. They helped preordering the alternative options based on my allergies. We felt very safe so we dined here every meal. The gluten free options are not only safe but also delicious.

Staffs are very attentive , friendly and energetic. We enjoy the executive lounge very much. The check-in process was very smooth and the concierge staff are very eager to help us with everything.

Thank you everyone for your efforts.

user_reviews.Name: Tracy
user_reviews.Description:
My partner and I stayed at the JW Marriott for our anniversary and had an amazing stay.

CHECK IN: Ambassadors have their own check-in counter at the executive lounge. We arrived in the evening and were able to enjoy the happy hour while the staff prepared our key cards and brought our luggage upstairs so it didn’t feel like a wait at all.

EXECUTIVE LOUNGE: The lounge has breakfast, afternoon tea, and happy hour. Breakfast has similar food to the downstairs buffet, which had a wider variety of dishes so we opted to eat the breakfast buffet most days but Ambassadors can dine at both on the same day. I adored the rosemary cookies from afternoon tea and my partner loved the mango tart and banh mi, which admittedly didn’t look that appealing but ended up surprisingly tasty. The food options ranged from modern take on Vietnamese to other cuisines (e.g., sushi, scones, salads). There was fresh fruit and juices for every meal as well as coffee (try the Vietnamese iced coffee!), teas, and fresh coconut. The view was lovely from the 19th floor.

STAFF & SERVICE: Everyone was incredibly kind and attentive, probably the best I’ve seen at any JW Marriott. I never felt like anyone was trying to quickly wrap up a conversation - they were great at humoring me and answering my questions, even if it was something as mundane as what the names of the tropical fruits were (one woman, and I’m sad I forgot her name, was so detailed in her explanation and excited to share more about the Vietnamese culture). The executive lounge staff was especially notable.

Rooms were cleaned multiple times a day (it felt like they cleaned up after us every time we left the room) and housekeeping was very accommodating of our variable schedule. Any issues or requests we had were addressed very quickly.

ROOM: Our suite was huge with a beautiful view overlooking the city.

FOOD: I’ve observed with most Western hotels, the breakfast buffets tend to be a few Vietnamese dishes and a lot of other cuisines (Indian, American, Korean, Japanese primarily). It was included in our stay so we ate there every morning. They had a noodle station with beef and chicken pho that we ate each time. These were surprisingly delicious. We opted for the seafood dinner buffet one night and were blown away by the variety and tastiness.

OTHER: FX exchange rate is exactly what you’d get at the bank, making it very convenient for guests. The hotel also has a sauna, swimming pool, full gym, spa, and rooftop garden / bar where you can watch the sunset.

Excited to come back after the full remodel!

user_reviews.Name: Trip.com Member
user_reviews.Description: great location.. service was good just that check in was a bit of problematic.. we booked 4 rooms there was a lot of errors.. this need to be improved.. other then that everything else were perfect.. all other staff service was excellent.. just messed up for check in..

user_reviews.Name: Jeff Mendoza
user_reviews.Description:
We had a very pleasant stay at the hotel even though there were renovations in the lobby. We were disappointed that the renovations including restaurants and bar were not completed as the Bonvoy advertised them. I was only notified ~2 weeks before our booking this was the case.

However, putting that aside, the hotel staff, facilities (pool, gym, exec lounge) and existing restaurant made more than made up for it! All staff were friendly and helpful from conceirge, pool bar (I was a regular), gym and breakfast! I enjoyed chilling by the pool after a big day of sight seeing.

Breakfast was great and the buffet dinner was probably the best buffet we had and close to the best lobster, if not the best. They also had pretty good wine selection and attentive service, which I honestly wasn’t expecting for a buffet.

Also the hotel is in a great location. HCMC is so large so you generally can’t go wrong with most hotels. You can walk or grab it to a destination easily. From a price perspective I cannot fault it - it is value for money.

We look forward to seeing it fully renovated.

Thank you for looking after us.

user_reviews.Name: Kimmy Dang
user_reviews.Description:
I had some food and drinks at the hotel’s lobby lounge, where I was kindly served a complimentary glass of about 50 ml of drinking water. Because the bread was quite dry, I politely asked for another small glass of water.
To my surprise, every staff member I spoke to gave the same response: they only provide one complimentary glass. If I wanted more, I would have to purchase a full bottle. I explained that I didn’t want to buy an entire bottle—just another small sip—but I was still refused.
I completely understand that hotels have policies designed to generate revenue by selling bottled water rather than offering unlimited free refills. However, making a guest unhappy over something so minor can do far more harm than the cost of that extra 50 ml of water.
There are two ways to handle such a request:
1.  Politely explain the policy and then, as a gesture of goodwill, offer one additional small glass. Most guests, once the policy is explained, won’t ask again. The cost is negligible, but you gain a happy, healthy, and likely returning customer.
2.  Strictly explain the policy and refuse any further water. This was the approach taken here—and as a result, I have no intention of returning, and the hotel has lost the opportunity to sell me anything else (which would have been worth far more than 50 ml of water).
I didn’t originally plan to post this publicly, but when I asked staff how I could share my feedback, they directed me to leave a Google review. This is the first time I’ve ever felt compelled to write a public review about unsatisfactory service, simply because I was given no other option.
When the property transitioned from InterContinental to JW Marriott, I assume the goal was to elevate the overall guest experience to a more luxurious level. Unfortunately, in this instance, the service standard did not feel upgraded. Staff would benefit greatly from better training—particularly in how to handle small guest requests and minor complaints with warmth, flexibility, and empathy.
Hope this review note comes across to the hotel management/ decision makers.

user_reviews.Name: Mai Nguyen
user_reviews.Description: We were warmly welcome at the front desk. Our bed was unexpectedly comfortable and bathroom was on par. Towels were soft and amenities were top notch. Staff was friendly and helpful.  By far, best hotel we stayed at in Saigon. We were welcome with a friendly gifts in the room. Water was refill on daily basis with 4 bottles. They offer us a late check-out and we gladly accepted.

user_reviews.Name: Vijay Karne
user_reviews.Description:
Title: Disappointing Experience at JW Marriott

Today I checked inn stayed at JW Marriott, expecting a luxurious experience, but unfortunately, it fell short of expectations. The experience was not what I anticipated from such a renowned brand.
Check-In Process:
The check-in process was frustratingly inefficient. There was a lack of clear communication and explanation upon arrival. The staff seemed disorganized and did not provide the warm welcome or detailed information one expects when checking in at a hotel known for its luxury and service.

Room Quality:
The quality of the rooms was another letdown. They lacked the upkeep and elegance one associates with JW Marriott. The interiors felt outdated and worn, and the amenities provided did not match the high standards the brand promises. Attention to detail seemed to be missing, leaving the room far from the luxurious experience I was hoping for.
Overall Experience:
Although the hotel carries a prestigious name, the service and room quality did not meet those expectations. I left feeling underwhelmed and disappointed. For the price paid, the experience was not justified.

The lack of luxury and attention to detail made this stay less enjoyable than it should have been. I hope this feedback is taken into consideration for improvements, as the potential for a fantastic experience is there. Unfortunately, I cannot recommend this hotel based on this experience. I hope that future guests will have a more positive experience as improvements are made.

user_reviews.Name: Abhay Kumar
user_reviews.Description:
JW Marriott Saigon Suites is undoubtedly a good property with comfortable rooms and a very good breakfast spread, which initially left us happy and satisfied. The check-in experience was also pleasant. However, unfortunately, the overall experience was seriously marred by the attitude and behavior of certain staff members.

The most disappointing incident occurred on 31st December at around 11:58 PM at the pool bar, involving Mr. Sang. He abruptly and rudely stopped us from entering the main lawn, from where my 7-year-old daughter could have witnessed the New Year fireworks. There was absolutely no empathy, no courtesy, and no willingness to understand that this once-a-year midnight moment can never be brought back—especially for a child.

His behavior was shockingly rude. The way he physically blocked my wife and daughter from stepping onto the lawn, with cold and unpleasant facial expressions, is something I will never forget. It felt as if access to an open garden was being denied purely because we had not purchased tickets for the New Year celebration. This approach was insensitive, rigid, and completely against the hospitality standards one expects from a brand like JW Marriott.

What makes this experience even more disappointing is that no one from the hotel management reached out to us afterward. In other reputed hotels across countries, when staff members make such mistakes or encounter serious guest dissatisfaction, they proactively report the incident to their General Manager, who personally reaches out to the guest to calm the situation and restore trust. Unfortunately, nothing of that sort happened here—no apology, no acknowledgment, and no attempt to understand or correct the damage caused.

Additionally, during breakfast on other days, we noticed a few staff members displaying unhappy and frustrated expressions, which further affected the overall dining experience and guest comfort.

Despite being a loyal member, such incidents force me to seriously reconsider my choices in the future. Next time I travel to another country, I would prefer exploring other brands or properties that value basic human courtesy, empathy, and guest emotions, rather than rigid rules and indifferent behavior.

Well done, Mr. Sang, for turning what should have been a magical New Year moment for a child into a bitter and unforgettable disappointment.

title: Holiday Inn & Suites Saigon Airport by IHG
address: 18E Cộng Hòa, Street, Tân Sơn Nhất, Hồ Chí Minh 700000, Vietnam
plus_code: RM24+M2 Tan Son Nhat, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam
description: Informal hotel featuring dining, an outdoor pool & a free airport shuttle, plus a gym.

owner.name: Holiday Inn & Suites Saigon Airport by IHG (Owner)

complete_address.state: Tan Son Nhat, Ho Chi Minh

user_reviews.Name: Trip.com Member
user_reviews.Description: We had a wonderful one-night stay at this apartment hotel. near the airport. The property has a beautiful pool and a beautiful delicious buffet restaurant.  was Their exceptional hospitality really made our short stay wonderful, they were incredibly accommodating—preparing a breakfast for us to take away and even providing a baby bed, even though I only mentioned it at check-in. I was so pleasantly surprised by the effort they put in, making our pre-flight stay seamless and comfortable.

user_reviews.Name: Affinity MM
user_reviews.Description: After searching for quite a while in the city centre and not finding a hotel that felt quite right, we decided to stay near the airport at this Holiday Inn, and it turned out to be a great choice. The hotel is relatively new, and both the rooms and service are up to the usual IHG hotel standard. And despite being close to the airport, it only takes about 10–15 mins by Grab to reach the city centre. The hotel also provides a shuttle bus to the airport, which is very convenient. Overall great value for money and highly recommended.

user_reviews.Name: Plan•Travel•Eat•Repeat
user_reviews.Description: This is an amazing place to stay if you need to be close to the airport. We stayed two nights. Had a huge breakfast buffet each morning. They have an airport shuttle but does have limited times so unfortunately we could not use it either time for the airport. Easy to use Grab to drop off and pick up so no big deal. The staff are super friendly. Many amenities offered it the bathroom and also had coffee and machine in room. We paid extra for the Seafood buffet one of our nights and there was a lot of variety.

user_reviews.Name: Henry Le
user_reviews.Description:
Phenomenal Stay in Saigon!
I cannot recommend this hotel enough to anyone visiting Saigon! From start to finish, the experience was seamless and welcoming.
A special thank you to Tri and An for their incredible hospitality. They were both so helpful, friendly, and truly embodied professionalism in everything they did. They went above and beyond to ensure my stay was perfect.
A few highlights:
• The Service: Personalized, attentive, and warm.
• The Breakfast: Absolutely lovely! There were so many choices, and everything was delicious.
• The Vibe: Perfect for tourists looking for a high-quality experience.
I will definitely be back on my next trip to the city. Thank you for the 5-star experience!

user_reviews.Name: Yong Ting Lim
user_reviews.Description:
I stayed for three nights on a business trip and overall had a very pleasant experience. The hotel’s close proximity to SGN Airport is a major advantage, saving time and avoiding the heavy traffic into the city center where most other hotels are located.

As an IHG Diamond Elite member, I was upgraded to a one-bedroom suite with a pool view, which made for a quiet and comfortable stay compared to the road-facing rooms. The gym was well-equipped with both weights and cardio machines, perfect for maintaining a workout routine. Staff were consistently pleasant and helpful throughout my stay. I also appreciated the convenience of having a drinking water dispenser available in the corridor.

user_reviews.Name: Craig Brotherston
user_reviews.Description: Great place to stay close to the airport with the hotel shuttle bus only taking about 10 minutes to get there. The rooms are very well presented very clean with nothing out of place. Huge swimming pool with plenty of places to sit, relax and enjoy the bar service. The food was great with morning buffet breakfast with basically everything you ask for. The night we had dinner there it was a Seafood Buffet which was just as amazing as the breakfast with more than enough to pick from.  Staff were great and every ever so friendly and helpful with everything you asked of them. Would highly recommend this place to anyone.

user_reviews.Name: Danny
user_reviews.Description:
Very happy with my stay here, amazing staff and facilities. Very close to Tah Son Nhat International Airport terminal 3 and 5k from district 1. My grab rides averaging $3 to $4, so I'm not bothered about staying in this area. pool is huge and restaurant buffet is good except the lobster in the seafood buffet is tiny and had a hard time getting it out of the shell. Breakfast buffet also good. Gym facility is very good and Republic Plaza at lobby level has a 7/11, Phuoc Long coffee and Texas BBQ. Super convenient. Port cochere and ground level lobby is under renovation and scheduled to last a while longer and made ingress and egress cumbersome. Lotte Mart just down the street was also great 😃
Thanks so much.

user_reviews.Name: Jules Abella
user_reviews.Description: WE LOVE VIETNAM! 💖 This is our second time staying at this hotel, and they never disappoint us. The staff are very friendly and accommodating. The breakfast buffet is good with a wide variety of choices. The room is clean, spacious, and comfortable. Overall, a great stay and we would definitely come back.

title: Saigon Hub Hostel
address: 283/2C Phạm Ngũ Lão, Phường Phạm Ngũ Lão, Bến Thành, Hồ Chí Minh 71012, Vietnam
plus_code: QM8R+XF Ben Thanh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

complete_address.borough: Phường Phạm Ngũ Lão, Ben Thanh

user_reviews.Name: Trip.com Member
user_reviews.Description: This hotel was an ideal base for my travels. Its central location meant I could walk everywhere, and the room was incredibly clean and cozy. The staff were fantastic—always ready to help and gave me some brilliant tips for local restaurants. Highly recommend it for first-time visitors!

user_reviews.Name: Shelby Mehs
user_reviews.Description:
I had a truly terrible experience staying at this hostel last night. From the moment I arrived, basic necessities were missing. I was never given towels or toilet paper, even after asking for them.

There wasn’t even a proper blanket provided. Instead, we were handed a single duvet cover for a twin bed to share between two people and were told it was a “summer blanket.” That’s completely unacceptable.

On top of that, there was a horrible sewage smell throughout the place that made it very uncomfortable to stay. While they do have air conditioning, it hardly makes up for the barely running water and the lack of basic hygiene supplies.

Overall, this was a very disappointing and uncomfortable stay. I would not recommend this hostel to anyone expecting even the most basic level of comfort or service.

user_reviews.Name: ONE 2 DIVE
user_reviews.Description: I deeply regret not checking the reviews before booking. This place is a breeding ground for pests—a literal hellish for bed bugs and a farm for mosquitoes. To make matters worse, the living conditions were unacceptable: the bathroom was filthy, moldy, and completely lacked ventilation. Even the air conditioning failed to cool the room, making the stay unbearable. Avoid this hostel at all costs unless you want to deal with an infestation and a broken, dirty room.

user_reviews.Name: Joseph Han
user_reviews.Description: Not fit for human habitation. The mattress was clearly unwashed, which caused a skin allergy after lying on it. There was no hairdryer. The room floors were full of dirt and fine sand. The air quality was terrible with a strange smell. The TV was completely covered in sand—this is the most appalling TV I have ever encountered in my life.

user_reviews.Name: T
user_reviews.Description: no hot water in the shower! no keys, so everyone could literally walk in to your bed. not really safe lockers. rooms only have one bathroom, also there is no real bar as seen on pictures, maybe just openend on special occasions or for the online pictures. The AC was cold, but only a very thin blanket, freezing all night. friendly but very unorganised team, the pictures look much better than real. no soul or concept in details, very improvised hostel. i liked the low price though. And the location is close to Bui Vien Street. Its ok but really basic, so i did not extend my stay and found something better instead for the same price

user_reviews.Name: Joseph Wang
user_reviews.Description:
If you're seeking to be at the heart of the city, this location fits the bill. Our stay was during Halloween and the location of this hostel was just around the corner from all the excitement and festivities. The location was also close to open markets where one can purchase produce and prepared meals. Be prepared for torrential rains during this time of year.
However, it's not suitable if you're looking for accommodations rated 3 stars or higher. We chose this place hoping for a hostel-like experience, and that's what we encountered.
Upon check-in, we were switched from our original room to another one where the hot water heater was malfunctioning. The bathroom mirror was streaked, and we had to request toilet paper since none was provided. The room hadn’t been cleaned, and we also had to ask for towels, which unfortunately smelled of sweat.
On a positive note, the bed was comfortable, and there was a refrigerator in the room. Given the low price, we would consider returning, as our expectations of the hostel were quite modest but our experience of the food, people and culture was very good.

user_reviews.Name: Julie Whiteside
user_reviews.Description:
DO NOT STAY HERE!

Bed bugs! Bricked-up windows! Rats????? Smoking on the top floor of a building with no fire exits! Dusty, dingy and disgusting. Do not stay here!

As soon as we arrived we realised it was nothing like the photos. It was late, just past midnight. The guy was helpful and even carried my bag, but I should have known something was wrong when he said we would be moving to a different room in the morning.

Went to take a shower and the hot water heater was not connected to the shower. This was the least of my worries because it was so humid outside of the sleeping area the cold water was fine.

It also stank of stale smoke and people were clearly smoking in the common area of our 5th floor room. This was made even more alarming by the bricked up windows on every floor.

Got into bed and realised the blanket was dusty. The walls of the pod were dirty and it looked like something had leaked from the top bunk.

My friend's bedding was stained and looked unclean. She found a scab on the underside of her pillow.

Exhausted from a long day of travel, decided I could live with these things. Then heard a suspicious squeaking sound that was very rodent-like. It only gets better because I realised I was not alone in my bed and I was joined by bed bugs.

We are now panicking that all of our belongings, that we had to keep on the beds because the locker area was so untidy, are contaminated. Was the dust actually bug poo… probably. Was the drip down the wall human excrement…. Likely. We had best not question the source of the white stuff on the blanket too closely.

Decided that was enough and an hour after checking in we decided to leave. It was 1.30 am and it took us hours to find somewhere to stay for the night.

I wish I had taken more photos but I was in a rush to escape. When I showed the receptionist he did give a full refund, but honestly I would have paid to get out.

user_reviews.Name: Mayinn Yoyi
user_reviews.Description:
Not the worse hostel experience I’ve ever had, unfortunately.
To be fair, the owner was very nice, accommodating, and responsive, which I truly appreciated. However, the photos online were not realistic compared to what we actually saw on site.
It’s cheap and basic, which is fine, but we just didn’t feel comfortable, especially since we were traveling with senior citizens who had difficulty going up and down the stairs.
We decided not to complete our stay and flew out the very next morning to our next location earlier than expected. I think this hostel options is best for friends or backpackers, we did not feel safe at all leaving out stuff, we had a hard time locking the door.

title: Redhome Dorm
address: 74/1 Đường số 6, khu phố 3, Linh Xuân, Hồ Chí Minh 700000, Vietnam
plus_code: VQGC+W6 Linh Xuan, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Nguyễn Khánh Linh
user_reviews.Description:
Just moved in there.

The first good thing is, there's general kitchen area. Which is good, since cooking can be smelly.

Location still depends on where you work, but if you work near here, there won't be any problems.

user_reviews.Name: HIỀN DROPPII
user_reviews.Description: Mình ở đây vui lắm , các bạn trẻ đều dễ thương, cứ như là 1 gia đình vậy

user_reviews.Name: A T (TA)
user_reviews.Description:
Máy lạnh, điện nước đều miễn phí nhen. Vô được luôn nệm với ly nước mới.
Không gian giường và chỗ sinh hoạt được chủ nhà thiết kế rất hợp lý, không cảm thấy bí bách.
Còn có thêm sảnh sinh hoạt chung và chỗ nấu nướng 🥰 nch là 10/10 nha
Còn nhìu cái tui hơi bị mê mà chưa kể đâu!

user_reviews.Name: Trần Thị Anh Thư
user_reviews.Description: Mô hình quá tuyệt vời, ở rất vui, thoải mái.

user_reviews.Name: Trúc Mai
user_reviews.Description: Ở đã và mê không muốn chuyển chỗ khác

user_reviews.Name: Haiauvsjs Huzuwghs
user_reviews.Description: Thoải mái, tiện nghi lắm

title: Base Backpackers
address: Đề Thám, Cầu Ông Lãnh, Hồ Chí Minh, Vietnam
plus_code: QM8V+2W Cau Ong Lanh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

complete_address.state: Cau Ong Lanh, Ho Chi Minh

user_reviews.Name: Max Noyce
user_reviews.Description:
Amazing stay at Base Backpackers Hostel! The rooms were spotlessly clean and very comfortable, which made a huge difference after long travel days. The staff were incredibly friendly, welcoming, and always happy to help with recommendations or bookings — they really make you feel at home. Special shout out to Alix, Kan, Ken and Phoebe who are legends!

The tours and activities were fantastic, well-organized, and a great way to meet other travelers while exploring the area. The overall vibe of the hostel is social, fun, and relaxed, but still perfect for getting a good night’s sleep.

I’d 100% recommend Base Backpackers to anyone looking for a clean, friendly, and fun place to stay. I’d definitely come back!

user_reviews.Name: Tanner Payne
user_reviews.Description:
I had a great experience at Base Backpackers, I read some of the lower scored reviews and didn't experience anything that was mentioned.

AC in the rooms was brisk (feels nice after getting in from a 35 Celcius day). Wifi was fast (this was a pleasant surprise).

Would stay here again, felt like a good mix of party/social and chill.

user_reviews.Name: Jordan
user_reviews.Description: Good value and decent rooms. You also get a full and reasonably sized bathroom to yourself including the shower which is nice for a hostel. There are plenty of bathrooms too. Free beers in the evening is a nice touch also. Laundry is good here too

user_reviews.Name: Mitchell Young
user_reviews.Description: The best hostel I have ever stayed at with the most warm and welcoming staff. Alix, Ken and Phoebe were the most warm and welcoming staff and it feel like a home not a hostel. I would strongly recommend it to anymore looking for a place to stay in Ho Chi Minh

user_reviews.Name: Vieri S.
user_reviews.Description:
Don't recommend booking buses with them: payed 770k for a sleeper bus to Phnom Penh, yet got shuttled to a normal one!!

The bus smells, is really dark and the window is covered with a foil that does not allow to enjoy the views. I was the only foreigner, which also seemed a bit suspicious.

The rooms itself were fine. I joined the pub crawl and noticed that after a couple of affordable places where they were serving free shots, they eventually took the crowd to overpriced places (85k for a tiger beer in a can??)

user_reviews.Name: Etienne Hayward
user_reviews.Description: Overall it is decent, the rooms are good, bathrooms are good. Was quite loud however through the halls, could hear people walking up and down the stairs. Not much of a social hostel, but good location.

user_reviews.Name: alaska webber
user_reviews.Description: perfect location! clean dorms and bathrooms! staff are so lovely and go beyond to help with anything! was very easy to book the tunnel tour through the hostel and at a good price!! bonus that they have pub craws every Tuesday thursdays and saturdays that are well organised

user_reviews.Name: Ilona Lahti
user_reviews.Description: Good place to connect with other travellers. Clean rooms and nice bathroom. Service could be better; Tried to book trip to other town and the staff gave me wrong information.

title: Saigon Authentic Hostel & Tours
address: 81 Số 45, Phường 9, Khánh Hội, Hồ Chí Minh 70000, Vietnam
plus_code: QM5X+JR Khanh Hoi, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Josh Tierney
user_reviews.Description:
Had a great experience here, decent social atmosphere, clean and comfortable rooms and also many different options for bookable tours. The local walking food tour is a must and the host Duy was a great guy and made sure we tried out loads of interesting dishes.

Rooftop pool table and seating area is great and gives you a great view of central HCM.

user_reviews.Name: 152martes
user_reviews.Description: We had a really good stay at Saigon Authentic Hostel & Tours. The hostel is family-owned and they are very kind & helpful. I really recommend their streetfood tour, which is a fantastic way to explore the local neighborhood and try great foods (best Bánh Mí!). The streetfood tour ended at a rooftop bar with a must-see view of the city.

user_reviews.Name: pfui 1
user_reviews.Description:
Very clean hostel suitable for travellers on a budget. The staff are very friendly and make sure you‘re comfortable during your stay.
We booked the private five person tour to Cu Chi Tunnels and our guide did a great job at explaining the history behind them while making sure we‘re avoiding the larger crowds. Can only recommend!

user_reviews.Name: TJS
user_reviews.Description: Genuinely mean it. In terms of value for money I feel I got the best possible experience here. Didn’t even join the tours but a hostel under $10 with a proper gym with bench press, friendly + informative staff, clean (most important for me) and I got real lucky because the people in my room were respectful and nice. On top of that a free breakfast with choice of continental is incredible icing on the cake. Really can’t think of a better experience elsewhere. Five stars!!!

user_reviews.Name: Lotti Küh
user_reviews.Description: Awesome staff and hostel! We did the cooking class too! Everything was tasty and we had such a fun time. The staff all speak English very well and the free walking tour was just very insightful.

user_reviews.Name: Charlene May Mangubat
user_reviews.Description:
I stayed at this hostel for four days and had a wonderful experience. The room was clean and well-maintained, and the staff truly made me feel well taken care of — they were always polite and quick to help with any concerns. The breakfast included in the stay was homemade and absolutely delicious, which made mornings even better.

Highly recommended for anyone looking for a comfortable and welcoming place to stay!

user_reviews.Name: Aeron Alvarado
user_reviews.Description: Excellent hostel with activities such as Cu Chi tunnels, Mekong delta tours, locally organised social activities such as walking food tours around district 4, home cooking classes with Duy and staff members. Good local knowledge and suggestions of things to do locally and in HCMC. Has a nice friendly atmosphere day and night. Would definitely recommend!

user_reviews.Name: Jalmari Skyttä
user_reviews.Description: I stayed for 3 nights. The room temperature was just right—not too cold or too hot—perfect for sleeping. There are many restaurants nearby, which is very convenient. At the reception, they also give you a list of points of interest and even offer tours at quite affordable prices. The breakfast and coffee were delicious; I had eggs, bread, and vegetables every morning. I highly recommend this place.

title: Aloha Saigon Hostel
address: 40/12 Trần Quang Diệu, Phường 14, Nhiêu Lộc, Hồ Chí Minh, Vietnam
plus_code: QMQH+9F Nhieu Loc, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Ariana Falkner
user_reviews.Description: This is THE BEST hostel I stayed at in Vietnam! The rooms are spacious and comfortable and so clean. There is a free water station with hot and cold water and free breakfast and coffee in the morning. I stayed at this hostel in November and it was so nice that I recommended my friend to stay here and we returned in January and stayed for almost a month. The staff here are exceptional. The owner, Ha, is so welcoming and kind, and Hien, An, and Gem are soooo nice, helpful, and responsive. They all did everything they could to ensure I had a wonderful stay and they really made my time in HCMC so nice. The hostel is in a great location. It’s in a local neighborhood, so you get a feeling for how the locals live, but it’s not too far from D1 - you can walk or easily take a Grab. I love this hostel and recommend it to everyone!!! You won’t regret staying here!

user_reviews.Name: Zivel Duenas
user_reviews.Description:
Oh where oh where do I start with this little place that feels like home away from home.

First the outside, it's on a street that doesn't get much traffic so you have peace and quiet in a crazy loud city.

Once you're inside, you're greeted with a clean and well setup lobby that offers a freshly made breakfast in the mornings for freeeeee. (Coffee and tea was also offered, hooray!)

In the sleeping rooms, beds are super comfortable with AC.

Listen, stay here if you want to recharge your battery so that you can play all day and night in the city. Trust me! 😎

Also, Hien and the others that work here are sweethearts with big smiles. It's so nice to be in a place that makes you feel warm and fuzzy inside ❤️

user_reviews.Name: Antony Le
user_reviews.Description:
Super comfy stay. Friendly and knowledgeable host. I loved how signs were everywhere, so i didn’t feel lost (remember to take off your shoes!). Very clean and loved the firm and wide mattress.

Got everything I needed. A shower with a dry towel at no extra charge, a night’s rest and fruits from 7-eleven down the street!

Also, Mr Ha what scent is the room? So fresh!

Thanks!

user_reviews.Name: Trip.com Member
user_reviews.Description: Great hostel amazing owners I love staying here

user_reviews.Name: Amaru
user_reviews.Description:
Truly a 5 star 🌟 hostel in Saigon, Vietnam.
Rooms are cleaned daily, location is top tier and hostel staff are the friendliest. Shout out to Trihn & Hien for making me feel comfortable during my stay!

Bonus - having a fresh juice/café just next door is great. :)

user_reviews.Name: Łukasz Tyszkiewicz
user_reviews.Description:
Very good hostel, comfortable wide beds to put some belongings on, bathroom with warm shower, great location outside of touristy area.
Good breakfasts and helpful crew, definitely recommend!
Could improve bathroom as it is a bit slippery and the bed curtains could be more covering as they are partially transparent

user_reviews.Name: Maya Maesaroh
user_reviews.Description: My favorite hostel in Vietnam, I cudnt find many options of hostel which provide female dormitory in vietnam, but they provide that room for female. The place was really clean and homie, I stayed there for so many times and made alot of friends because the social environment is pretty good. Sometimes they have a english club event to help local community to improve their english but meeting and interacting with foreigners who stay there. Very innovative, helpful and good contribution for the community. Sadly I dont have a pic of the room

user_reviews.Name: Reka C
user_reviews.Description:
The person who called me at 9:30 pm the night before I was meant to check in was extremely rude. I’ve stayed in many places over the years and have never experienced anything like this before. I was essentially accused of being “too flexible” with my travel plans because I had changed my dates twice, both times well outside the 24-hour notice period, fully within their policy.

I was told I had to pay in advance because they didn’t trust that I would actually show up, despite the fact that I had already confirmed my arrival time. The staff member insisted that every hostel and hotel requires upfront payment, and claimed that because it was high season, they needed to make sure I wouldn’t change my plans again within the next 12 hours.

I explained that I had every intention of arriving as planned and had already communicated my date changes several days in advance via email and WhatsApp. That still wasn’t enough. This person spoke to me as if I had no idea what I was doing and repeatedly told me I would probably change my plans again. Their statements about “all hostels/hotels requiring upfront payment” were not only condescending but also completely contradictory to their own policy.

When I asked to speak with the owner or manager to resolve the issue, I was told I couldn’t do that. At that point, I decided to cancel the booking entirely. The interaction was uncalled for, unprofessional, and left me feeling extremely uncomfortable before even arriving.

title: Bandlive Backpacker Hostel
address: Vietnam, Hồ Chí Minh, Cầu Ông Lãnh, Nguyễn Trãi, Bandlive Backpacker Hostel邮政编码: 700000
plus_code: QM7Q+V4 Cau Ong Lanh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

complete_address.state: Cau Ong Lanh, Ho Chi Minh

user_reviews.Name: T
user_reviews.Description: i never disagreed so much with all the good ratings and really wonder about them. first of all there is no well managed reception. just an improvised table with almost never anyone behind it. you are constantly confused who might be working there. the beds are making sounds with every movement, and the lockers are so small that you cant even fit a small bag inside it. which means that the room is also messy, because everyone is leaving their belongings on the floor. the curtains are not really good, and in general this place is of older age already. at the reception table you will find a whatsapp number to call for help, but noone reacts to texts or calls at all. they dont provide any form of entry key or code, so literally everyone could walk straight up to your bed. the location is also not really nice. to reach the hostel in that small street you first have to walk along a very loud road. its not calm or relaxing at all. i stayed in cheaper but better places in HCM.

user_reviews.Name: Ollie S
user_reviews.Description: really cheap so that's good but not the best stay for me. men constantly smoking in the bathroom and on the stairs next to the room so it stunk everywhere. really loud. handy location. not very clean and only one bathroom for a floor of 16 people so good luck having a shower 😂

user_reviews.Name: Mohan priya
user_reviews.Description: No towel given, bed very poor, blanket  is not good, so much of mosquitoes, only one restroom for 2 floors. 4th floor doesn't have restroom. No response from receptionist and through call,mail or message. Sockets are not working its died. Used to charge my gadgets using kitchen socket. No free water given. Disappointed for a single night. They are only focusing on money. Very noisy even mid night

user_reviews.Name: Cham Novie
user_reviews.Description: Great accommodation and highly recommended…this hostel offers clean, comfortable rooms with comfy beds and fresh linens, welcoming common areas like a cozy lounge and well-equipped kitchen, and a friendly, responsive staff that’s full of handy local tips. The location is prime for easy access to sights and transit, providing excellent value with reliable Wi‑Fi, secure bookings, and extras like travel info. Overall, it’s a reliable, sociable choice I’d happily stay at again. 😊🥳

user_reviews.Name: Ugne Dunčytė
user_reviews.Description: We booked an accommodation long time ago on booking.com and arrived yesterday night. Unfortunately us and some more people were told that there is no more rooms available for us even if the booking let us book. So we ended up looking for another place in the middle of the nights + the booking sent a notification that we never showed up and that we will be charged. So we are trying to fix it with the customer service support now… Unbelievable.

user_reviews.Name: Kumar
user_reviews.Description:
This is my first solo travel and I found this hostel very affordable and friendly. The host has been accomodating for small requests and bathrooms are very clean.

Only thing is hostel lacks elevator but everything is great. Location of hostel is awesome. Everything I wanted was always close by somehow.

user_reviews.Name: Alex Davis
user_reviews.Description:
Well located, friendly service, comfortable beds, great craic in common area.

Great travellers visit here, truly makes you feel like you're at home whilst on the road.

user_reviews.Name: W. B
user_reviews.Description: Not my first time staying at a hostel and in a dorm, but I would say this was one of the worst I’ve ever been to. If you care about basic hygiene, this place is not for you. My room (201) was a bit smelly, the bed sheet and blanket were not really clean, and the bathroom was the same. The worst part was the receptionist, they were not around until I called them and kept me waiting more than 15 minutes for both check-in and check-out. In the lobby, people were smoking, which made the environment unpleasant. I’m sorry, but as a frequent traveler, this review is based on my personal experience.

title: LaCa Hostel
address: 84/34 Bùi Viện, Phường Phạm Ngũ Lão, Bến Thành, Hồ Chí Minh, Vietnam
plus_code: QM8V+X6 Ben Thanh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

complete_address.borough: Phường Phạm Ngũ Lão, Ben Thanh

user_reviews.Name: Rod Buja
user_reviews.Description: In the heart of Bùi Viện walking street and still a very quite place from all party noises, the owner is an amazing person very helpful and friendly, definitely a good place to stay ✔️

user_reviews.Name: Cassandra Jen Florentino
user_reviews.Description:
Surprisingly good, clean, and well maintained. It's airconditioned, has hot shower and toiletries, and bunk beds have fair enough space. Location is nice if you like to party at night because it is located in the Bar Street.

Overall the hostel itself is good. The problem is it's all mixed gender in a room, and it's located inside a maze/alley that stinks.. so it's not really for people who are sensitive with smell and polution.

user_reviews.Name: Caland
user_reviews.Description:
The owner is very unfriendly.
I stayed at this hostel for 3 days, and apparently the ownership changed last year.
I can’t understand the good reviews and didn’t feel comfortable at all.
It was very dirty. There were bed bugs in two of the beds, and the owner didn’t care.
The area feels very unsafe — not recommended for women.

user_reviews.Name: Ngarui Manukau
user_reviews.Description:
The place itself is not bad value for money. It is located close to the walking street and near the center of the city. It's narrow and cramped in general, with the bathrooms quite difficult to navigate. The bed cubicles have a fair amount of room and good curtains. For what you pay I would say it's reasonable.

The experience was alright until the last day when someone stole my cash and another guys wallet from the dorm. When I told the owner about my cash, she had no reaction and was not helpful at all. When the guy asked about the cameras, she said they were off. The building seems quite secure as there's a code lock on the front door you can access with pin or card. Unfortunately for the guy and I, we both accidentally left our stuff outside the locker: mine in my bag on the top bunk and his by his stuff on the bottom. The window of time this could've happened was quite small as the guy was out only a few hours without his wallet and I was out a bit longer. When I left, the owner was downstairs and she was still there when I came back. Given the lock on the front door, it seems like there would only be the guests or the owner and employees that would have access to the money and wallet. I feel like barely any effort by the owner went into assisting in this situation. Getting money stolen is annoying, but a whole wallet is devastating and it happened on their first day in the country. It's not a big hostel either and since it was during the day, the amount of people around was pretty minimal and the owner was on the premises when this happened. The cameras being off makes the situation very suspicious.

user_reviews.Name: Ryan
user_reviews.Description: Located in a maze of small streets, this hostel can be difficult to find but once you get familiar with the street map it's fun to traverse the winding streets to get there. It is pretty tight quarters, there is not a ton of room but the hostel has done a good job organizing the space. The rooftop patio is very lovely. The host was so amazing and the highlight for me. She has a beautifully natural charm to her that makes you feel instantly welcomed.

user_reviews.Name: ana redondo
user_reviews.Description: This place is a must if you are in Saigon.  The owner is the most helpful person ever (tips for change money, buy cigarettes, laundry..), honest, flexible and kindly. The location is in full center without any noise and the accommodation is clean and modern. She even take care of my stuff at the meantime I was in Mekong and later leave it on my room. Just incredible level of care and detail. Don’t doubt to book!

user_reviews.Name: Daan Faverey
user_reviews.Description: Hostel is on the small side, but that doesn’t take away the social aspects this place offers. Owner is the best and is very attentive, helps out with what ever question you may have. Besides she really cares for all of her customers and invites them to do whatever! Would 100% recommend, if i may come back to Saigon, this is where i’m spending my nights!

user_reviews.Name: zoi zoi
user_reviews.Description: The best hostel in HCM! Nice food made with love, nice coffee and the most important good people with a lot of energy ready to help you and make you feel so comfortable as in your hometown! I definitely recommend this place , especially for backpackers !

title: Nhà Nghỉ An An - Biên Hòa
address: Nhà Nghỉ An An, 1/5 Hẻm Số 1070 Đ. Nguyễn Ái Quốc, khu phố 8, Tân Triều, Đồng Nai 76117, Vietnam
plus_code: XV93+95 Tan Trieu, Dong Nai, Vietnam

owner.name: Nhà Nghỉ An An - Biên Hòa (Owner)

complete_address.street: Nhà Nghỉ An An, 1/5 Hẻm Số 1070 Đ. Nguyễn Ái Quốc

user_reviews.Name: Freebird Cánh chim tự do
user_reviews.Description: Khách sạn sạch sẽ, phục vụ ân cần vui vẻ và chu đáo giá cả hợp lý bình dân,  có dịp đi công tác biên hoà sẽ ghé ủng hộ tiếp

user_reviews.Name: Anh Văn
user_reviews.Description: Khách sạn rất thoáng và sạch sẽ nhé mọi người, nhất là giá rẻ và đưocj miễn phí kẹo và đồ uống, cho thấy chủ nhà rất ân cần với khách

user_reviews.Name: Trang Trương
user_reviews.Description: Nhà nghỉ sạch sẽ, giá rẻ so với khu vực, bãi đậu xe rộng rồi, chủ nhà nhiệt tình

user_reviews.Name: Jane Pham
user_reviews.Description: Trải nghiệm tệ, sáng sớm đang ngủ, chưa tới 9h sáng, cứ bị gõ cửa 2, 3 lần. Để chủ cho thợ vào sơn phòng, đang ngủ kêu qua phòng khác ngủ, ủa ủa, làm ăn kiểu j dị. Mà tiền phòng thì đã trả trước tới 12h trưa. Chứng tỏ là chủ khách sạn ko có tôn trọng khách đâu. Mọi người nên cân nhắc!

user_reviews.Name: Thịnh Anh
user_reviews.Description: Giá tốt phòng sạch sẽ. Sẽ ghé lại lần sau

user_reviews.Name: Phương Sầm
user_reviews.Description: Nhà nghỉ sạch sẽ, chăn ga trắng trẻo. Thơm tho. Rất ưng ý. Lần sau đi công tác sẽ ghé tiếp

user_reviews.Name: Lợi Trương
user_reviews.Description:
Đoàn mình đi thi đấu ở quảng trường tỉnh, đi lại khá thuận tiện, phòng ốc sạch và giá rẻ nhất khu vực mình kiếm, còn được miễn phí nước và kẹo....
Rất biết ơn nhà nghỉ đã phục vụ nhiệt tình

user_reviews.Name: Duytan Le
user_reviews.Description: Sạch sẽ, chủ nhà thân thiện. Nhiệt tình . giá rẻ khu vực này nhé cả nhà. Nên ghé

title: Dream Box Hostel Vietnam
address: 27-29-31, Trưng Nhị, Street, Vũng Tàu, Hồ Chí Minh 790000, Vietnam
plus_code: 83XG+82 Vung Tau, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Kathy Chin
user_reviews.Description:
I do not recommend this hostel.
Several listings on Agoda are inaccurate or misleading. “24-hour front desk” is not true. AC is not included in the base price. Amenities listed such as beach, free parking, fitness centre, bar, golf course, and water park are not on-site or affiliated with the hostel — they are nearby attractions.
I booked and paid through Agoda and requested early check-in (10–11am). There was no response through Agoda, Zalo, or email. When I arrived at 10am, no one was at reception despite the 24-hour listing. I waited 30 minutes and returned later. Check-in was disorganized and unclear. It was difficult to identify staff or where to park my bicycle.
I was charged an additional 50k for AC and 30k for one hour early check-in. This made the total 80k higher than the advertised price. AC was ineffective in the enclosed bunk design.
Showers were dirty, several shower fixtures were broken, water pressure was poor, and some stalls had no hot water. Toilets upstairs smelled strongly; the downstairs toilet was dirty. Dorm bunks are beside common areas and not in a separate room. During my stay, someone smoked in the dorm area.
Overall, the experience was disorganized and more expensive than advertised.

user_reviews.Name: DAN
user_reviews.Description: Very noisy place! Can hear streets motorbike noise. Beds are fine. Manager is good 👍.

user_reviews.Name: twobigfeets
user_reviews.Description: This hostel is very clean. The showers are great and there are facilities to wash and dry your clothes. I noticed a toad (frog) jumping around the sleeping area, which wasn't a major concern. Wifi is good. Walking distance to ferry terminal. The only thing that lets this hostel down is the beds are so hard. These are hardest beds I have come across in Vietnam.  I liked the bed set up up with power points and blind. The portable air conditioner was an interesting idea.

user_reviews.Name: R2468RDdavidh
user_reviews.Description: New hostel close to front beach. Very clean, staff are exceptional and Sarah and her mum went out of their way to make sure our stay was the best it could be. On the morning we left they even arranged to have breakfast delivered for our early start. Highly recommended!

user_reviews.Name: Thang Tran
user_reviews.Description: Sleeping box is nice, cheap price and near some amazing food street. In the ground floor,  we have drinks at night. The place is also near the front beach of Vung Tau city.  The owner of the hostel is friendly. She always asks the guests where you intend to you and show you the directions. Love the way she manages her hostel.

user_reviews.Name: Grant
user_reviews.Description: If you plan to sleep don't stay here.  Bright and early the local kids and parents that stay here like get up before 6am and have loud conversations while their kids run around screaming.  Two days in a row.  Absolutely zero respect for other people around them.  Bed was comfortable, facilities were nice.... I will never stay here again though.  The kids were running around screaming past midnight and up before 6 doing the same.

user_reviews.Name: Patricia L. Huyler
user_reviews.Description:
It is very clean but felt very corporate to me with the open bays like prisons. Toilet paper out in the bathroom which was a little hike even for private rooms that had Hello Kitty themes. No courtyard. No community spaces. No hostel like things such as plants growing in plastic containers or swings from half pallets or seats from old tires and string. No writing on the wall from clients. Shades for dorm beds nice for privacy seekers nice but the one person occupying didn't use them. I don't know
Maybe we need each other and this hostel owner doesn't recognize this fact.

user_reviews.Name: Người Bò
user_reviews.Description:
The only good point is location.
The beds are on three hall way, it’s super noisy. Rest room are not clean.
It was super uncomfortable when starting here.

title: Saigon Chill Hostel
address: 28/10 Bùi Viện, Phường Phạm Ngũ Lão, Bến Thành, Hồ Chí Minh 700000, Vietnam
plus_code: QM9V+7R Ben Thanh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

complete_address.borough: Phường Phạm Ngũ Lão, Ben Thanh

user_reviews.Name: Daniel Sutter
user_reviews.Description:
I had an absolutely wonderful stay at Saigon Chill Hostel! The location right on Bùi Viện in District 1 couldn’t be better — just steps away from fantastic restaurants, cafes, and local attractions, making it so easy to explore Ho Chi Minh City on foot. My little bunk bed capsule was very comfortable and the mattress was very soft.
The staff were incredibly friendly, helpful, and went above and beyond to make my stay memorable — they offered great tips on places to eat and things to do, and always greeted me with a smile. The rooms were clean, comfortable, and well-appointed, and the overall vibe of the hostel was welcoming and social without being overwhelming. I especially loved the free breakfast and relaxed common areas where it was easy to meet fellow travelers. Whether you’re a solo adventurer or traveling with friends, this place feels like a home away from home. Highly recommended!

user_reviews.Name: X5938NWamym
user_reviews.Description: Can’t fault this hostel - a really great stay for the price. We stayed in a private en suite room which was spotless, with a fan and decent air conditioning. We had no window in our room but that didn’t affect our stay at all. Breakfast is great - try the noodles with fried eggs! - as was the coffee served with it. Staff were friendly and helpful too. A great location within walking distance of all the main spots in downtown Ho Chi Minh City.

user_reviews.Name: Aldrin Martins
user_reviews.Description: One of the amazing hostels in Ho Chi Minh. They give you free beers every day. The breakfast is amazing. It comes along with a drink. The beds were good. The washrooms were good. It was very clean. It’s amazing value for money, especially if you have beer in the evening and you have breakfast in the morning. You will not regret staying here, walking distance to most of the tourist spots in the city, Money Exchange, just opposite and loads of restaurants around.

user_reviews.Name: Conor Prenderville
user_reviews.Description: This hostel is really great. We booked a double ensuite, which was a little dark but otherwise perfect. It's great value for money, super central, yet slightly removed from the main drag. The free daily beers mean that the downstairs bar is super social and easy to meet people. Win, Kitty and the crew are so friendly, smiley and helpful and the breakfast is great. 10/10.

user_reviews.Name: Nathan
user_reviews.Description: Nini and Win really made my stay wonderful. They are so helpful and make a lot of effort to really listen and provide amazing suggestions on things to do, places to eat and insight into the Vietnamese life. They work hard to make the hostel feel like a home and "free beer time" something to come back to the hostel for. The location is great, steps away from the action without any of the the noise. I've got to come back thru HCMC and I'm actually looking forward to getting one more stay even if it's just for a few hours.

user_reviews.Name: Andrea Giunta
user_reviews.Description:
Had an awesome stay at Saigon Chill Hostel! Super friendly staff that makes you feel at home right away. Perfect spot in District 1, but still quiet enough to relax after exploring.

Dorms are clean, comfy, and nicely cooled. Showers too, everything well kept.

All in all: way better than expected, definitely recommend if you want a cozy, clean place in Saigon!

user_reviews.Name: Sam thurai
user_reviews.Description:
One of the best hostels near Bui Vien Street. This was my second stay, and Kitty at the front desk really went above and beyond with tour help and food recommendations. She always greets you with a warm smile.

A friend from the US recently came to visit and booked a private room for two nights.. everything went smoothly, and he was really impressed with the room and amenities. Big thanks to Kitty and the staff for keeping the hostel clean, comfortable, and welcoming.

user_reviews.Name: Gav
user_reviews.Description: I loved staying here. The hostel has a chill vibe but the people I met were great. The building big and has many rooms. By far my favourite hostel I stayed at in HCM! The beds are really nice. There was a mini closet with hangers, mirror, storage box and shelving all in this bed set up. I stayed in a 8 person mixed dorm.
