🌿This resort is one of best place we have lived in this Southern area. Surrounded by serene beach and rugged hills, the place offers a peaceful retreat with absolutely breathtaking views. Whether you’re jogging the nearby beach or simply relaxing by the water, the scenery is nothing short of spectacular.

🌿The calm, clear water of swimming pool reflects the surrounding landscape like a mirror, and the elevated viewpoints along the internal path give you panoramic glimpses of the scenery and beyond. It’s quiet, uncrowded, and feels like a true escape into nature; perfect for photography, or just unwinding in a beautiful, natural setting.

🌿If you’re looking for a peaceful vacation and a place that captures the essence of outdoor beauty, this place is a must-visit in this region. A true hidden gem worth discovering!

🪗Note: we booked it with quite cheap price, only vnd 1.400k per night included breakfast buffet via Agoda. Highly recommend book through this app.

user_reviews.Name: Nata Ro
user_reviews.Description: A nice resort with an extensive green territory. A bit far from everything, so if you don’t mind staying at one place - should be fine. Gym is tiny. Breakfast is ok. Good value of money.

title: Orson Hotel & Resort Côn Đảo
address: 10 Bến Đầm, Côn Đảo, Hồ Chí Minh 790000, Vietnam
plus_code: JHWM+XQ Con Dao, Ba Ria - Vung Tau, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Alison Zimmermann
user_reviews.Description: Orson is located above a stunning bay with a pristine beach, excellent ocean swimming conditions and snorkelling opportunities at both ends of the bay. The hotel has good facilities, great food and friendly, helpful staff. Everything is clean and comfortable. Rooms with an ocean view have large balconies with clear views to the ocean, comfortable beds and a spacious area. The bathroom was clean and functional. Nick is the heart of this resort. His engaging, entertaining personality and impeccable attention to guest service and comfort are commendable. No request is too complex, and Nick will offer options and suggestions for activities and places to visit around Con Dao. He is a gem. He also trainsxand mentors local young people in hospitality skills, and integrates this seamlessly into the guest experience. The infinity pool at the resort is lovely, with sun loungers and complimentary swimming towels available for guests. Breakfasts are great value for money (western and Asian options, and a wide variety of fruits) and guests have the options of eating in the light and spacious breakfast dining area, or outside under the trees. We will definitely visit Orson again for a nature-immersion, fresh air escape for Ho Chi Minh City. Thank you Nick, Alex and staff for a memorable and relaxing experience.

user_reviews.Name: oliyanas2023
user_reviews.Description: AMAZING!!! Highly recommend! The staff are so friendly especially Nick- what a legend, knows everyone’s name and goes above and beyond to make your stay more memorable. Victor was amazing too, so friendly and helpful, told me about the local community and advice about the island 👌 Overall everyone is very hospitable and the resort is spectacular! Ocean is accessible when low tide and you won’t be disappointed by the views and the rooms are big and clean and have amazing views! Food is delicious and endless! I’m so sad to leave! Such a special island. Thank you! I will be back!

user_reviews.Name: Loan Brennan
user_reviews.Description:
My family and I stayed at Orson Resort for 6 nights and had a wonderful experience. The resort is located in a quiet area, perfect for relaxing, with a beautiful beach just steps away. Our room was very nice, clean, and comfortable, making us feel at home throughout our stay.

Although the resort is a bit far from town, we rented motorbikes and enjoyed riding around the area, which added to the adventure. The service was consistently good, and the staff were attentive and welcoming. The owner was very friendly, and Manager Nick was always walking around, checking in and asking how we were doing, which made us feel truly cared for.

Overall, Orson Resort is a great choice if you’re looking for peace, relaxation, and genuine hospitality. We would happily return and recommend it to anyone seeking a quiet getaway by the beach.

user_reviews.Name: cat shanley
user_reviews.Description:
Pros:
- The setting is beautiful with a nice little beach & pool area.
- Nick was great. We looked for him to give him a tip when we left but couldn’t find him. Very helpful.
- Airport shuttle.
- Could rent motorbike.
- Staff was very nice.

Cons:
- Facility is falling apart.
- No gym (even though it’s advertised).
- Very overpriced for the comfort level.
- Could be a lovely hotel if a little more thought went into details: more chairs & pillows around the pool/ chairs closer to beach, windows cleaned for the view, etc.

user_reviews.Name: Chris
user_reviews.Description:
This hotel is trying to be something that it is not

Beach, and pool are beautiful, but the overall experience did not match the price or 4-star expectations. Check-in was done without explanations they just handed me they key. No info about facilities or breakfast hours, airport transfer etc which constantly forced me to ask questions to have more information.

The first room had rust in the shower and a broken shower head. After requesting a change, a new room was given 24 hours later, but it had a leaking bathtub and at least 10 dead flies on the floor, i asked 2 times for it to be cleaned… it wasn’t.

Food service was repeatedly slow and disorganized, with long waiting times (at LEAST one hour if you order at the pool bar), they dont give you any cutlery and napkins, incorrect items served (you order beef they serve chicken), and plates left for hours in the sun after your meal, attracting all sorts of insects and ants next to the swimming pool. Staff were often on their phones instead of attending to guests, and didn’t even tried to communicate in english for basics things (ex: I asked for 1 Coca Cola at the bar) they instantly pulled out the translator….

The spa/pool toilet area was in very poor condition: only one toilet, hidden behind a curtain next to two showers that were in bad condition. The toilet is not even in a properly enclosed room !!! lacking privacy and cleanliness, and not acceptable for a 4* hotel that costs 80€ a night. (See picture)

The restaurant was very expensive compared to other local restaurants, and the food is below average. On two consecutive evenings, the regular restaurant was closed due to a grill and new year event, leaving no alternative on site or within walking distance. The only option was to pay 766,000 VND per person to eat (27-30€) which is CRAZY when you’re used to pay 5-8€ for a meal. I learned later that they were other alternatives (buffet at half price for smaller portions), but again no one bothered explaining  it. The manager will say the costs are high because they are on an island but every other restaurant on the island is still cheaper.

Due to this terrible experience, i decided to leave early and requested not to be charged for unused nights, which was refused.

Alex, the general manager, couldn’t care less about my experience. They kept offering an upgrade to a room with sea view which I didn’t want because the service would remain the same. The GM Alex didn’t even apologise and the interaction became very uncomfortable. After mentioning the intention to share the experience online, he took a photo of me without consent, and stated that he had connections with other hotels on the island, which felt intimidating and created a sense of being unsafe, so I left shortly after. Definitely not the type of service I was expecting.

Also, when you read the other negative reviews that all address the same problems, they will respond that they have lots of other positive reviews from other international travelers. They do not address the issues directly. But the pictures speak for themselves.

I then stayed at different properties across the island which were all better. The service from the hotel secret Con Dao is far far better at the same price range. The staff cares, speaks English, good and reasonably priced food and an awesome swimming pool.

Edit: I’m not surprised with the hotel’s response to my comment. It matches the quality of the hotel

user_reviews.Name: Maurizio Valacchi
user_reviews.Description:
The hotel is placed in a quite area: is a recent construction, beside the reception there is a big area with 2 billiard tables. on the opposite side the breakfast area and in the middle bar-restaurant. It's a huge open space where a relaxing music is playing at the correct volume and very bright with large windows with access to the swimming pool area. The pool is so big where you can easily swim with separate area for kids. Over the pool, there is the beach. My room 433 prfectly cleaner, was so big, with double view side and sea view with large windows and a huge balcony with comfortable chairs where you can enjoy the view in tranquillity sipping s drink for a whell stocked mini bar well refrigerated: a daily appointment for me! Bathroom too has a big area with either bathrube and shower. The bed is kind size, very comfortable.
The food is good quality and the personal is lovely considering that their english is often beginner level but they are so efficient with translator so is not difficult communicate with them. All of them are kind and lovely as per their culture. Nick the manager, is an absolute positive vibes man! He speaks english very well and has a spontaneous guest attention including a huge problem solving ability.
In my case i had a problem with booking due to my fault and he solved it. Moreover, i had a problem with my back and he took me to the hospital for x ray check assisting me in each phase i have been there treating me like a brother: LOVELY, thank you for everything you have done for me.
Pick up from/to airport was included and well appreciated. Scooter/bike rent service is also provided. For my expectation: 5 stars!

user_reviews.Name: Gregory Allan Datu Cendana
user_reviews.Description:
First, I want to say that I appreciated the service that Nick provided—remembering names and greeting guests during breakfast. I also enjoyed how secluded the space was and the pool beach were easily accessible from the resort.

Second, I’d only recommend this place if you want something more secluded and are willing to take a taxi or rent a car to get into the town. They do provide towels.

Third, the pictures don’t accurately represent the condition of the property, I was never told about snorkeling or the tour, the fitness center is being remodeled but there was no update on the website or during booking and they need a better system for charging to your room. They were trying to charge me for a bottle of wine that I never ordered or drank and required multiple steps to confirm it wasn’t me.

user_reviews.Name: Jiří Vlček
user_reviews.Description:
The hotel is located in a beautiful bay, about a twelve-minute drive from the town center. It can be peaceful for lounging by the pool—unless karaoke starts. The loud, fake “singing” is absolutely unbearable. I do have to compliment the bidet spray in the bathroom—it has great pressure. Unfortunately, that’s where the positives end.

I honestly don’t understand how this hotel has so many good reviews. Every guest I spoke to here was dissatisfied with something. The hotel tries to present itself as having a “European standard,” but I seriously doubt anyone involved has ever been to Europe.

The photos on Booking look beautiful, but the reality is the exact opposite. Everything is extremely old, creaky, and rotten. There was mold in the room, and the bathroom was completely crooked. To be fair, the mountain view from the room is beautiful—I’ll give them that.

Another major issue is the price. This is the third most expensive hotel on the island, yet the price is completely unjustified compared to other hotels. I visited several other hotels in town, and the difference was very noticeable.

The staff barely speaks English at all. Only one person—probably the manager—can communicate properly. Unfortunately, he comes across as extremely fake, constantly trying too hard to please and saying whatever he thinks you want to hear, which quickly becomes exhausting and insincere.

The food is absolutely not worth the price. For Vietnam, everything here is outrageously overpriced. They are clearly trying to pretend to be something they are not. Service is painfully slow, and quite often you receive something completely different from what you ordered.

To sum it up: I would never stay at this hotel again, and I would strongly recommend anyone visiting Con Dao to avoid it as well. Stay in town instead, find a quieter small hotel on the outskirts, and you’ll be much happier.

title: An Lam Retreats Saigon River
address: 21/4 Trung Street, Bình Hòa, Hồ Chí Minh 70000, Vietnam
plus_code: VMCX+9J Binh Hoa, Binh Duong, Vietnam
description: Chic suites & villas in a tranquil riverfront resort with an open-air restaurant, a spa & a pool.

user_reviews.Name: kieren mccormack
user_reviews.Description: If we could give 6 stars we would. Amazing, amazing, amazing. The staff can’t do enough for you. My wife had a cold (clearly nothing to do with the hotel) while we stayed and the staff ensured she had plenty of ginger tea and expressed real concern for her wellbeing. The food is incredible, yes, more pricey than the rest of Vietnam but not silly. The quality of the food is great, very tasty- including the western food and the Italian restaurant. The grounds are beautfilly kept, with really interesting plants, a beautiful garden and huge respect shown for the natural grounds. Without question we would stay again and again. Our room was always spotless,  the products offered were of a good quality and plenty of space. Our view of the river was lovely. It would be nice to have a sun lounger on the balcony but we appreciate that would be expensive to add to all the rooms. We loved everything about it. The boat from HCM was pretty fast and very easy to organise. The gym is very well stocked and plenty big enough. The butler experience we had was extraordinary. The grounds and staff really make the hotel a wonderful experience you don’t really want to leave!!

user_reviews.Name: Trip.com Member
user_reviews.Description: It was a great way to experience Ho Chi Minh city without too much hustle, bustle city. The boat transport is a real attraction. That was the deciding factor for me to choose the place. Several things can be better: breakfast was rather moderate. swimming pool maintenance was not good. My husband stepped on a broken pallet. very dangerous.

user_reviews.Name: Shahad Manea
user_reviews.Description: This is a very pretty resort with an amazing spa (almost all staff are very competent even the trainees) but the location is very far and the hotel is verly priced for the services they provide  as we have received better service from a 3 star hotel. It is filled with insects, there was take out food the the house keeping staff left and never picked up for days. The complementary boat service is at specific times like a bus, for a 5 star that cost this much it should be on demand as soon as you request it specially given the location. The breakfast does not reflect the 5 star rating the pho and the burger is excellent.

user_reviews.Name: Renata Waihi
user_reviews.Description:
Our stay at An Lam Retreats Saigon River was nothing short of exceptional.

From the moment we arrived, the level of care, warmth, and attention to detail shown by the team was outstanding. Every single staff member went above and beyond — not because they had to, but because they genuinely wanted to. That kind of service cannot be trained; it’s a culture, and An Lam has mastered it.

The restaurant team were brilliant — knowledgeable, attentive, and effortlessly gracious. Each meal felt thoughtful and calm, perfectly in tune with the retreat’s serene setting. The service was seamless without ever feeling intrusive.

Even the boat transfers were a highlight. Our driver was professional, welcoming, and made every journey feel relaxed and special — a small detail that speaks volumes about the care taken across every touchpoint of the guest experience.

An Lam is not just a place to stay; it’s a place to exhale. A rare combination of luxury, authenticity, and genuine hospitality that stays with you long after you leave.

We cannot recommend it highly enough and look forward to returning

user_reviews.Name: Nguyen Quoc Gia Huy
user_reviews.Description:
My recent stay at An Lâm Retreat was exceptional in every aspect. From the stunning riverfront pool villa to the thoughtful details throughout the property, everything exceeded my expectations. However, what truly elevated this experience was the incredible service from my personal butler, Tấn.

Tấn anticipated our needs with precision, demonstrating impeccable attention to detail and genuine warmth. His professionalism, proactive approach, and attentiveness made our stay seamlessly enjoyable and utterly relaxing. Any request, no matter how small, was handled efficiently and thoughtfully, making us feel truly valued and cared for.

Thank you, Tấn, for your outstanding service and dedication—you’re a significant reason why An Lâm Retreat now holds a special place among my favorite luxury destinations. Highly recommend this retreat for anyone seeking an unforgettable, personalized hospitality experience.

user_reviews.Name: L8674FGmarian
user_reviews.Description: This “space” was so beautiful and our rooms were amazing. The jungle vibes was so relaxed and not a hint of Saigon stress or pace. |The most amazing thing about our stay was the staff. So kind and helpful that we almost felt bad. Everytime our baby dropped her toy, they came running and picked it up. Very ‘5 star hotel’ treatment (cleaning, details in the afternoon in our rooms, dinner, breakfast, pool). An Lam could definately be on a season of “White Lotus”. |They made us feel so welcome with our two needy kids (4 years and 6 months). Perfect way to end an amazing trip to Vietnam! |Only comment was the amount of mosquitos! First day they ate us! Especially our 4 year old son had around 30 huge mosquito bites. An lam has free mosquito spray in the rooms and “stay away” lemongrass candles. That def. Helped.

user_reviews.Name: Shakeel Khan
user_reviews.Description:
An Lam Saigon River – a peaceful escape just minutes from the city
Such a beautiful and relaxing place! From the moment you board the little boat across the river, you feel like you’ve left Ho Chi Minh City behind. The resort is surrounded by lush greenery, with stunning riverside views and a calm, natural vibe. My room was spacious, spotless, and designed with so much attention to detail.
The staff were amazing — genuinely kind and attentive. My Villa Host (Triet) went above and beyond, always checking in and making everything effortless. The food at Luc Binh restaurant was delicious, especially the local Vietnamese dishes and the beautiful breakfast by the river.
If you’re after peace, privacy, and great service, this is the perfect spot. It’s close enough to the city but feels a world away. I left feeling refreshed and can’t wait to return!
Special shout out to my host, Triet for looking after us. Also to Thao for managing our special dietary requirements. Thank you Diego (Resort Manager) and to rest of the team for your hospitality.

user_reviews.Name: Linda D
user_reviews.Description: You could not ask for a better place for start or end to a vacation. I’ve been here for both- this visit was at the start of our vacation and helped us get over our jet lag. The people at the beautiful resort are absolutely amazing. From the moment you step on boat, you are well taken care of-our concierge Tran met us when we arrived and from then on, it was luxury at its best. We had a private 2 bedroom villa fit for a king. The food is amazing and the spa is top notch. That’s probably why I’ve been back 2 times. They are super accommodating to food allergies and any special requests. Thank you A lam and Tran for a wonderful experience.

title: Golden Emerald Resorts
address: 29 Phạm Thị Trích, Ấp 1, Bình Mỹ, Hồ Chí Minh, Vietnam
plus_code: XJCX+G7 Binh My, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Maggie Gardener
user_reviews.Description: An hour from city centre with very few services. Nice pool, cheap stay for a quiet chill. No tea/coffee facilities in room. No ferry to cross river. Breakfast included is not a buffet and you need to pay for drinks. No tour services around area.

user_reviews.Name: Chuk Chuk
user_reviews.Description:
Although the hotel is located in Củ Chi, it's more convenient and closer to travel to Thủ Dầu Một for leisure activities compared to the Củ Chi central area.

The parking lot is spacious. The overall space is wide, with plenty of trees, a swimming pool, and a garden.

The room is beautiful, spacious, clean, and quite new, with a view overlooking the pool and the Saigon River. The soundproofing between rooms is good, but noise from the hallway can be clearly heard inside the room.

While walking in the hallway, I occasionally noticed an unpleasant odor. Additionally, there are speakers in the hallway playing music, and since the soundproofing isn’t perfect, it sometimes made the room a bit noisy.

The receptionist was super friendly and kind. There are also two well-behaved and adorable dogs on the property.

I stayed during a holiday, and the price was around 1.5M VND for three nights. Considering the quality of the room and other amenities, I found it to be worth it.

user_reviews.Name: Andy Lefevere
user_reviews.Description: Away from the buzzing sounds of the city you find this quiet spot with some very nice and helpful staff!   They have a small zoo area, nice swimming pool and everything makes you feel serene and at peace!

user_reviews.Name: Vian Nguyễn
user_reviews.Description: It’s pretty nice but still have something need to change here

user_reviews.Name: Thuy Nguyen
user_reviews.Description: Good services. Delicious foods. Good view. And nice place

user_reviews.Name: Linh huỳnh
user_reviews.Description: This place is wonderful, quiet and very romantic💐

user_reviews.Name: Nguyễn Tiếng
user_reviews.Description:
Không thể hiểu nổi cái Resort này. Mình và những người bạn có nhã ý đến đây nhưng ba tháng qua chưa 1 lần book được phòng.

Có lẽ Resort quá đông chăng.
Book trong tuần ko có,
rồi thử lần khác book trước 1 -2 tuần cũng ko có,
rồi những người khác cùng thử cũng ko có phòng.

Hehe phải chăng là Resort có đang hoạt động ??? :D

user_reviews.Name: htt
user_reviews.Description: Phòng hơi nhỏ nhưng sáng, sạch, wc rộng rãi nhưng không có bồn tắm, có cửa sổ view sông sài gòn. Chăn hơi mỏng, không có sẳn caffe và trà, cách âm cũng không tốt. Nhân viên lễ tân rất nhiệt tình.

title: Oceanward Hotels & Resorts
address: TT, Khu phố, Phước Hải, Hồ Chí Minh, Vietnam
plus_code: C78P+52 Phuoc Hai, Ba Ria - Vung Tau, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: TN
user_reviews.Description: Very nice place and clean room. The pool was clean and offered great activities. Proximity to the beach makes this place all the more valuable as we were able to do many fun activities. Staff were helpful to attend to our needs when we needed more soap and shampoo. Generally, we would definitely come back to stay away from more crowded and touristy spots!

user_reviews.Name: rope297
user_reviews.Description: The rooms were good: neat, fresh atmosphere, convenience. However, i was not satisfied for service quality: the floors are always wet because of no having any private way leading to the pool. Buffet for breakfast was so bad: bad food, bad staff such as nobody set up clean table for pax. For breakfast buffet, the pax had to take a breakfast ticket to be eaten. In case you lost the ticket or your team-leader forgot to take it for you, u would have no food in stomach because the check-in staff could not handle  that situation flexibly. Of course, your room card  had no value in that case for no checking from the staff

user_reviews.Name: Phuc Thao Nguyen
user_reviews.Description: Not good services. Room is too old with uncoformtable furniture. Unprofessional  receptionist. No luggage cart, no help to carry luggage from car parking to the lobby & from lobbly to the room.

user_reviews.Name: duoivan
user_reviews.Description: I connected to the hotel's Zalo chat account to inquiry about the room rate. Admin wasn't helpful at all. He/she took so much time and did not even ask for my name while I already booked reservations. ||||Until the point I got really upset, I told them I will complaint on TripAdvisor, the staff then texted me with a smiley face, with the text "You need to think again whether you can do it". The staff revoked the guest and challenged me to rate them on social media. What a ridiculous and worse services ever I have experienced. ||||When I got to the hotel, there were no apologies, they did not even mentioned the issue upon check-ing in, they pretended like there was no issue at all. ||||I gave you a chance to rectify your problem but you disappointed me Oceanward hotel!

user_reviews.Name: PaalM4
user_reviews.Description: This wonderful hotel just outside Long Hai has a perfect location right on the beach. Between the beach and the hotel, you will find a great pool, so for a relaxing holiday this hotel is perfect. The staff is very friendly and serviceminded. Breakfast is served either on the top floor of the hotel or in a nice beachside restaurant next to the hotel. The hotel is newly build and the whole structure is fresh, clean and appealing. Due to the humid climate, places which have ben around some years can look a bit worn. Across the street is a lovely seafood restaurant and down the block you will find a small village.

user_reviews.Name: Jay
user_reviews.Description:
TERRIBLE EXPERIENCE.

After making a reservation through Agoda and confirming the reservation, I visited the hotel, but the hotel requested the normal price, saying that there was no confirmation from Agoda. When I complained about this, I asked why Agoda was at fault and why I was angry with myself. I asked the hotel reception to confirm with Agoda, but Agoda refused, saying that work could only be done by email. Afterwards, they said that they would refund the difference when the reservation was confirmed, but they were already upset. The hotel did not contact Agoda. So I contacted the Agoda customer service directly, got the customer service phone number, called the customer service directly and resent the email to the hotel. Check-in takes approximately 1 hour. I can't understand that the hotel approved the reservation and received a confirmation email from Agoda, but it said that it was never approved. Retraining of hotel reception staff required.

user_reviews.Name: mariusontour
user_reviews.Description: Booked this hotel for 2 nights on hotels.com when we arrived they didn’t find our booking even when we showed them our booking confirmation. We decided to leave and when we asked for help for taxi. They said 1 hour. After 2 hours still bo taxi. A nightmare of experience.

user_reviews.Name: Matt
user_reviews.Description: Nice staff, fine pool, great access to beach. Reasonable priced beer and bottled water available in lobby. Biggest problem (reason for four stars) was that the AC in our room wasn't near strong enough. It took hours to get to below sweating temperature.

title: Trân Châu Beach & Resort
address: ĐT44A, ấp Hải Sơn, Phước Hải, Hồ Chí Minh 794650, Vietnam
plus_code: 97W9+M6 Phuoc Hai, Ba Ria - Vung Tau, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Annelize Engelbrecht
user_reviews.Description: We visited the restaurant for lunch. It has beautiful views of the ocean and some lovely photo taking opportunities outside. Staff couldn't speak English but could be communicated with through translation. The menu is also fully translated. Even though we were the only people in the restaurant at that point, the food and even the drinks took an extremely long time to come. We ordered fish and chips, fried beef noodles, cubed beef and vegetables and chicken wings with butter (instead of fish sauce). The fish and chips were great, the noodles were fine but the beef in the noodles was cold. The cubed beef dish was also cold. The chicken was fine. Dishes were very expensive for what they were and not really worth the price. Paying was also an issue as they have very old, outdated card machines. Rather come with cash.

user_reviews.Name: HUNG NGUYEN
user_reviews.Description:
I had a wonderful 2-night stay at Tran Chau Resort. The room was clean, spacious, and well-equipped. The atmosphere was peaceful and relaxing—perfect for a short getaway. The food was tasty and satisfying, and the staff were incredibly friendly and helpful throughout my stay.

If you’re visiting Vietnam and looking for a quiet, comfortable place to relax by the sea, I highly recommend Tran Chau Resort. It’s a great spot to unwind and enjoy a slower pace of life. I’ll definitely return in the future!

user_reviews.Name: Stephen McGrath
user_reviews.Description: Not a swimming beach, but they do have a pool and the beach is nice to walk on and enjoy the view. The breakfast buffet is decent. This resort is better for company trips than foreign tourists, though.

user_reviews.Name: RobRoy McBinson
user_reviews.Description:
Not bad considering we were there for one day. Again more of a resort for the locals as not really up to western standards.
Room basic but air-conditioning more than up to par, one restaurant with ok Vietnamese food.
View good but beach and surroundings pretty dirty with trash everywhere.
When will they learn that Vietnam is their country!

user_reviews.Name: Bryan Thomlison
user_reviews.Description:
No complaints! Fantastic resort. Wonderful staff.  Excellent salt water swimming pool. Gorgeous beaches. Nice rooms. Comprehensive breakfast buffet as well as delicious Vietnamese cuisine in the restaurant.
Also, great coffee bar.

user_reviews.Name: Luan Ninh
user_reviews.Description: Nice beach from far, but also plastic waste swimming in the water. Eating here is definetly not cheap. Better bring your own staff with you. We were here on a holiday, so a lot of locals bringing their own cooking utensils. I regret paying 160.000 đ/p for the entrance just to go into the sea...nice sea though.

user_reviews.Name: Carol Pickering
user_reviews.Description:
Enormous resort open to day trippers. Very busy.
Our room was dated and plugs and USB points didn't work . It was clean and comfortable. Food overpriced but breakfast buffet included is good. Staff friendly and helpful

user_reviews.Name: Andrew Tran
user_reviews.Description: Very nice resort. Entry fee is only $5.5 and guest can have nice day at the beautiful beach. Shades, leisure chairs and freshwater showers are all provided at no cost

title: Vietsovpetro Resort Ho Tram
address: Hồ Tràm, Hồ Chí Minh 794970, Vietnam
plus_code: F9CF+2F Ho Tram, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Neonflame Studio
user_reviews.Description:
The “resort” is outdated and the room we had was moldy and smelled.  Leaking faucets and slow flowing toilets.  The WiFi was not working in the room and I had to call for service before I figured out how to make it work by rebooting the router.  There was no restaurants around and we had to taxi 20 mins for lunch and dinner.

I definitely won’t come back again and don’t recommend it to friends.

user_reviews.Name: Francoius Strydom
user_reviews.Description: Not a place to visit. Morning breakfast you will share a table with 10 others as they don't have small table. Resort build to close to the beach, so they destroyed that. 3 km from the closest coffee shop. Maybe good for locals but not foreigner.

user_reviews.Name: Thang Tran Viet
user_reviews.Description:
Nice resort with great and big swimming pools.

A big plus is 1 tennis court
And 3 pickleballs

Tennis court: I hope management see this comments and fix the tennis net. Good to have net separating the pickleballs from getting into tennis court

user_reviews.Name: Velosity
user_reviews.Description: I have no comments about the rooms, because I with my family haven't been there. But there are all time great Bayview, and during the Sunset the hotel turned into wonder paradise. It's pleasant to hear the sea lyrics and enjoy "all-inclusive" equipment

user_reviews.Name: Nhat Huy
user_reviews.Description: Quite old and outdated. But the rooms are spacious and fully furnished. Even the cheapest room type has bathtub and separate working table.

user_reviews.Name: KeiganPage
user_reviews.Description: This place has the potential to be great but it seems like the owners and management don't care. Rooms are large and bed is very comfortable but the couches are stained and dirty. Lots of space in the bathroom but no hot water in the shower, when I asked about it they sent someone who just turned the shower on and said hot while the water was barely warm. Pools are gorgeous but again need a bit of love and upkeep. This place could be incredible if somebody gave it the TLC it needs.

user_reviews.Name: Robert Posthuma
user_reviews.Description:
Pros:
Good looking, well maintained big stylish resort right at the beach. Great pools, nice rooms with a spectacular view and very friendly staff.

Some small cons:
- Best to have your own transportation or use taxi for everything as there is no rentals available and nothing within walking distance.
- No furniture on the balcony.
- Cannot swim in the ocean in front.

user_reviews.Name: Minh Le
user_reviews.Description:
Had a wonderful stay at Vietsovpetro Resort Ho Tram! The beachfront location was amazing, with stunning views and easy access to the beach. The pool was perfect for relaxing, and the staff was incredibly friendly and helpful throughout our stay. We would definitely recommend this resort to anyone looking for a relaxing getaway in Ho Tram.
This review highlights the positive aspects of the resort, including the beachfront location, pool, and friendly staff. It also uses positive adjectives and adverbs to create an inviting and persuasive tone.

title: Chi-Bu Riverside
address: 12/6/8, Chính Nghĩa, Vĩnh Thạnh, Phước An, Đồng Nai, Vietnam
plus_code: MV93+3X Phuoc An, Dong Nai, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Elias Kolling
user_reviews.Description:
Amazing staff. Very kind, attentive, service minded and willing to go above and beyond. During our stay we were able to lend both a paddle board and motorbike to explore the area, which was very lovely.
The food was also delicious and the sizes of the portions were very large.

Their only flaw was the cleanliness in the rooms.
Also in the honeymoon suite, we would have loved to have two, rather than just one, sun bed on the private terrace.

The service however, does weigh up for this. Simply put: extraordinary service both from the staff and the owner.

user_reviews.Name: Lee Sarah
user_reviews.Description:
Beautiful view, lots of greenery and relaxing vibe.
The staff are super friendly, helpful, and truly caring: Hiệp, english-speaking staff, and others  I didn’t ask their name
The food is delicious, more than I expected. So wowwww
The road in is a bit tricky at first, but the staff came out to guide us, so no problem at all.
Highly recommended for families or companies planning a short weekend getaway!

user_reviews.Name: piersb2013
user_reviews.Description: A magical place to spend the weekend. We stayed in the honeymoon suite which was pure heaven. Super confortable bed and a million dollar view waking up and opening the curtains and seeing the river 10 meters away. Great breakfast with huge pancakes banana and syrup was a great way to start the day. We took paddle boards down the river and back up agin for a much needed lunch. I would highly recomend Chi Bu. 55 mins in a taxi from Thao Dien and only 260,000 vnd.

user_reviews.Name: alexandra rendall
user_reviews.Description: I love this haven! 45 minutes away from Thao Dien, a peaceful, green 24 pause. Bliss. Food is delicious, staff is very friendly and attentive. Rooms are beautiful. I go to recharge every once in a while. The magic happens every time.

user_reviews.Name: Ben Knight
user_reviews.Description: Difficult to access as several of the roads leading there are either flooded or blocked by construction, so getting in with a car requires a bit of trial and error. As for the resort, locals on deafeningly loud boats frequently pass through, destroying the otherwise peaceful riverside atmosphere.

user_reviews.Name: dave gibbons
user_reviews.Description:
We had a great stay at Chibu. The location was beautiful and relaxing on the river. Only an hour out of the city, perfect for a weekend getaway. The rooms were well presented and the restaurant and river front were beautiful.
We went as a family with 2 teenagers and a dog. There was lots there for the kids to do - pool, paddle boarding, badminton - all included in the room price. Chibu was very dog-friendly too and she had a fantastic time running around in the countryside.
The owners were very welcoming and the staff were extremely attentive. They brought chilled drinking water and ice to the room on several occasions and when it rained, they delivered umbrellas to the cottage. They even brought the dog a bowl of food and water during dinner.
Breakfast was included in the room rate with western and Vietnamese options - I can recommend the smoked salmon and scrambled eggs with sourdough toast. We ate dinner in the restaurant both nights and were impressed with the quality and price of the food.

user_reviews.Name: review guy
user_reviews.Description: The acro yoga community of Vietnam just had a great weekend retreat at Chi Bu. It’s a great location for yoga and relaxation. They have wonderful facilities for practice, a beautiful river and the staff is very friendly and welcoming. We will be back soon. Thanks so much!!!

user_reviews.Name: Brandon Davies
user_reviews.Description:
The staff were the highlight, all really lovely people, I wish we had more time to spend with them.
It's not cheap but to support a good cause and people with good values, I'm happy to do that.
It's also a very quiet and serene respite from the bustling Saigon.

Thank you Babu for the Birthday gifts, the care and conversations.

title: KIM DELTA TRAVEL
address: 268 Đề Thám, Phường Phạm Ngũ Lão, Bến Thành, Hồ Chí Minh 700000, Vietnam
plus_code: QM9V+9F Ben Thanh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

complete_address.borough: Phường Phạm Ngũ Lão, Ben Thanh

user_reviews.Name: Thein Htun Aung
user_reviews.Description: We have great families for Mekong delta today Tony. Everything was very good and happy. We actually have enjoyed and interesting local people and tour guide too. Definitely recommend the trip. We liked elephant ear fishing from restaurants. Thank you very much Tony

user_reviews.Name: Tanya
user_reviews.Description: Good tour services and a super friendly English speaking guide, Mr. Tony, who took great care of us throughout the Mekong Delta tour. The experience was thoughtfully planned and included coconut candy making, visits to bee farms, and multiple scenic boat rides that made the day memorable. Everything was smooth, informative, and enjoyable. Highly recommended for anyone visiting the Mekong Delta.

user_reviews.Name: Florian Brun
user_reviews.Description:
Excellent Mekong Delta Tour with Phil!
My wife and I had an amazing experience on our Mekong Delta tour with Phil. He was extremely professional, friendly, and respectful throughout the entire trip. Phil took care of everyone in the group and made sure that we all felt comfortable and informed.

Even when our boat had a small technical issue and we had to wait about 20 minutes on the river, he handled the situation perfectly, calmly explaining what was happening and keeping everyone relaxed. It was clear that he truly cares about his guests and wants everyone to have a great experience.

We learned a lot from him about local life and culture, and his communication was excellent. We would book another tour with Phil again without hesitation. Highly recommended!

user_reviews.Name: Brian Thatcher
user_reviews.Description: We did Mekong delta travel yesterday. The trip was very wonderful and great trip. Tour guide Tony did a good trip and got nice lunch to coconut island and excellence. Happy trip and funny journey from public holidays. Remember!

user_reviews.Name: Ts. Dr. Ahmad Soyuti Hj Sabran
user_reviews.Description: The full-day tour of the Mekong Delta was a highly enjoyable and informative experience. Our guide, Tuyen, provided exceptionally educational commentary that greatly enhanced the trip. While the overall package was outstanding, two areas require significant improvement: the availability of Halal food options & place for praying (zohor/asar) for Muslim tourists and the provision of safety life jackets during boat rides. Aside from these critical safety and dietary concerns, the tour merits a full rating for its excellent organization and content.

user_reviews.Name: C m Sharma
user_reviews.Description: The city was crowded people after happy new year . We saw many people and good trip for history tour. Thank you very much for our guide Tony. He was very helpful and excellent

user_reviews.Name: Jernej Kuhar
user_reviews.Description:
Mekong Delta (My Tho – Ben Tre)
TURIST IN, TURIST OUT

Worst guide ever. The guide had no real knowledge of the Mekong River – no history, no context, nothing. He kept repeating the same few generic phrases over and over again.

The tour itself is extremely touristy and superficial. You learn absolutely nothing about local life or the importance of the Mekong.

Regarding lunch: despite choosing the menu in advance (chicken / vegetarian), the guide seated people at different tables randomly. Couples were separated, and even families were split – in one case, a child was seated at a different table from their parents, which is completely unacceptable.

On top of that, tips are expected for literally everything.

Overall, the entire experience is designed only to extract money from tourists, without offering any meaningful information or authentic insight.

user_reviews.Name: Riyani Rainy
user_reviews.Description: The busy at weekend was extremely oexcited and happy people. Whole tour was very enjoyable and good weather by Tony. Highly recommended the tour tony. Very good tour

title: KIM TRAVEL - Daily Tours - Cu Chi Tunnels - Mekong Delta Tour from HCM city
address: 17 Thủ Khoa Huân, Bến Thành, Hồ Chí Minh 700000, Vietnam
plus_code: QMFW+9W Ben Thanh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

owner.name: KIM TRAVEL - Daily Tours - Cu Chi Tunnels - Mekong Delta Tour from HCM city (Owner)

user_reviews.Name: Thea Balich
user_reviews.Description:
We did the Luxury Speedboat 2-Way Tour from Ho Chi Minh City to the Cu Chi Tunnels, and it was an absolutely exceptional experience.
Moving, immersive and unforgettable!

Traveling by luxury speedboat along the Saigon River was a real highlight. Cruising past riverbank villages, dodging water hyacinths, and seeing locals wave as we passed felt like a peaceful contrast to the powerful history we were about to explore. It was a privilege to approach Cu Chi this way rather than by road.

Our guide, Thanh, was truly outstanding. Being guided through this experience by the son of a Vietnam War veteran added a depth and authenticity that is hard to put into words. Thanh is incredibly passionate about history, meticulous in his knowledge, and presents everything with calm neutrality. No bias, no bitterness, and no grudges, which is remarkable given the emotional impact the war had on his own family. His storytelling was thoughtful, respectful and deeply moving.

Exploring the Cu Chi Tunnels themselves was fascinating and intense. We saw the ingeniously designed booby traps, storage areas, kitchens, command centres, field hospitals and trap doors created by local villagers who had war forced upon them. Walking through the trenches, seeing a tank derailed by a landmine, and learning how people survived underground was eye-opening.

We chose to crawl through two tunnels. First a 100 metre tunnel reaching both Level 1 and Level 2 underground, followed by an even narrower 80 metre tunnel for a more realistic experience. Climbing down through a hidden entrance, disguising it with leaves above our heads, was both surreal and unforgettable.

The experience also included a visit to the shooting range, where we fired AK-47s, leaving us with gunpowder on our faces and a very visceral sense of the past. We sampled local cassava (tapioca), the staple food eaten by soldiers during the war, followed by a huge and delicious lunch with plenty of choice.

This was a powerful, educational and genuinely moving experience, made all the more significant by how much the history clearly means to Thanh. We learned so much and felt incredibly well cared for throughout the day.

I cannot recommend this tour highly enough. It’s a must-do in Vietnam, and worth every moment!

user_reviews.Name: Janek Tšuškin
user_reviews.Description: We had a tour with James. English very good. Interesting tour, a lot of things to see. Most liked a boat trip with a smaller boat to lunch place. Tasty food, reasonable prices for extra things. Recommend one day tour with Kim travel Mekong Delta to see and feel the nature. Pick- up information was not so clear and on time. Can be better, also mine seat in the bus, no room for legs.

user_reviews.Name: Mia Bentsen Blomberg
user_reviews.Description:
I did two trips with this company. First to Cu Chi tunnels, which was really nice and informative with exciting experiences like firing the AK-47 and walking/crawling in the tunnels. No beggars, no pressure to buy anything. Our guide Mike was magnificent and entertaining. Five stars.
The second trip to the Mekong delta started out quite nice, but here the pressure to buy was ruining the trip. Especially the boat driver on the rowing boat who spent half the trip nagging about tip, madam, tip. Money, money, money. And the fact that the guide defended this with saying that the whole trip costs the same as a breakfast in my country is just another insult. Then they have to add a little to the price and pay people properly. It's just not ok to not pay the employees and make them bother the guests like this. Other than that, our guide Tim did a good job. But in general, the Mekong delta trip is just not worth the time. I learned little about Vietnam except getting a little more insight into the tourist industry. One star for this trip.
The busses was not very comfortable, and on the Mekong delta tour the driver was reckless and kept full speed over speed bumps making the travel really unpleasant.
In general, I do not recommend Kim Travels for budget tours.

user_reviews.Name: Leandro Sato lemarky
user_reviews.Description:
The tour is good. The guide tourist of today, "Khaihn" is very kindly. I understand all about the trip.
The food of the lunch break is amazing. And I like a lot visit coconut farm. Really I like coconut 🥥
The group is friendly. And I recommend this tour, coz the price,service and communication  this travel agency is good.
5 ⭐ stars 🤩

user_reviews.Name: Vassia Gkiritzioni
user_reviews.Description: The tour exceeded our expectations ! We had a tour in the Mekong Delta and the Cu Chi Tunnels and the full day was amazing thanks to our guide Xem! Xem was very polite, welcoming and taking care of all of us the whole day. He is a very good speaker and explained everything to us around the history of the places we visited, but also the history of Vietnam and the Ho Chi Mihn area. He also introduced us to the traditions and the everyday life of the locals with his stories. He is the best tour guide we ever had and we enjoyed the tour very very much!!! We totally recommend it!! 😊😊

user_reviews.Name: Michal Kuban
user_reviews.Description: Awesome crew, recommend by all means! We had a unique experience with the 1/2 day trip to the Cu Chi tunnels. Absolutely worth it. Our guide Khanh was a great guy. We had many tours and many guides in Vetnam, Khanh was probably the best one - polite, very good english, knowledgeable, fantastic narrative adding to the whole picture. You almost felt like being there with the brave soldiers and experienced their daily challenges and struggles. Do not hesitate, go see Cu Chi (or maybe do other tours - we didn't have more time, unfortunately), with these guys it will be a great excursion to the truly genious resourcefulness, courage and perseverance of the Vietkong soldiers. 👍

user_reviews.Name: Maine Consultant
user_reviews.Description: I found this company on GetYourGuide App. We did the afternoon (Half Day) tour to Cu Chi Tunnels and our guide is James Bon. He is a 5 star rating, request for him if possible. He speaks great English, he is funny and very informative. We had a wonderful tour experience from the time we got picked up at our Hotel till the end of the tour.

user_reviews.Name: Eve Holbrook
user_reviews.Description:
I went on the Cu Chi Tunnel tour with Kim Travel, my guide being Bao. I don’t usually do these type of tours, but I wanted the vast knowledge of the history of the war, and that I definitely got.

The entire morning with Bao was incredible, he is such an informative, fun and genuine person who was very real about the history and was more than happy to answer questions - always with a knowledgable response. I really felt he went above and beyond on this trip, which essentially made the whole experience so worthwhile.

The Cu Chi Tunnels itself was deeply moving and fascinating, definetly returning with so much additional understanding and perspective of the war for the Vietnamese. We also stopped into the Sơn Mài Lâm Phát 2-Handicapped & Handicraft, which blew me away with its authentic and beautiful journey of the surviving people of Agent Orange. The art pieces are expensive, but justified with the long process of making these with the raw organic materials they use.

Please watch this if you want to understand why the art is at the price they sell it for: Tại sao tranh Sơn Mài lại đắt đến vậy| Đắt quá

We were also given extras during the trip such as water bottles, snacks and a lovely cold towel after being in the hot sun all morning.

Could not recommend this more!

title: VIETNAM TRAVEL GROUP JSC
address: 55B Đỗ Quang Đẩu, Phường Phạm Ngũ Lão, Bến Thành, Hồ Chí Minh 700000, Vietnam
plus_code: QM8R+R7 Ben Thanh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

complete_address.borough: Phường Phạm Ngũ Lão, Ben Thanh

user_reviews.Name: Brian Young
user_reviews.Description: We took this tour yesterday and paid the little extra for the limousine pkg ( actually a very comfortable van with relining seats and usb ports) but was totally worth it our guide Chloe was awesome, very knowledgeable and entertaining as was our driver Wang, im a driver in Canada and hats off to Wang for his exceptional driving. The tour was flawless from start to finish with pickup at your hotel a 2 hour drive to the tunnels with a stop at a local art factory, then to the  tunnels, Chloe provided us with so much information ( she is exceptionally good)  from there we went to lunch which was so good then off to Mekong Delta for a river cruise and a trip to the coconut factory, we were also treated to some local singers performing for us. The river tour was 2km long and a unique experience, after that a ride back to your hotel in our luxurious van. Thank you again Chloe and Wang for your services, you made this a very memorable experience for us all.

user_reviews.Name: Maryanne Du
user_reviews.Description:
If you are planning on going to the Cu Chi tunnels, I would really recommend this tour!! We booked on get your guide, saw the reviews and decided to wing it. First off, they’ll pick you up at your accomodation directly. Then it’s a couple of hours drive to the tunnels, but Aqua made sure to keep
us entertained with many interesting history facts. The tunnels themselves were so fascinating, my favourite part of the tour was crawling through the tunnels and trying to cassava and tea, and shooting the AK-47!

Just keep in mind that buying the bullets is 750k per person, and you can’t share them with someone, and it’s minimum 10 bullets. But it’s worth it because how often do you get to say you’ve shot an AK-47?!

Aqua is hilarious and very knowledgeable about the war. I highly recommend this tour, it’s the funnest thing i’ve done in Saigon :))))

user_reviews.Name: Anna Shimon Korol
user_reviews.Description:
I did the Cu chi tunnels + mekong delta 1 day tour.
Our tour guide was the tall handsome skinny successful talanted Tin. And oh, how lucky we were to have him as our guide.
He made the experience super enjoyable from start to finish.
He has incredible english, a great sense of humour, clearly knows a lot about Vietnamese history and is very passionate about it.
He didn't just plainly tell us the facts, he explained it in a simple, yet interesting way that made everyone listencarefully. He answered all of the questions we had without a problem.
The entire group looooved him and his little funny jokes, and we had a splendid time.
So, thank you very much Tin the tiny tour guide for a wonderful journey, and if you get him as your tour guide you are lucky!!!!

user_reviews.Name: Ines 063
user_reviews.Description:
My husband and I took Cu chi tunnels and delta Mekong tour yesterday and we had an amazing experience. The entire day was superbly organized from start to finish. Seamless Organization, pickup from our hotel was very convenient, and the itinerary flowed perfectly. We visited an authentic Vietnamese souvenir factory, the fascinating Cu Chi Tunnels, a coconut candy workshop, and enjoyed a beautiful boat ride through the Mekong Delta. We had a exceptional guide, a young woman named Aqua, deserves all the praise. She was professional, fun, and incredibly knowledgeable. We learned so much history and fascinating facts we hadn't known before. She took excellent care of our group throughout the whole journey. The tour price included all entrance fees, refreshing bottled water, and a truly excellent lunch. Full praise to the agency for a flawless tour, and a special thanks to Aqua for making it such a memorable and unforgettable day!
Highly Recommended!

user_reviews.Name: 22 cc
user_reviews.Description: Hawey is a very hardworking, sweet, and dedicated tour guide. Throughout the journey, he took great care of everyone and explained the country’s history in a clear and engaging way that was easy to understand. I could really feel how proud he is of his country. Wherever we went, even when mixed with other large groups, his voice was confident, clear, and stood out. No matter the location, he always explained everything carefully and thoroughly. Highly recommended.

user_reviews.Name: Trang Le
user_reviews.Description: We had a great time on this Mekong Delta 2-day tour. First of all, they were able to accommodate our request to take multiple big luggages on the tour since we don’t have a place to store them. Already off to a great start! We did everything that was written in the itinerary, though I wish we could have done biking as well, as stated in the header. Nevertheless, we had a wonderful time! Our guide Huy was honestly the highlight. He’s super knowledgeable, incredibly helpful, and very respectful. He even sent us recommendations for places to eat and visit outside of the tour’s schedule and at one point jumped into a muddy pond to help a guest retrieve his phone! You can tell Huy genuinely cares about his guests. Highly recommend this tour, especially if you get Huy as your guide.

user_reviews.Name: Bkat Travels
user_reviews.Description: Tin the tiny tour guide aka Pepsi can was an exceptional guide, he has great English and a very cheeky sense of humour. He made the tour so much better, even though it was so busy we still managed to learn so much about the history and the traps and tunnels. Our family thoroughly enjoyed going through the cu chi tunnels and the lunch stop was delicious too. This was a great way to spend half a day in Ho Chi Minh.

user_reviews.Name: Mushroom Lau
user_reviews.Description: Excellent tour, Our tour guide, Lee was a walking encyclopedia on vietnam's history. He was fun and engaging all the way from start to finish, the tour went through a factory for disabled people from the effects of vietnam war, and how they empower them with work by making lacquerware. Afterwards, in Cu chi tunnels we learnt about the history and how the vietnamese survived. Our tour guide explained throughly all the happenings in the past and how the vietnamese people in cu chi managed to live and survive during the war. We got to shoot the ak47 as well which was eye opening. 10/10 tour, if you find yourselves in ho chi minh city do check out this tour.

title: An Travel

plus_code: QM9V+HP Ben Thanh, Ho Chi Minh, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Jan Michael Amar
user_reviews.Description: The Mekong River Delta day tour with Mr. Ho was fun and informative. A nice tour to learn about the local life, trade, and traditions. A good value for your money. 👍

user_reviews.Name: ND T
user_reviews.Description: Cheap and we had a lot of activities. Our tour guide, Hoa, was top-notch; his English is perfect and he's friendly. Very good food. Thanks very much and we look forward to our next visit.

user_reviews.Name: K K
user_reviews.Description:
AVOID IF YOU DON'T WANT TO SUPPORT ANIMAL CRUELTY.
I am deeply shocked with what I saw at the trip to Mekong Delta. They took us to a place where they keep wild animals in captivity in cruel conditions - loads of fish in small tanks, snakes in an empty concrete cage with no greenery or other place to hide, tens of crocodiles lying on top of each other in a small concrete pool also with no greenery and no shadow, a big snake kept in a tiny cage just to be taken out for tourists to take a photo of it on their shoulders. I'm shocked places like this exist, it should be illegal.
The rest of the trip also sucked - I thought I'd see some interesting nature but instead they dragged us through different shops trying to make us buy multiple products. The boat ride lasts only a couple of minutes and we were pressured to tip the rower - are those people even paid by the company? If not or not enough it's just outregous. As a cherry on top we were forced to listen to a "concert" and again they tried to make us tip the singers.
Waste of time and money.
I don't blame the guide (he was very nice) or any other employee because they are just doing their job but the whole company is trash.

user_reviews.Name: Rishinath Balasandran
user_reviews.Description: Great amd excellent guide by Mr Ho. Communicated well in English and friendly. He explained on the heritage and attractions of Vietnam and Mekong River. Will definitely come again! Totally worth for the money

user_reviews.Name: Manousos Lerios
user_reviews.Description:
Lam for the win
When you take the tour for chu chi tunnels, you are going in a place (last picture i uploaded here) that some disabled people make art with eggshells!
Check their reviews before buying!! Some people got scammed, I didn't buy anything🤷‍♂️ I wanted to tho 😂

user_reviews.Name: John Christer
user_reviews.Description: We had a nice day for visiting Mekong delta. Our guides Zhung Hoa was very helpful and kind. He helped us to take various photos and explained very perfect itinerary. We extremely impressed the first t place from honey and nice garden pomelos. And then later, it was very amazing and excellent. Hoa was worth of best guide we’ve ever met. Thank you very much Hoa and back soonest from other vocation

user_reviews.Name: Nerizza Dador
user_reviews.Description: The trip was well arranged. The guide was so helpful. He made sure no one is left behind. Very kind, friendly and funny. He introduced Meikong river, the culture, the livelihood of Vietnamese people well. Great trip and will surely book againm

user_reviews.Name: Mateusz Jaworski
user_reviews.Description:
We did the Cu Chi Tunnels and Mekong  River tour which we loved. It's a good mix of history in the morning and relaxation in the afternoon.

A special mention to Ken, our guide who was very knowledgeable and entertaining, making the whole experience amazing!

user_reviews.Name: Wangden Tshering Yolmo
user_reviews.Description:
We had an incredible experience on the Mekong Delta and Cu Chi Tunnels tour, and Bac truly made the day unforgettable.
From the start, Bac was energetic, knowledgeable, and extremely well-organized. At the Cu Chi Tunnels, he explained the historical context with so much clarity and passion that it really brought the site to life. He didn’t just repeat facts — he told stories, answered every question patiently, and made sure we understood both the Vietnamese and American perspectives of the war. It felt informative without ever feeling heavy or rushed.
In the Mekong Delta, Bac completely shifted the tone to something lighter and more relaxed. He guided us smoothly through each activity, explained local traditions, and made sure everyone felt comfortable during the boat rides and village visits. His sense of humor and timing were perfect — he kept the energy high even after a long morning.
What stood out most was how attentive he was to everyone in the group. He checked in often, managed time efficiently, and made sure we stayed hydrated and comfortable throughout the day. You could tell he genuinely cared about giving us the best experience possible.
If you’re doing this tour, you’re lucky if Bac is your guide. He turns a good tour into a great one.

user_reviews.Name: Andrew Bernardin
user_reviews.Description: This was 100% worth it. We had amazing tour guides - Nicole and a guide in training (I'm so sorry I cannot remember their name) were very knowledgeable each in their own unique way. They both made the entire trip fun and approachable. It was nice getting to meet a few other travelers. You could be as hands on or hands off as you wanted to be on the tour but every place we went to felt comfortable and showed a better picture of the culture. Loved getting to try a taste of everything as well as the different modes of travel. The boat ride was cool but the cart ride was even cooler.

user_reviews.Name: Tiberiu Ivan
user_reviews.Description:
Absolutely amazing experience — worth every single penny!

I did the full-day tour to the Cu Chi Tunnels and the Mekong Delta, and it ended up being one of the best days of my entire Vietnam trip. For such a small price, you get a full day packed with experiences you’ll remember for life.

You ride on a boat, a tuk-tuk, and even a “champagne boat” through the narrow Mekong canals. You taste delicious local products, see how people live in the countryside, and enjoy a beautiful mix of nature, culture, and adventure.

A huge part of what made the day unforgettable was our guide — Big Handsome Dave. He was funny, full of great energy, and shared a lot of interesting historical facts in a way that kept everyone engaged. Thanks to him, we laughed all day long.

If you want a WOW experience, ask for Dave — the handsome one. He truly made the difference!

Highly recommend this tour — 10/10.

user_reviews.Name: Sára Pálfy
user_reviews.Description: We did the small group tour to the Cu Chi Tunnels with the guidance of Tuan, who was an amazing tour guide. This gentleman is a war veteran and lived through the horrors of the war, therefore was able to give us incredibly special insights. We were very honored and enjoyed our time there learning about history from a different perspective. Wish Tuan the best, and it was a once in a lifetime experience visiting the tunnels.

user_reviews.Name: Bel Clare
user_reviews.Description: Amazing tour today to Cu Chi Tunnels with Sang, he was entertaining and very knowledgeable on the history of the tunnels and the wars they were used in.  VAT was great with the hotel pick up and drop off.  A great experience overall - highly recommended.  Guyen was a fantastic driver also - very calm in the traffic!

user_reviews.Name: Nicole Yu
user_reviews.Description: I booked the Mekong Delta full day trip with Vietnam Adventure Tours and thoroughly enjoyed my experience! The tour guide, Simon, was friendly and knowledgeable about the HCMC (our starting/ending location) as well as the local islands that we visited. I really liked how the tour included local experiences via the different islands that were visited. 10/10 experience and would definitely recommend!

user_reviews.Name: Bülent PİRKUL
user_reviews.Description:
Title: Unprofessional Conduct Inside Tour Vehicle

During my recent tour with Vietnam Adventure Tours, I observed conduct that I consider inappropriate for a professional tour operator.

A staff member was seated barefoot at the front of the vehicle, placing his feet directly on the vehicle floor in full view of paying guests. I have attached photos as evidence.

This is not about cultural differences. It is about hygiene, professionalism, and respect in a shared passenger environment.

Before posting this review, I contacted the company privately and gave management the opportunity to respond. Unfortunately, I did not receive any reply.

As a frequent international traveler, I rarely leave negative feedback. However, service standards matter.

UPDATE:
The company explained that the guide had a medical issue. While we understand that health problems can happen, this does not justify unprofessional behavior inside a tour vehicle.

In addition, my wife speaks limited English and she was loudly dismissed in front of other guests, which was embarrassing and unnecessary.

The wet wipes that were supposed to be distributed were never given. The water that should have been provided upon boarding the vehicle was only distributed after we reminded him.

This does not reflect the level of professionalism expected from a paid tour service.

Our review is based entirely on what we personally experienced that day.

user_reviews.Name: Murray Hutchison
user_reviews.Description: Tuan was an outstanding tour guide for our Chu chi tunnels tour. He fought in the war and was able to share many stories with our small group. Ask for him to see if you can join his group. Well worth it.

user_reviews.Name: Jerry Trang
user_reviews.Description: My family’s trip to South Vietnam was incredible! Our tour guide, Finn (who was incredibly knowledgeable), spoke great English to me and had a great sense of humor. The bus ride is comfortable and the hotels are nice as well. I highly recommend this company to tour you around Vietnam because they are the best of the best. I am glad I chose this company and if I can, I will come back to tour with this company again!

user_reviews.Name: evelyn sabate
user_reviews.Description: Thank you so much I really enjoyed and wonderful trip. It was a great trip with our tour guide Lim. He is amelini tour guide. A lot of fun they make the group at home especially that we are only Filipina in the group. Thank you so much Lim. You a great tour guide! To the Sunrise Ang envy thanks for the amazing trip.

user_reviews.Name: Nina Bui
user_reviews.Description:
This was our first time using this travel agency for a Mekong River trip, and it truly surpassed our expectations. We booked a private three-day, two-night Mekong River tour from Ho Chi Minh City for a group of eight. The tour included numerous stops and a wide range of activities, so there was never a dull moment.

What made the experience exceptional was our tour guide, Ðạt (Ethan). He is extremely knowledgeable, personable, and funny, and he kept us entertained throughout the car rides by sharing stories, jokes, and local insights. During mealtimes, he was always attentive and thoughtful, making sure everyone had everything they needed and was well taken care of before stepping away for his own break. His dedication, professionalism, and genuine care for the group made the entire trip even more enjoyable and memorable.
We will definitely use this agency and Ethan‘s service again.

user_reviews.Name: Emma Tann
user_reviews.Description:
We were at the Saigon Post Office and saw Sunrise Travel stand - after a quick chat we had booked last minute a 2 day tour to the Mekong Delta. The package was brilliant with a beautiful hotel, lots of food and a packed interesting itinerary! Our guides Hung, Binh and Liem were all great at English and knew lots about the tour activities and lots about Vietnam and its history.  They were all happy to chat and tell us about life.
The tour was really well organised, well informed and super affordable. What we got included in the price was amazing. 100% recommend. Thanks Team! Emma & Andreas Dec2025.

user_reviews.Name: Ingrid Dingle
user_reviews.Description:
Liem was a super, friendly and knowledgeable guide. He a also has a very good sense of humour. We thoroughly enjoyed the tour and learnt a lot about the delta. The food was excellent and our accommodation was good. We have to mention the driver. He drove very responsibly.
Thank you for very enjoyable and informative 2 days.

user_reviews.Name: Cong He
user_reviews.Description: Amazing company!  Our tour guide Andy showed us around Can Tho, he was both informative and funny, he made sure we were able to see all the amazing spots and educated us on its historical and societal impact to the local Vietnamese community. During our drive from Ho Chi Minh to Can Tho he gave us little Vietnamese language class to common phrases and also explained the cultural norms with examples from the views on our drive. Despite time restraints he always made sure to prioritize the wants of the group and allowed significant time for us to explore the areas. The food organised by this team were the most delicious and high quality meals we have had during our whole trip in Vietnam - except for dinner on the boat cruise (food was bland). I could not recommend Andy and Sunrise Travel enough and thank them for planning such a fun trip to Can Tho for our group

user_reviews.Name: Rul García
user_reviews.Description: The tour was really nice, and the guide Andy was amazing—very helpful and kind to us. If I come here again, I will definitely go with this company again. The driver was also very kind; he picked us up at our hotel, which was far from the others.

user_reviews.Name: Joanna Zlotowicz
user_reviews.Description:
Andy our tour guide was fantastic!!! We did Mekong Delta (two days) tour with him and he was super prepared and organized and kept our group in great spirits during the whole trip. He looked after us so well and kept calling our group „my family” which was very sweet. If we could we would do all the trips on the Sunrise Travel Group offer with him without hesitation. Normally we avoid organized trips because we prefer to explore on our own terms but thank to Andy it was an unforgettable experience!!!
We passed by the office in Saigon when visiting the market and weren’t planning on doing any trips but after talking to a very nice girl Phon we decided to give it a go and we’re very happy that we did!
One very nice 5star bus with lots of leg room recliner seats and proper aircon picked us up on time from our hotel in District 1 and from there it was one surprise after another. Andy went an extra mile for us for sure!

user_reviews.Name: Alida
user_reviews.Description:
I joined the Sun Tourist tour to Black Virgin Mountain/Tây Ninh and overall had a pretty enjoyable experience—though there were definitely some moments that could’ve been better.

My day started at 6:49 AM when I got picked up directly from my stay. Booking was simple, and although I originally chose a different date, the company asked if I could reschedule and made the change easily.

The bus was okay—no weird smells and seats were fairly comfortable. The group was small (9 people total), mostly travelers from Taiwan and Korea, and honestly, they were the highlight of the tour. Such a sweet, fun group—I got really lucky.

Early on, the guide asked if anyone was vegetarian and mentioned we’d be having phở... which never really happened. I think there might’ve been one shared bowl somewhere, but out of nowhere the driver just came back with bánh mì for everyone. Personally, I’m not a fan, so it would’ve been nice to get clearer info about the meals ahead of time.

After about 1.5 hours, we stopped at a shop where victims of Agent Orange create and sell artwork. I’ve been to similar places on other tours and they tend to sell the same types of souvenirs, so it felt a bit commercial. That said, the guide treated it more like a coffee and toilet break.

Next was a beautiful Buddhist temple with some unique elements I hadn’t seen before, followed by a stop at a fascinating temple/church that blends Buddhism, Christianity, and Confucianism—a belief system unique to the region. That was genuinely interesting to see.

Around 11, we had lunch at a local restaurant near the mountain. There was a nice variety of traditional Vietnamese dishes, and the food was good!

We took the gondola up Black Virgin Mountain (not included in the tour fee), which gave us stunning views. Once at the top, we spent about 3 hours walking through various sites—but always with the guide. There was no opportunity to explore on our own, which made the experience feel a bit rushed and rigid at times. The guide was friendly and talkative, but the explanations about the sites were pretty surface-level.

Still, the natural beauty of the mountain was amazing, and I had such a good time thanks to the people in the group.

The tour was followed by a wonderful lunch at Cu Chi Restuarant. We then made our way towards the Mekong River which was packed with rich cultural experiences. From trying local honey and coconut candies to punting on the Tien River. I highly recommend this tour to everyone. If you want to see the real South Vietnam this is a great place to start!

All the fellow passengers were also great, and we all got along amazingly. We all talked throughout the experience, and bonded over everything!

At the Cu Chi Tunnels, Mr. Bar explained everything in a clear and engaging way, bringing the history to life and answering all of our questions with great detail. He made sure everyone felt comfortable and kept the tour both informative and fun.

The Mekong Delta portion was just as memorable — beautiful scenery, smooth logistics, and perfectly timed stops. Mr. Bar took care of everything and made the day completely stress-free. His local insights and personal stories really added something special that you wouldn’t get from a typical tour.

If you’re visiting Vietnam and want a well-organized, educational, and enjoyable experience, I highly recommend Mr. Bar. Truly a 5-star guide!

user_reviews.Name: Sebin Fernandez
user_reviews.Description:
This was my first international trip, and I saw many positive reviews for Ann, and so I had to take a leap of faith. It turned out to be the best decision. She was very kind and planned the trip according to our needs. From Day 1 onwards, she coordinated everything from stays, which were really good, to the city tours, which were a good experience. We really enjoyed Ho Chi Minh and its cafe culture. I have never seen more cafes in a single street than in my whole life.

On Day 2, we went to the Cu Chi Tunnels and learned about the experiences their ancestors went through, and then we headed to Mekong Bay, which was a good experience. We then headed to Hanoi, and the most beautiful part was our trip to Halong Bay. It was such a refreshing and calm sight, and seeing all the limestone mountains in the background of the ocean was an eye-opener. It was also the first time my wife and I went on a cruise. We did kayaking as well in the open ocean, which was a really energizing experience.

The last trip was to Ninh Binh, where we did the boat trip surrounded by lotus flowers and a short trek up a mountain with a spectacular view of the rice fields.

So, overall, the Vietnam trip was a success and a much-needed break for my wife and me, and it's all thanks to the incredible team of Ann.

user_reviews.Name: Kevin Busick
user_reviews.Description:
We had an unforgettable trip through Da Nang, Hue, My Son, and Hoi An, and it was all thanks to the incredible planning and support from EVT Travel and our coordinator, Hally. From the moment we landed in Da Nang, everything was seamless — airport pickup, hotel check‑in, and every day’s activities were perfectly arranged.

Our itinerary was packed with amazing experiences: riding the Ba Na Hills cable car to the Golden Bridge, exploring the Imperial City of Hue, wandering through the ancient Champa ruins at My Son, and enjoying the peaceful basket boat ride in the Coconut Forest before a magical evening in Hoi An releasing lanterns on the river. Each day felt effortless because the logistics, timing, and transportation were handled so smoothly.

What stood out most was the personal care. Hally checked in regularly, made sure we were comfortable, and adjusted details when needed. The guides were knowledgeable and friendly, and the drivers were always punctual. We never had to worry about a thing — we could just enjoy the beauty of Vietnam and make memories with our family.

This was truly a 5‑star experience from start to finish. We highly recommend EVT Travel and especially Hally to anyone wanting a well‑organized, stress‑free, and unforgettable journey through Vietnam.

The itinerary was well balanced, allowing us to see and experience many aspects of local life without feeling rushed.
A special mention goes to Black, our guide, who truly made the difference. He was knowledgeable, friendly, and very attentive to everyone in the group. His explanations about the Mekong Delta, local traditions, and daily life were clear, interesting, and delivered with genuine passion. He was always ready to answer questions and made sure everyone felt comfortable and engaged.
The activities were authentic and enjoyable, the boat rides were relaxing, and the overall atmosphere of the tour was excellent. It was clear that a lot of care went into planning this experience.
I would definitely recommend this tour to anyone visiting Vietnam who wants a well-organized, informative, and enjoyable day in the Mekong Delta. Thank you to the entire team, and especially to Ghiut, for such a memorable experience!

user_reviews.Description:

They ensured proper baby seats when requested, arranged comfortable vans, and provided very knowledgeable guides. The itinerary across Hanoi, Da Nang, and Hoi An was perfectly timed. Never rushed and very baby-friendly.
A big plus was how well the Indian meals were planned throughout the trip, which made things so much easier while travelling with babies. Communication was seamless with instant replies, and even last-minute requests were handled smoothly without any stress.
Highly recommend EVT Travels for anyone planning a family or baby-friendly trip to Vietnam.
